papers.title: DeepRitzSplit Neural Operator for Phase-Field Models via Energy Splitting
papers.summary: The multi-scale and non-linear nature of phase-field models of solidification requires fine spatial and temporal discretization, leading to long computation times. This could be overcome with artificial-intelligence approaches. Surrogate models based on neural operators could have a lower computational cost than conventional numerical discretization methods.   We propose a new neural operator approach that bridges classical convex-concave splitting schemes with physics-informed learning to accelerate the simulation of phase-field models. It consists of a Deep Ritz method, where a neural operator is trained to approximate a variational formulation of the phase-field model. By training the neural operator with an energy-splitting variational formulation, we enforce the energy dissipation property of the underlying models.   We further introduce a custom Reaction-Diffusion Neural Operator (RDNO) architecture, adapted to the operators of the model equations. We successfully apply the deep learning approach to the isotropic Allen-Cahn equation and to anisotropic dendritic growth simulation. We demonstrate that our physically-informed training provides better generalization in out-of-distribution evaluations than data-driven training, while achieving faster inference than traditional Fourier spectral methods.

ai.tldr: 提出一种结合能量分裂与物理信息学习的神经算子方法，用于加速相场模型的模拟。
ai.motivation: 相场模型的多尺度非线性特性需要精细的时空离散化，导致计算时间长。
ai.method: 采用Deep Ritz方法，训练神经算子近似相场模型的变分形式，并通过能量分裂变分公式训练以保持能量耗散特性；设计了专用的反应-扩散神经算子架构。
ai.result: 成功应用于各向同性Allen-Cahn方程和各向异性枝晶生长模拟，物理信息训练在分布外评估中比数据驱动训练泛化更好，推理速度比传统傅里叶谱方法更快。
ai.help_to_user: 为计算材料科学中相场模拟提供了一种高效、物理一致的加速方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将经典凸凹分裂方案与神经算子学习桥接，通过变分形式嵌入物理约束
idea_spark.risk: 对复杂各向异性或多物理场耦合的扩展性需验证
idea_spark.inspiration: 可用于其他具有能量结构的偏微分方程系统，如相分离、断裂力学等

papers.title: Late Fusion Neural Operators for Extrapolation Across Parameter Space in Partial Differential Equations
papers.summary: Developing neural operators that accurately predict the behavior of systems governed by partial differential equations (PDEs) across unseen parameter regimes is crucial for robust generalization in scientific and engineering applications. In practical applications, variations in physical parameters induce distribution shifts between training and prediction regimes, making extrapolation a central challenge. As a result, the way parameters are incorporated into neural operator models plays a key role in their ability to generalize, particularly when state and parameter representations are entangled. In this work, we introduce the Late Fusion Neural Operator, an architecture that disentangles learning state dynamics from parameter effects, improving predictive performance both within and beyond the training distribution. Our approach combines neural operators for learning latent state representations with sparse regression to incorporate parameter information in a structured manner. Across four benchmark PDEs including advection, Burgers, and both 1D and 2D reaction-diffusion equations, the proposed method consistently outperforms Fourier Neural Operator and CAPE-FNO. Late Fusion Neural Operators achieve consistently the best performance in all experiments, with an average RMSE reduction of 72.9% in-domain and 71.8% out-domain compared to the second-best method. These results demonstrate strong generalization across both in-domain and out-domain parameter regimes.

ai.tldr: 提出Late Fusion Neural Operator架构，通过解耦状态动态与参数效应学习，提升PDE参数空间外推性能，在四个基准PDE上超越现有方法。
ai.motivation: 实际应用中物理参数变化导致训练与预测数据分布偏移，现有神经算子模型参数融合方式限制其泛化能力，需改进参数处理机制。
ai.method: 结合神经算子学习潜在状态表示与稀疏回归结构化融合参数信息，实现状态动态与参数效应的解耦学习。
ai.result: 在平流、Burgers、1D/2D反应扩散四个PDE基准测试中，相比次优方法平均RMSE降低72.9%（域内）和71.8%（域外），均取得最佳性能。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理PDE参数外推问题提供解耦学习框架，通过结构化参数融合提升跨参数泛化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将稀疏回归与神经算子结合实现参数结构化注入

idea_spark.inspiration: 启发多模态学习中特征解耦与结构化融合的设计思路

papers.title: FLARE: A Data-Efficient Surrogate for Predicting Displacement Fields in Directed Energy Deposition
papers.summary: Directed energy deposition (DED) produces complex thermo-mechanical responses that can lead to distortion and reduced dimensional accuracy of a manufactured part. Thermo-mechanical finite element simulations are widely used to estimate these effects, but their computational cost and the complexity of accurately capturing DED physics limit their use in design iteration and process optimization. This paper introduces FLARE (Field Prediction via Linear Affine Reconstruction in wEight-space), a data-efficient surrogate modeling framework for predicting post-cooling displacement fields in DED from geometric and process parameters. We develop a predefined-geometry DED simulation workflow using an open-source finite element framework and generate a dataset of simulations with varying geometry, laser power, and deposition velocity. Each simulation provides full-field displacement, stress, strain, and temperature data throughout the manufacturing process. FLARE encodes each simulation as an implicit neural field and regularizes the corresponding neural-network weights so that they follow the affine structure of the input parameter space. This enables prediction of unseen parameter combinations by reconstructing network weights through affine mixing of training examples. On this DED benchmark, the method shows improved accuracy compared to baseline methods in both in-distribution and extrapolation settings. Although the present study focuses on DED displacement prediction, the proposed affine weight-space reconstruction framework offers a promising approach for data-efficient surrogate modeling of physical fields.

ai.tldr: FLARE是一种数据高效的代理建模框架，用于预测定向能量沉积工艺中冷却后的位移场。它通过仿射权重空间重构，利用少量训练数据即可预测新参数组合下的全场位移。
ai.motivation: 定向能量沉积工艺会产生复杂的热机械响应，导致零件变形和尺寸精度下降。传统有限元仿真计算成本高、物理建模复杂，限制了其在设计迭代和工艺优化中的应用。
ai.method: 开发了基于开源有限元框架的预定义几何DED仿真工作流，生成包含不同几何、激光功率和沉积速度的仿真数据集。FLARE将每个仿真编码为隐式神经场，并正则化神经网络权重使其遵循输入参数空间的仿射结构，通过训练样本的仿射混合重构网络权重来预测新参数组合。
ai.result: 在DED基准测试中，该方法在分布内和外推设置下均比基线方法表现出更高的精度。
ai.help_to_user: 为制造工艺仿真提供了一种数据高效的代理建模方案，显著降低计算成本，加速设计优化流程。

idea_spark.idea: 将物理仿真编码为神经场，通过参数空间的仿射结构正则化权重，实现数据高效的外推预测
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于参数空间的仿射假设，在高度非线性系统中可能失效
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将几何与工艺参数联合建模的思路，为多物理场耦合问题提供轻量化解决方案

en.tldr: FLARE is a data-efficient surrogate modeling framework for predicting post-cooling displacement fields in Directed Energy Deposition. It uses affine weight-space reconstruction to predict full-field displacements for new parameter combinations with limited training data.
en.motivation: Directed Energy Deposition produces complex thermo-mechanical responses leading to part distortion and reduced dimensional accuracy. Traditional finite element simulations are computationally expensive and physically complex, limiting their use in design iteration and process optimization.
en.method: Developed a predefined-geometry DED simulation workflow using an open-source finite element framework, generating a dataset with varying geometry, laser power, and deposition velocity. FLARE encodes each simulation as an implicit neural field and regularizes neural network weights to follow the affine structure of the input parameter space, enabling prediction of unseen parameter combinations through affine mixing of training examples.
en.result: On the DED benchmark, the method shows improved accuracy compared to baseline methods in both in-distribution and extrapolation settings.
en.help_to_user: Provides a data-efficient surrogate modeling solution for manufacturing process simulation, significantly reducing computational costs and accelerating design optimization workflows.

idea_spark.idea: Encode physical simulations as neural fields, regularize weights through the affine structure of parameter space to enable data-efficient extrapolation
idea_spark.risk: Method relies on affine assumptions about parameter space, which may fail in highly nonlinear systems
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires joint modeling of geometric and process parameters, providing lightweight solutions for multiphysics coupling problems

papers.title: Neural Shape Operator Surrogates -- Expression Rate Bounds
papers.summary: We prove error bounds for operator surrogates of solution operators for partial differential and boundary integral equations on families of domains which are diffeomorphic to one common reference (or latent) domain $D_{ref}$. The pullback of the PDE to $D_{ref}$ via affine-parametric shape encoding produces a collection of holomorphic parametric PDEs on $D_{ref}$. Sufficient conditions for (uniformly with respect to the parameter) well-posedness are given, implying existence, uniqueness and stability of parametric solution families on $D_{ref}$. We illustrate the abstract hypotheses by reviewing recent holomorphy results for a suite of elliptic and parabolic PDEs.   Quantified parametric holomorphy implies existence of finite-parametric, discrete approximations of the parametric solution families with convergence rates in terms of the number $N$ of parameters. We obtain constructive proofs of existence of Neural and Spectral Operator surrogates for the shape-to-solution maps with error bounds and convergence rate guarantees uniform on the collection of admissible shapes. We admit principal-component shape encoders and frame decoders.   Our results support in particular the (empirically reported) ability of neural operators to realize data-to-solution maps for elliptic and parabolic PDEs and BIEs that generalize across parametric families of shapes.

ai.tldr: 证明了在微分同胚于公共参考域的域族上，用于偏微分方程和边界积分方程解算子的神经与谱算子代理的误差界和收敛速率。
ai.motivation: 为神经算子实现跨参数化形状族的数据到解映射的泛化能力提供理论支持。
ai.method: 通过仿射参数形状编码将PDE拉回至参考域，得到全纯参数PDE族，分析其适定性，并构造有限参数离散逼近。
ai.result: 获得了形状到解映射的神经与谱算子代理的误差界和一致收敛速率保证。
ai.help_to_user: 为在参数化形状族上使用神经算子求解PDE/BIP提供了严格的理论收敛性框架。

idea_spark.idea: 利用全纯参数依赖性证明有限参数逼近的存在性，进而构造具有误差保证的算子代理。
idea_spark.risk: 理论假设（如一致适定性、全纯性）在实际复杂几何或非线性问题中可能难以验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 该框架可扩展至其他参数不确定性（如材料属性、边界条件）的算子学习理论分析。

en.tldr: Proves error bounds and convergence rates for neural and spectral operator surrogates of solution operators for PDEs and BIEs on families of domains diffeomorphic to a common reference domain.
en.motivation: To provide theoretical support for the empirically observed ability of neural operators to generalize data-to-solution maps across parametric shape families.
en.method: Pull back PDEs to a reference domain via affine-parametric shape encoding, yielding holomorphic parametric PDE families. Analyze well-posedness and construct finite-parametric discrete approximations.
en.result: Obtains constructive proofs for existence of neural/spectral operator surrogates for shape-to-solution maps with error bounds and uniform convergence rate guarantees.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous theoretical convergence framework for using neural operators to solve PDEs/BIEs on parametric shape families.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage holomorphic parametric dependence to prove existence of finite-parametric approximations, then construct operator surrogates with error guarantees.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical assumptions (e.g., uniform well-posedness, holomorphy) may be difficult to verify for complex geometries or nonlinear problems.
idea_spark.inspiration: The framework could be extended to theoretical analysis of operator learning for other parametric uncertainties (e.g., material properties, boundary conditions).

papers.title: Neural Adjoint Method for Meta-optics: Accelerating Volumetric Inverse Design via Fourier Neural Operators
papers.summary: Meta-optics promises compact, high-performance imaging and color routing. However, designing high-performance structures is a high-dimensional optimization problem: mapping a desired optical output back to a physical 3D structure requires solving computationally expensive Maxwell's equations iteratively. Even with adjoint optimization, broadband design can require thousands of Maxwell solves, making industrial-scale optimization slow and costly. To overcome this challenge, we propose the Neural Adjoint Method, a solver-supervised surrogate that predicts 3D adjoint gradient fields from a voxelized permittivity volume using a Fourier Neural Operator (FNO). By learning the dense, per-voxel sensitivity field that drives gradient-based updates, our method can replace per-iteration adjoint solves with fast predictions, greatly reducing the computational cost of full-wave simulations required during iterative refinement. To better preserve sensitivity peaks, we introduce a stage-wise FNO that progressively refines residual errors with increasing emphasis on higher-frequency components. We curate a meta-optics dataset from paired forward/adjoint FDTD simulations and evaluate it across three tasks: spectral sorting (color routers), achromatic focusing (metalenses), and waveguide mode conversion. Our method reduces design time from hours to seconds. These results suggest a practical route toward fast, large-scale volumetric meta-optical design enabled by AI-accelerated scientific computing.

ai.tldr: 提出神经伴随方法，利用傅里叶神经算子预测三维伴随梯度场，将元光学体积逆向设计时间从数小时缩短至数秒。
ai.motivation: 元光学高性能结构设计是高维优化问题，传统伴随优化需要数千次昂贵的麦克斯韦方程求解，计算成本高。
ai.method: 构建由求解器监督的代理模型，使用傅里叶神经算子从体素化介电常数体积预测三维伴随梯度场；引入分阶段FNO逐步细化残差误差，更好保留灵敏度峰值。
ai.result: 在光谱分选、消色差聚焦和波导模式转换三个任务上验证，设计时间从小时级缩短至秒级。
ai.help_to_user: 为大规模体积元光学设计提供了AI加速的科学计算实用路径。

idea_spark.idea: 用分阶段神经网络渐进学习物理场中不同频率分量的残差，可能提升对尖锐特征（如边界层、奇点）的建模能力。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于配对的正向/伴随仿真数据集的质量和覆盖范围；对未见几何或材料参数的泛化能力需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将“学习梯度场而非直接输出”的范式与多尺度神经架构结合，可能为其他迭代优化问题（如拓扑优化、控制）提供新加速方案。

idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于配对的正向/伴随仿真数据集的质量和覆盖范围；对未见几何或材料参数的泛化能力需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将“学习梯度场而非直接输出”的范式与多尺度神经架构结合，可能为其他迭代优化问题（如拓扑优化、控制）提供新加速方案。

en.tldr: Proposes the Neural Adjoint Method using Fourier Neural Operators to predict 3D adjoint gradient fields, reducing volumetric meta-optics inverse design time from hours to seconds.
en.motivation: Designing high-performance meta-optical structures is a high-dimensional optimization problem; traditional adjoint optimization requires thousands of costly Maxwell solves, making industrial-scale optimization slow.
en.method: Builds a solver-supervised surrogate model that uses a Fourier Neural Operator to predict 3D adjoint gradient fields from a voxelized permittivity volume; introduces a stage-wise FNO to progressively refine residual errors with increasing emphasis on higher-frequency components, better preserving sensitivity peaks.
en.result: Validated on three tasks: spectral sorting, achromatic focusing, and waveguide mode conversion, reducing design time from hours to seconds.
en.help_to_user: Provides a practical route toward fast, large-scale volumetric meta-optical design enabled by AI-accelerated scientific computing.

idea_spark.idea: Using a stage-wise neural network to progressively learn residuals of different frequency components in physical fields may improve modeling of sharp features (e.g., boundary layers, singularities).
idea_spark.risk: The method relies on the quality and coverage of paired forward/adjoint simulation datasets; generalization to unseen geometries or material parameters requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining the paradigm of 'learning gradient fields rather than direct outputs' with multi-scale neural architectures may offer new acceleration schemes for other iterative optimization problems (e.g., topology optimization, control).

papers.title: Singularity Formation: Synergy in Theoretical, Numerical and Machine Learning Approaches
papers.summary: This thesis develops numerical and theoretical approaches for understanding and analyzing singularity formation in Partial Differential Equations (PDEs). The singularity formation in the Navier-Stokes Equation (NSE) is famously challenging as one of the seven Clay Prize problems. Unlike simpler equations such as the Nonlinear Heat (NLH) or Keller-Segel (KS) equations, where formal asymptotics near blowup are better understood, the intrinsic complexity of NSE makes quantitative analytical treatment difficult, if not impossible, without numerical guidance.   Building on numerical insights, we introduce a robust analytical framework to simplify and systematize pen-and-paper proofs for simpler singular PDEs. We present a novel approach based on enforcing vanishing modulation conditions for perturbations around approximate blowup profiles, complemented by singularly weighted energy estimates. We demonstrate the efficacy of our method on PDEs with complicated asymptotics, such as NLH and the Complex Ginzburg-Landau (CGL) equation, and address the open problem of singularity formation in the 3D KS equation with logistic damping.   We develop and refine numerical approaches that facilitate deeper insights into singularity formation. We demonstrate that machine learning methods significantly enhance our capability to identify and characterize potential blowup solutions with high precision. We improve on existing Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) and Neural Operator (NO) frameworks. Moreover, we present a novel machine learning paradigm, the Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) architecture, whose interpretability and excellent scaling properties are achieved through learnable nonlinearities.

ai.tldr: 本论文开发了数值与理论方法，用于理解和分析偏微分方程中的奇点形成，特别针对纳维-斯托克斯方程等复杂问题，并引入了机器学习增强的数值框架。
ai.motivation: 纳维-斯托克斯方程的奇点形成是千禧年难题之一，其内在复杂性使得纯解析处理极为困难，需要数值指导。
ai.method: 结合数值洞察，建立了基于扰动调制条件与奇异加权能量估计的解析框架；改进并提出了PINN、神经算子及可解释的KAN架构等机器学习方法。
ai.result: 方法在非线性热方程、复金兹堡-朗道方程等复杂渐近行为PDE上验证有效，并处理了带逻辑阻尼的3D Keller-Segel方程的奇点形成问题。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了系统化的奇点分析框架与机器学习增强的数值工具，可用于复杂PDE的奇点研究与数值模拟。

idea_spark.idea: 利用机器学习（特别是KAN）的高精度与可解释性来识别与表征潜在的爆破解，辅助理论分析。
idea_spark.risk: 对极高维或随机PDE的奇点，当前方法可能面临维数灾难与训练不稳定性；理论框架对强非线性耦合系统的推广仍需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索KAN在湍流直接数值模拟中的替代作用，或将其可解释性用于发现未知的奇异标度律。

en.tldr: This thesis develops numerical and theoretical approaches to understand and analyze singularity formation in PDEs, particularly for challenging cases like the Navier-Stokes equations, and introduces machine learning-enhanced frameworks.
en.motivation: Singularity formation in the Navier-Stokes equations is a Clay Millennium Prize problem whose intrinsic complexity makes purely analytical treatment extremely difficult without numerical guidance.
en.method: Established an analytical framework combining vanishing modulation conditions for perturbations around blowup profiles with singularly weighted energy estimates, based on numerical insights; improved and proposed machine learning methods including PINNs, Neural Operators, and the interpretable KAN architecture.
en.result: The method proved effective on PDEs with complicated asymptotics like the Nonlinear Heat and Complex Ginzburg-Landau equations, and addressed singularity formation in the 3D Keller-Segel equation with logistic damping.
en.help_to_user: Provides a systematic framework for singularity analysis and machine learning-enhanced numerical tools for studying blowup in complex PDEs and performing simulations.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage the high precision and interpretability of machine learning (especially KAN) to identify and characterize potential blowup solutions, aiding theoretical analysis.
idea_spark.risk: For very high-dimensional or stochastic PDE singularities, current methods may face the curse of dimensionality and training instability; generalization of the theoretical framework to strongly nonlinear coupled systems requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore KAN as a surrogate in direct numerical simulation of turbulence, or use its interpretability to discover unknown singular scaling laws.

papers.title: Faster by Design: Interactive Aerodynamics via Neural Surrogates Trained on Expert-Validated CFD
papers.summary: Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) is central to race-car aerodynamic development, yet its cost -- tens of thousands of core-hours per high-fidelity evaluation -- severely limits the design space exploration feasible within realistic budgets. AI-based surrogate models promise to alleviate this bottleneck, but progress has been constrained by the limited complexity of public datasets, which are dominated by smoothed passenger-car shapes that fail to exercise surrogates on the thin, complex, highly loaded components governing motorsport performance. This work presents three primary contributions. First, we introduce a high-fidelity RANS dataset built on a parametric LMP2-class CAD model and spanning six operating conditions (map points) covering straight-line and cornering regimes, generated and validated by aerodynamics experts at Dallara to preserve features relevant to industrial motorsport. Second, we present the Gauge-Invariant Spectral Transformer (GIST), a graph-based neural operator whose spectral embeddings encode mesh connectivity to enhance predictions on tightly packed, complex geometries. GIST guarantees discretization invariance and scales linearly with mesh size, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy on both public benchmarks and the proposed race-car dataset. Third, we demonstrate that GIST achieves a level of predictive accuracy suitable for early-stage aerodynamic design, providing a first validation of the concept of interactive design-space exploration -- where engineers query a surrogate in place of the CFD solver -- within industrial motorsport workflows.

ai.tldr: 本研究提出了GIST（规范不变谱变换器），一种基于图的神经算子，用于构建高精度CFD代理模型，并发布了由专家验证的赛车高保真RANS数据集，实现了赛车空气动力学交互式设计探索。
ai.motivation: 计算流体动力学（CFD）在赛车空气动力学开发中至关重要，但每次高保真评估需要数万核心小时，严重限制了实际预算内的设计空间探索。现有公开数据集主要基于平滑的乘用车形状，无法充分测试代理模型在决定赛车性能的薄、复杂、高负载部件上的表现。
ai.method: 1. 引入基于参数化LMP2级CAD模型的高保真RANS数据集，涵盖直线和转弯六种工况，由Dallara空气动力学专家生成验证；2. 提出GIST神经算子，其谱嵌入编码网格连接性，保证离散不变性且随网格大小线性扩展；3. 在公开基准和赛车数据集上实现最先进精度。
ai.result: GIST在公开基准和提出的赛车数据集上达到最先进精度，其预测精度适用于早期空气动力学设计，首次验证了在工业赛车工作流程中用代理模型替代CFD求解器进行交互式设计空间探索的概念。
ai.help_to_user: 为AI4Science研究人员提供了专为复杂几何优化的高性能神经算子架构（GIST）和高质量的领域特定数据集，展示了如何将代理模型集成到工业设计流程中实现交互式探索。

idea_spark.idea: 开发一个轻量级插件，将训练好的GIST代理模型集成到主流CAD/CAE软件中，允许工程师实时调整参数并可视化流场预测，形成设计-反馈闭环。
idea_spark.risk: 代理模型的精度严重依赖于训练数据的质量和覆盖范围。对于训练数据未覆盖的极端设计或工况，预测可能不可靠。需要建立不确定性量化机制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 本研究证明了针对特定领域几何特征（如赛车薄壁组件）定制神经算子架构的重要性。启发我们思考：在其他科学领域（如生物分子结构、地质构造），哪些独特的几何或拓扑特征需要专门的模型归纳偏置？

en.tldr: This work presents GIST (Gauge-Invariant Spectral Transformer), a graph-based neural operator for building high-accuracy CFD surrogate models, and releases an expert-validated high-fidelity RANS dataset for race cars, enabling interactive design-space exploration for automotive aerodynamics.
en.motivation: Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) is central to race-car aerodynamic development, but its cost -- tens of thousands of core-hours per high-fidelity evaluation -- severely limits design space exploration. Public datasets are dominated by smoothed passenger-car shapes that fail to exercise surrogates on the thin, complex, highly loaded components governing motorsport performance.
en.method: 1. Introduce a high-fidelity RANS dataset based on a parametric LMP2-class CAD model spanning six operating conditions, generated and validated by Dallara aerodynamics experts; 2. Propose GIST, a graph-based neural operator whose spectral embeddings encode mesh connectivity, guaranteeing discretization invariance and scaling linearly with mesh size; 3. Achieve state-of-the-art accuracy on both public benchmarks and the race-car dataset.
en.result: GIST achieves state-of-the-art accuracy on public benchmarks and the proposed race-car dataset. Its predictive accuracy is suitable for early-stage aerodynamic design, providing the first validation of interactive design-space exploration using surrogates instead of CFD solvers within industrial motorsport workflows.
en.help_to_user: Provides AI4Science researchers with a high-performance neural operator architecture (GIST) optimized for complex geometries and a high-quality domain-specific dataset, demonstrating how to integrate surrogate models into industrial design workflows for interactive exploration.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a lightweight plugin to integrate trained GIST surrogate models into mainstream CAD/CAE software, allowing engineers to adjust parameters in real-time and visualize flow field predictions, creating a design-feedback loop.
idea_spark.risk: Surrogate model accuracy heavily depends on training data quality and coverage. Predictions may be unreliable for extreme designs or conditions not covered in training data. Uncertainty quantification mechanisms are needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: This study demonstrates the importance of customizing neural operator architectures for domain-specific geometric features (e.g., race-car thin-walled components). It inspires us to consider: in other scientific domains (e.g., biomolecular structures, geological formations), what unique geometric or topological features require specialized model inductive biases?

papers.title: Attraction, Repulsion, and Friction: Introducing DMF, a Friction-Augmented Drifting Model
papers.summary: Drifting Models [Deng et al., 2026] train a one-step generator by evolving samples under a kernel-based drift field, avoiding ODE integration at inference. The original analysis leaves two questions open. The drift-field iteration admits a locally repulsive regime in a two-particle surrogate, and vanishing of the drift ($V_{p,q}\equiv 0$) is not known to force the learned distribution $q$ to match the target $p$. We derive a contraction threshold for the surrogate and show that a linearly-scheduled friction coefficient gives a finite-horizon bound on the error trajectory. Under a Gaussian kernel we prove that the drift-field equilibrium is identifiable: vanishing of $V_{p,q}$ on any open set forces $q=p$, closing the converse of Proposition 3.1 of Deng et al. Our friction-augmented model, DMF (Drifting Model with Friction), matches or exceeds Optimal Flow Matching on FFHQ adult-to-child domain translation at 16x lower training compute.

ai.tldr: 本文提出DMF，一种带摩擦力的漂移模型，通过引入线性调度的摩擦系数，解决了原始漂移模型中局部排斥和平衡点识别问题，在图像域转换任务上以更低计算成本达到或超过最优流匹配性能。
ai.motivation: 原始漂移模型存在两个未解决问题：漂移场迭代在双粒子代理中允许局部排斥状态，且漂移消失不能保证学习分布匹配目标分布。
ai.method: 推导代理模型的收缩阈值，引入线性调度的摩擦系数以获得误差轨迹的有限时域界，并在高斯核下证明漂移场平衡点的可识别性。
ai.result: DMF在FFHQ成人到儿童域转换任务上，以16倍更低的训练计算成本，达到或超过最优流匹配的性能。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种更高效、理论更完备的生成模型训练方法，特别适用于需要快速推理的域转换任务。

idea_spark.idea: 在动力学模型中引入受控耗散（摩擦）以稳定训练并保证收敛。
idea_spark.risk: 理论分析依赖于特定核函数（高斯核），在其他核函数下的泛化能力需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 物理中的摩擦概念可用于约束生成模型中的粒子轨迹，防止发散或振荡。

idea_spark.risk: 理论分析依赖于特定核函数（高斯核），在其他核函数下的泛化能力需验证。

en.tldr: This paper introduces DMF, a friction-augmented drifting model that addresses local repulsion and equilibrium identifiability in original drift models via a linearly-scheduled friction coefficient, matching or exceeding Optimal Flow Matching on image domain translation with 16× lower training compute.
en.motivation: Two open questions in the original drifting model: the drift-field iteration admits a locally repulsive regime in a two-particle surrogate, and vanishing drift does not guarantee the learned distribution matches the target.
en.method: Derive a contraction threshold for the surrogate, introduce a linearly-scheduled friction coefficient for a finite-horizon error bound, and prove drift-field equilibrium identifiability under a Gaussian kernel.
en.result: DMF matches or exceeds Optimal Flow Matching on FFHQ adult-to-child domain translation at 16× lower training compute.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more efficient and theoretically sound generative model training approach, particularly suitable for domain translation tasks requiring fast inference.

idea_spark.idea: Introduce controlled dissipation (friction) into dynamical systems to stabilize training and ensure convergence.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical analysis relies on specific kernel functions (Gaussian); generalization to other kernels needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: The physical concept of friction can constrain particle trajectories in generative models to prevent divergence or oscillation.

papers.title: FlowRefiner: Flow Matching-Based Iterative Refinement for 3D Turbulent Flow Simulation
papers.summary: Accurate autoregressive prediction of 3D turbulent flows remains challenging for neural PDE solvers, as small errors in fine-scale structures can accumulate rapidly over rollout. In this paper, we propose FlowRefiner, a flow matching-based iterative refinement framework for 3D turbulent flow simulation. The method replaces stochastic denoising refinement with deterministic ODE-based correction, uses a unified velocity-field regression objective across all refinement stages, and introduces a decoupled sigma schedule that fixes the noise range independently of refinement depth. These design choices yield stable and effective refinement in the small-noise regime. Experiments on large-scale 3D turbulence with rich multi-scale structures show that FlowRefiner achieves state-of-the-art autoregressive prediction accuracy and strong physical consistency. Although developed for turbulent flow simulation, the proposed framework is broadly applicable to iterative refinement problems in scientific modeling.

ai.tldr: FlowRefiner是一种基于流匹配的迭代细化框架，用于提高3D湍流模拟的精度和稳定性。
ai.motivation: 神经PDE求解器在3D湍流自回归预测中，细微结构的小误差会在推演中快速累积，导致精度下降。
ai.method: 用确定性ODE修正替代随机去噪细化；所有细化阶段使用统一的流场回归目标；引入解耦的噪声调度，使噪声范围独立于细化深度。
ai.result: 在大规模多尺度3D湍流实验中，实现了最先进的自回归预测精度和强物理一致性。
ai.help_to_user: 为3D湍流模拟提供了一种稳定有效的迭代细化方法，框架可推广至科学建模中的其他迭代细化问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将随机去噪过程替换为确定性ODE修正，可能提升迭代过程的稳定性和收敛速度。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于准确的流匹配训练，在极高雷诺数或极端流动条件下可能需调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 解耦的噪声调度设计启发了如何将噪声控制与迭代深度分离，以保持细化稳定性。

zh.result: 在大规模多尺度3D湍流实验中，实现了最先进的自回归预测精度和强物理一致性。
zh.help_to_user: 为3D湍流模拟提供了一种稳定有效的迭代细化方法，框架可推广至科学建模中的其他迭代细化问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将随机去噪过程替换为确定性ODE修正，可能提升迭代过程的稳定性和收敛速度。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于准确的流匹配训练，在极高雷诺数或极端流动条件下可能需调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 解耦的噪声调度设计启发了如何将噪声控制与迭代深度分离，以保持细化稳定性。

en.tldr: FlowRefiner is a flow matching-based iterative refinement framework for improving the accuracy and stability of 3D turbulent flow simulation.
en.motivation: Small errors in fine-scale structures can accumulate rapidly during autoregressive prediction of 3D turbulent flows by neural PDE solvers, degrading accuracy.
en.method: Replaces stochastic denoising refinement with deterministic ODE-based correction; uses a unified velocity-field regression objective across all refinement stages; introduces a decoupled sigma schedule that fixes the noise range independently of refinement depth.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art autoregressive prediction accuracy and strong physical consistency on large-scale 3D turbulence with rich multi-scale structures.
en.help_to_user: Provides a stable and effective iterative refinement method for 3D turbulent flow simulation, with a framework broadly applicable to iterative refinement problems in scientific modeling.

idea_spark.idea: Replacing stochastic denoising with deterministic ODE correction may improve the stability and convergence speed of iterative processes.
idea_spark.risk: The method relies on accurate flow matching training and may require adjustments for extremely high Reynolds numbers or extreme flow conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: The decoupled sigma schedule design inspires how to separate noise control from iteration depth to maintain refinement stability.

papers.title: Neuroscience Inspired Graph Operators Towards Edge-Deployable Virtual Sensing for Irregular Geometries
papers.summary: Predicting full-field physics through the real-time virtual sensing of engineering systems can enhance limited physical sensors but often requires sparse-to-dense reconstruction, complex multiphysics, and highly irregular geometries as well as strict latency and energy constraints for edge-deployability. Neural operators have been presented as a potential candidate for such applications but few architectures exist that explicitly address power consumption. Spiking neuron integration can provide a potential solution when integrated on neuromorphic hardware but the current existing neuron models result in severe performance degradation towards regression-based virtual sensing. To address the performance concerns and edge-constraints, we present the Variable Spiking Graph Neural Operator (VS-GNO) which integrates a sophisticated spectral-spatial convolutional analysis and a previously developed Variable Spiking Neuron (VSN) and energy-error balance loss function. With a non-spiking $L_2$ error baseline of $0.4\%$, VS-GNO can provide a reconstruction error of $0.71\%$ with $15\%$ average spiking in its spectral-only form and $1.04\%$ with $24.5\%$ spiking in its entire form. These results position VS-GNO as a promising step towards energy-efficient, edge-deployable neural operators for real-time sparse-to-dense virtual sensing in complex, highly irregular engineering environments.

ai.tldr: 提出VS-GNO，一种结合可变脉冲神经元和图神经算子的模型，用于不规则几何体的边缘可部署虚拟传感，在低脉冲率下实现接近非脉冲模型的精度。
ai.motivation: 工程系统的实时全场物理预测（虚拟传感）面临稀疏到稠密重建、复杂不规则几何、严格延迟和能耗等边缘部署约束。现有脉冲神经元模型在回归任务中性能下降严重。
ai.method: 集成谱-空间卷积分析与可变脉冲神经元（VSN），采用能量-误差平衡损失函数，构建可变脉冲图神经算子（VS-GNO）。
ai.result: 在非脉冲L2误差基线0.4%下，仅谱形式的VS-GNO实现0.71%重建误差（平均脉冲率15%），完整形式实现1.04%误差（脉冲率24.5%）。
ai.help_to_user: 为复杂不规则工程环境中的实时稀疏到稠密虚拟传感提供了一种高能效、边缘可部署的神经算子解决方案。

idea_spark.idea: 在自动驾驶车辆的实时结构健康监测中，使用类似的脉冲图算子处理传感器稀疏的车辆骨架振动数据，进行全场应力预测。
idea_spark.risk: 脉冲模型在更复杂多物理场耦合或动态负载下的泛化能力未经验证；硬件部署的实际能效增益需实测。
idea_spark.inspiration: 神经科学的脉冲编码机制如何与工程系统的几何拓扑表示（图）高效结合，为边缘物理AI提供新范式。

en.tldr: Proposes VS-GNO, a model integrating variable spiking neurons with graph neural operators for edge-deployable virtual sensing on irregular geometries, achieving near-non-spiking accuracy at low spiking rates.
en.motivation: Real-time full-field physics prediction (virtual sensing) for engineering systems faces edge-deployment constraints: sparse-to-dense reconstruction, complex irregular geometries, strict latency, and energy limits. Existing spiking neuron models suffer severe performance degradation in regression tasks.
en.method: Integrates spectral-spatial convolutional analysis with Variable Spiking Neurons (VSN) and an energy-error balance loss function to construct the Variable Spiking Graph Neural Operator (VS-GNO).
en.result: With a non-spiking L2 error baseline of 0.4%, VS-GNO achieves 0.71% reconstruction error (15% average spiking) in spectral-only form and 1.04% error (24.5% spiking) in full form.
en.help_to_user: Provides an energy-efficient, edge-deployable neural operator solution for real-time sparse-to-dense virtual sensing in complex, irregular engineering environments.

idea_spark.idea: Apply a similar spiking graph operator for real-time full-field stress prediction on vehicle frames using sparse vibration sensor data, for structural health monitoring in autonomous vehicles.
idea_spark.risk: Generalization of spiking models to more complex multiphysics coupling or dynamic loads unverified; actual hardware energy efficiency gains require physical measurement.
idea_spark.inspiration: How neuroscientific spiking coding mechanisms can be efficiently combined with geometric/topological representations (graphs) of engineering systems, offering a new paradigm for edge physical AI.

papers.title: On the Generalization Bounds of Symbolic Regression with Genetic Programming
papers.summary: Symbolic regression (SR) with genetic programming (GP) aims to discover interpretable mathematical expressions directly from data. Despite its strong empirical success, the theoretical understanding of why GP-based SR generalizes beyond the training data remains limited. In this work, we provide a learning-theoretic analysis of SR models represented as expression trees. We derive a generalization bound for GP-style SR under constraints on tree size, depth, and learnable constants. Our result decomposes the generalization gap into two interpretable components: a structure-selection term, reflecting the combinatorial complexity of choosing an expression-tree structure, and a constant-fitting term, capturing the complexity of optimizing numerical constants within a fixed structure. This decomposition provides a theoretical perspective on several widely used practices in GP, including parsimony pressure, depth limits, numerically stable operators, and interval arithmetic. In particular, our analysis shows how structural restrictions reduce hypothesis-class growth while stability mechanisms control the sensitivity of predictions to parameter perturbations. By linking these practical design choices to explicit complexity terms in the generalization bound, our work offers a principled explanation for commonly observed empirical behaviors in GP-based SR and contributes towards a more rigorous understanding of its generalization properties.

ai.tldr: 本文为基于遗传编程的符号回归提供了泛化界分析，将泛化误差分解为结构选择项和常数拟合项，从理论上解释了常见实践的有效性。
ai.motivation: 尽管基于遗传编程的符号回归在实证上很成功，但其为何能泛化到训练数据之外的理论理解仍然有限。
ai.method: 对以表达式树表示的符号回归模型进行学习理论分析，在树大小、深度和可学习常数受限的条件下推导泛化界。
ai.result: 泛化界被分解为两个可解释的组成部分：结构选择项和常数拟合项，为遗传编程中的常见设计选择（如简约性压力、深度限制、数值稳定算子和区间算术）提供了理论视角。
ai.help_to_user: 为理解符号回归模型的泛化能力提供了理论框架，并解释了常见实践背后的原理。

idea_spark.idea: 将符号回归的泛化性能与模型结构的选择空间和常数优化的稳定性联系起来，为算法设计提供理论指导。
idea_spark.risk: 理论分析基于特定的约束条件（如树大小、深度限制），在实际无约束或更复杂场景下的适用性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对其它基于搜索的机器学习方法（如神经架构搜索）进行类似的泛化理论分析，或将稳定性机制（如区间算术）推广到其他数值优化问题中。

en.tldr: This work provides a generalization bound analysis for genetic programming-based symbolic regression, decomposing the generalization error into structure-selection and constant-fitting terms, offering a theoretical explanation for common practices.
en.motivation: Despite strong empirical success, the theoretical understanding of why GP-based symbolic regression generalizes beyond the training data remains limited.
en.method: A learning-theoretic analysis of SR models represented as expression trees, deriving a generalization bound under constraints on tree size, depth, and learnable constants.
en.result: The generalization bound is decomposed into two interpretable components: a structure-selection term and a constant-fitting term, providing a theoretical perspective on common GP design choices like parsimony pressure, depth limits, and interval arithmetic.
en.help_to_user: Provides a theoretical framework for understanding the generalization of symbolic regression models and explains the principles behind common practices.

idea_spark.idea: Linking the generalization performance of symbolic regression to the choice space of model structures and the stability of constant optimization provides theoretical guidance for algorithm design.
idea_spark.risk: The theoretical analysis relies on specific constraints (e.g., tree size, depth limits); its applicability to unconstrained or more complex real-world scenarios requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires similar generalization theory analyses for other search-based ML methods (e.g., neural architecture search), or the extension of stability mechanisms (e.g., interval arithmetic) to other numerical optimization problems.

papers.title: Autoregressive prediction of 2D MHD dynamics inferred from deep learning modeling
papers.summary: We develop two deep learning surrogate autoregressive models for the prediction of the temporal evolution of two-dimensional ideal magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) Kelvin-Helmholtz instabilities across a range of magnetic field strengths. Using two neural network architectures, a Koopman-based Transformer model and a ConvLSTM-UNet, our approach enables simultaneous prediction of vorticity and current density directly from high-resolution simulations. The models are trained in an autoregressive manner and are able to reproduce key features of the multiscale dynamics over several instability growth and nonlinear saturation phases. Beyond accurate field reconstruction, the surrogates preserve essential physical structures of ideal MHD dynamics, including the conservation trends of global invariants and the propagation of Alfvénic fluctuations. Compared to direct numerical simulations, the proposed surrogates offer substantially reduced computational cost while maintaining good agreement with the reference dynamics. These results suggest that deep learning based surrogate models can provide a promising complementary tool for the efficient and physically consistent exploration of high-fidelity plasma and fluid simulations.

ai.tldr: 开发了两种深度学习自回归代理模型，用于预测二维理想磁流体动力学中不同磁场强度下的开尔文-亥姆霍兹不稳定性演化。
ai.motivation: 高保真等离子体和流体模拟计算成本高昂，需要开发高效且物理一致的替代工具。
ai.method: 使用基于Koopman的Transformer模型和ConvLSTM-UNet两种神经网络架构，以自回归方式训练，直接从高分辨率模拟中同时预测涡度和电流密度。
ai.result: 模型能够再现多尺度动力学的关键特征，保持理想磁流体动力学的基本物理结构，包括全局不变量的守恒趋势和阿尔芬波动的传播，同时大幅降低计算成本。
ai.help_to_user: 为高效探索高保真等离子体和流体模拟提供了有前景的补充工具。

idea_spark.idea: 将Koopman算子理论与Transformer结合用于动力系统建模。
idea_spark.risk: 模型在极端参数或长时间预测中可能累积误差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将物理守恒律约束融入深度学习架构，增强代理模型的物理一致性。

en.tldr: Developed two deep learning autoregressive surrogate models for predicting the temporal evolution of 2D ideal MHD Kelvin-Helmholtz instabilities across varying magnetic field strengths.
en.motivation: High-fidelity plasma and fluid simulations are computationally expensive, necessitating efficient and physically consistent complementary tools.
en.method: Employed two neural network architectures—a Koopman-based Transformer model and a ConvLSTM-UNet—trained autoregressively to simultaneously predict vorticity and current density directly from high-resolution simulations.
en.result: The models reproduce key features of multiscale dynamics, preserve essential physical structures of ideal MHD (including conservation trends of global invariants and propagation of Alfvénic fluctuations), and substantially reduce computational cost while maintaining good agreement with reference dynamics.

idea_spark.idea: Combining Koopman operator theory with Transformer for dynamical system modeling.
idea_spark.risk: Models may accumulate errors under extreme parameters or long-term predictions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires incorporating physical conservation law constraints into deep learning architectures to enhance physical consistency of surrogate models.

papers.title: Geometric coherence of single-cell CRISPR perturbations reveals regulatory architecture and predicts cellular stress
papers.summary: Genome engineering has achieved remarkable sequence-level precision, yet predicting the transcriptomic state that a cell will occupy after perturbation remains an open problem. Single-cell CRISPR screens measure how far cells move from their unperturbed state, but this effect magnitude ignores a fundamental question: do the cells move together? Two perturbations with identical magnitude can produce qualitatively different outcomes if one drives cells coherently along a shared trajectory while the other scatters them across expression space. We introduce a geometric stability metric, Shesha, that quantifies the directional coherence of single-cell perturbation responses as the mean cosine similarity between individual cell shift vectors and the mean perturbation direction. Across five CRISPR datasets (2,200+ perturbations spanning CRISPRa, CRISPRi, and pooled screens), stability correlates strongly with effect magnitude (Spearman $ρ=0.75-0.97$), with a calibrated cross-dataset correlation of 0.97. Crucially, discordant cases where the two metrics decouple expose regulatory architecture: pleiotropic master regulators such as CEBPA and GATA1 pay a "geometric tax," producing large but incoherent shifts, while lineage-specific factors such as KLF1 produce tightly coordinated responses. After controlling for magnitude, geometric instability is independently associated with elevated chaperone activation (HSPA5/BiP; $ρ_{partial}=-0.34$ and $-0.21$ across datasets), and the high-stability/high-stress quadrant is systematically depleted. The magnitude-stability relationship persists in scGPT foundation model embeddings, confirming it is a property of biological state space rather than linear projection. Perturbation stability provides a complementary axis for hit prioritization in screens, phenotypic quality control in cell manufacturing, and evaluation of in silico perturbation predictions.

ai.tldr: 本文提出几何稳定性指标Shesha，量化单细胞CRISPR扰动响应的方向一致性，揭示调控架构并预测细胞应激。
ai.motivation: 基因组工程已实现序列层面的精确编辑，但预测细胞在扰动后将占据的转录组状态仍是一个开放问题。现有方法仅关注效应大小，忽略了细胞是否沿共同轨迹移动这一根本问题。
ai.method: 引入几何稳定性指标Shesha，计算单个细胞位移向量与平均扰动方向之间的平均余弦相似度，以量化扰动响应的方向一致性。在五个CRISPR数据集（涵盖CRISPRa、CRISPRi和混合筛选，超过2200个扰动）上进行分析，并与效应大小、分子伴侣激活等关联。
ai.result: 稳定性与效应大小强相关（Spearman ρ=0.75-0.97）。两者解耦的案例揭示了调控架构：多效性主调节因子（如CEBPA、GATA1）产生大但非相干的位移，而谱系特异性因子（如KLF1）产生紧密协调的响应。控制效应大小后，几何不稳定性独立与分子伴侣HSPA5/BiP激活升高相关。该关系在scGPT基础模型嵌入中持续存在。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种基于扰动响应方向一致性的新分析维度，可用于筛选中的命中优先级排序、细胞制造中的表型质量控制，以及评估计算扰动预测。

idea_spark.idea: 将Shesha指标应用于空间转录组数据，评估局部细胞群体对扰动的协调响应。
idea_spark.risk: 指标对数据降维方法和噪声敏感，可能需要针对不同技术平台进行校准。
idea_spark.inspiration: 方向一致性作为细胞状态空间的内在属性，可能为理解细胞命运决定的鲁棒性提供新视角。

Existing methods focus only on effect magnitude, ignoring the fundamental question of whether cells move together along a shared trajectory.
en.method: Introduces the geometric stability metric Shesha, which calculates the mean cosine similarity between individual cell shift vectors and the mean perturbation direction to quantify directional coherence of perturbation responses. Analyzes five CRISPR datasets (spanning CRISPRa, CRISPRi, and pooled screens, over 2,200 perturbations) and correlates stability with effect magnitude, chaperone activation, etc.
en.result: Stability correlates strongly with effect magnitude (Spearman ρ=0.75-0.97). Discordant cases where the two metrics decouple expose regulatory architecture: pleiotropic master regulators (e.g., CEBPA, GATA1) produce large but incoherent shifts, while lineage-specific factors (e.g., KLF1) produce tightly coordinated responses. After controlling for magnitude, geometric instability is independently associated with elevated chaperone HSPA5/BiP activation. The relationship persists in scGPT foundation model embeddings.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new analytical dimension based on directional coherence of perturbation responses, useful for hit prioritization in screens, phenotypic quality control in cell manufacturing, and evaluation of in silico perturbation predictions.

idea_spark.idea: Apply the Shesha metric to spatial transcriptomics data to assess coordinated responses of local cell populations to perturbations.
idea_spark.risk: The metric is sensitive to data dimensionality reduction methods and noise, potentially requiring calibration for different technological platforms.
idea_spark.inspiration: Directional coherence as an intrinsic property of cellular state space may offer a new perspective for understanding the robustness of cell fate decisions.

papers.title: Solving Stochastic Constraints by Oracle-based Gradient Descent and Interval Arithmetic
papers.summary: Stochastic constraints, which incorporate both deterministic parameters and random variables, extend classical deterministic constraints by explicitly accounting for uncertainty. These constraints are increasingly prevalent in data science, artificial intelligence, and bioinformatics; however, solving them requires addressing quantitative satisfaction problems that remain a significant challenge in computer science. In this paper, we propose a novel framework for deciding deterministic parameters that maximize the satisfaction probability. Our approach features a unique synergy between stochastic optimization and symbolic techniques: at the high level, it employs \emph{oracle-based stochastic gradient descent} to identify high-quality parameter candidates, while at the low level, it utilizes \emph{interval arithmetic} to compute rigorously certified lower bounds. This framework produces a sequence of sound and increasingly tight lower bounds for the true maximum satisfaction probability, supported by a high-probability convergence guarantee. We demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of our approach through its application to Stochastic Satisfiability Modulo Theories (SSMT) problems and a stochastic trajectory planning task.

ai.tldr: 提出一种结合随机梯度下降与区间算术的新框架，用于求解随机约束，以最大化满足概率。
ai.motivation: 随机约束在数据科学、人工智能和生物信息学中日益普遍，但其定量满足问题的求解仍是重大挑战。
ai.method: 高层采用基于预言机的随机梯度下降寻找高质量参数候选，底层利用区间算术计算严格认证的下界。
ai.result: 框架生成一系列可靠且逐渐收紧的下界序列，并具有高概率收敛保证，在SSMT问题和随机轨迹规划任务中验证了有效性与效率。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理含不确定性的约束优化问题提供了一种兼具优化效率与计算严谨性的通用求解框架。

idea_spark.idea: 在其他不确定性量化或安全关键优化问题中，引入区间算术为启发式搜索过程提供运行时认证。
idea_spark.risk: 区间算术可能带来计算开销，在实时或高维场景中需权衡严谨性与效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将“搜索-验证”循环范式应用于需要保证解质量或安全边界的其他随机或鲁棒优化问题。

en.tldr: Proposes a novel framework combining stochastic gradient descent and interval arithmetic to solve stochastic constraints by maximizing satisfaction probability.
en.motivation: Stochastic constraints are increasingly prevalent in data science, AI, and bioinformatics, but solving their quantitative satisfaction problems remains a significant challenge.
en.method: Employs oracle-based stochastic gradient descent at a high level to identify parameter candidates, and interval arithmetic at a low level to compute rigorously certified lower bounds.
en.result: The framework produces a sequence of sound and increasingly tight lower bounds with a high-probability convergence guarantee, demonstrated on SSMT problems and a stochastic trajectory planning task.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general solving framework that balances optimization efficiency and computational rigor for constraint optimization under uncertainty.

idea_spark.idea: Introduce interval arithmetic to provide runtime certification for heuristic search processes in other uncertainty quantification or safety-critical optimization problems.
idea_spark.risk: Interval arithmetic may incur computational overhead; trade-offs between rigor and efficiency need consideration in real-time or high-dimensional settings.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires applying the 'search-verify' loop paradigm to other stochastic or robust optimization problems requiring guaranteed solution quality or safety margins.

papers.title: When Spike Sparsity Does Not Translate to Deployed Cost: VS-WNO on Jetson Orin Nano
papers.summary: Spiking neural operators are appealing for neuromorphic edge computing because event-driven substrates can, in principle, translate sparse activity into lower latency and energy. Whether that advantage survives deployment on commodity edge-GPU software stacks, however, remains unclear. We study this question on a Jetson Orin Nano 8 GB using five pretrained variable-spiking wavelet neural operator (VS-WNO) checkpoints and five matched dense wavelet neural operator (WNO) checkpoints on the Darcy rectangular benchmark. On a reference-aligned path, VS-WNO exhibits substantial algorithmic sparsity, with mean spike rates decreasing from 54.26% at the first spiking layer to 18.15% at the fourth. On a deployment-style request path, however, this sparsity does not reduce deployed cost: VS-WNO reaches 59.6 ms latency and 228.0 mJ dynamic energy per inference, whereas dense WNO reaches 53.2 ms and 180.7 mJ, while also achieving slightly lower reference-path error (1.77% versus 1.81%). Nsight Systems indicates that the request path remains launch-dominated and dense rather than sparsity-aware: for VS-WNO, cudaLaunchKernel accounts for 81.6% of CUDA API time within the latency window, and dense convolution kernels account for 53.8% of GPU kernel time; dense WNO shows the same pattern. On this Jetson-class GPU stack, spike sparsity is measurable but does not reduce deployed cost because the runtime does not suppress dense work as spike activity decreases.

ai.tldr: 在Jetson Orin Nano上，脉冲稀疏性并未转化为部署成本优势，VS-WNO的延迟和能耗反而高于密集WNO。
ai.motivation: 脉冲神经算子理论上可通过事件驱动机制将稀疏活动转化为低延迟和低能耗，但尚不清楚其在商用边缘GPU软件栈上的实际部署优势。
ai.method: 在Jetson Orin Nano 8 GB上，使用五个预训练的变脉冲小波神经算子检查点和五个匹配的密集小波神经算子检查点，在Darcy矩形基准上进行测试，分析参考对齐路径和部署式请求路径的性能。
ai.result: VS-WNO在算法上表现出显著稀疏性，但部署成本未降低：其延迟为59.6毫秒、动态能耗为228.0毫焦，高于密集WNO的53.2毫秒和180.7毫焦，且误差略高。性能分析显示运行时未利用稀疏性优化。
ai.help_to_user: 揭示了在现有边缘GPU软件栈上，脉冲稀疏性未必带来实际部署收益，为硬件-算法协同设计提供实证参考。

idea_spark.idea: 开发稀疏感知的GPU运行时或专用内核，将算法稀疏性转化为实际计算节省。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖现有通用GPU软件栈可能无法发挥新型神经网络架构的潜在优势。
idea_spark.inspiration: 推动算法设计与底层硬件/运行时特性的更紧密协同优化。

en.tldr: On Jetson Orin Nano, spike sparsity does not translate to deployment cost advantage; VS-WNO shows higher latency and energy than dense WNO.
en.motivation: Spiking neural operators could theoretically translate sparse activity into lower latency and energy via event-driven mechanisms, but their actual deployment advantage on commodity edge-GPU software stacks remains unclear.
en.method: Evaluated five pretrained variable-spiking wavelet neural operator checkpoints and five matched dense wavelet neural operator checkpoints on Jetson Orin Nano 8 GB using the Darcy rectangular benchmark, analyzing both reference-aligned and deployment-style request paths.
en.result: VS-WNO exhibits substantial algorithmic sparsity but does not reduce deployed cost: 59.6 ms latency and 228.0 mJ dynamic energy per inference, worse than dense WNO's 53.2 ms and 180.7 mJ, with slightly higher error. Profiling shows the runtime remains launch-dominated and dense, not sparsity-aware.
en.help_to_user: Demonstrates that spike sparsity may not yield practical deployment benefits on existing edge-GPU software stacks, providing empirical insights for hardware-algorithm co-design.

idea_spark.idea: Develop sparsity-aware GPU runtimes or specialized kernels to translate algorithmic sparsity into actual computational savings.
idea_spark.risk: Relying on existing general-purpose GPU software stacks may fail to harness the potential advantages of novel neural network architectures.
idea_spark.inspiration: Promotes tighter co-design between algorithm development and underlying hardware/runtime characteristics.

papers.title: Amortized Inverse Kinematics via Graph Attention for Real-Time Human Avatar Animation
papers.summary: Inverse kinematics (IK) is a core operation in animation, robotics, and biomechanics: given Cartesian constraints, recover joint rotations under a known kinematic tree. In many real-time human avatar pipelines, the available signal per frame is a sparse set of tracked 3D joint positions, whereas animation systems require joint orientations to drive skinning. Recovering full orientations from positions is underconstrained, most notably because twist about bone axes is ambiguous, and classical IK solvers typically rely on iterative optimization that can be slow and sensitive to noisy inputs. We introduce IK-GAT, a lightweight graph-attention network that reconstructs full-body joint orientations from 3D joint positions in a single forward pass. The model performs message passing over the skeletal parent-child graph to exploit kinematic structure during rotation inference. To simplify learning, IK-GAT predicts rotations in a bone-aligned world-frame representation anchored to rest-pose bone frames. This parameterization makes the twist axis explicit and is exactly invertible to standard parent-relative local rotations given the kinematic tree and rest pose. The network uses a continuous 6D rotation representation and is trained with a geodesic loss on SO(3) together with an optional forward-kinematics consistency regularizer. IK-GAT produces animation-ready local rotations that can directly drive a rigged avatar or be converted to pose parameters of SMPL-like body models for real-time and online applications. With 374K parameters and over 650 FPS on CPU, IK-GAT outperforms VPoser-based per-frame iterative optimization without warm-start at significantly lower cost, and is robust to initial pose and input noise

ai.tldr: IK-GAT：一种基于图注意力网络的轻量级模型，通过单次前向传播从3D关节位置重建全身关节方向，用于实时人体虚拟形象动画。
ai.motivation: 实时人体虚拟形象动画中，每帧可用的信号通常是稀疏的3D关节位置跟踪数据，而动画系统需要关节方向来驱动蒙皮。从位置恢复完整方向是一个欠约束问题，且经典IK求解器依赖迭代优化，速度慢且对噪声敏感。
ai.method: 模型在骨骼父子关系图上进行消息传递以利用运动学结构。为简化学习，IK-GAT在骨骼对齐的世界坐标系表示中预测旋转，该表示锚定于静止姿态的骨骼坐标系。网络使用连续的6D旋转表示，并通过SO(3)上的测地线损失和可选的正向运动学一致性正则化器进行训练。
ai.result: IK-GAT仅需37.4万参数，在CPU上运行速度超过650 FPS，无需预热启动即可超越基于VPoser的逐帧迭代优化，且对初始姿态和输入噪声具有鲁棒性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种高效、鲁棒的从稀疏3D关节位置到动画就绪的局部旋转的转换方法，可直接驱动绑定虚拟形象或转换为SMPL类身体模型的姿态参数。

idea_spark.idea: 将欠约束的IK问题转化为基于图结构学习的回归任务，通过显式参数化旋转轴来消除歧义。
idea_spark.risk: 模型性能依赖于训练数据的质量和多样性；可能难以泛化到训练分布外的极端姿态或骨骼结构。
idea_spark.inspiration: 该方法可启发其他结构化预测问题（如机器人运动规划、蛋白质结构预测）中，利用领域知识设计网络架构和损失函数。

en.tldr: IK-GAT: a lightweight graph-attention network that reconstructs full-body joint orientations from 3D joint positions in a single forward pass for real-time human avatar animation.
en.motivation: In real-time human avatar pipelines, the available signal per frame is a sparse set of tracked 3D joint positions, whereas animation systems require joint orientations to drive skinning. Recovering full orientations from positions is underconstrained, and classical IK solvers rely on iterative optimization that is slow and sensitive to noise.
en.method: The model performs message passing over the skeletal parent-child graph to exploit kinematic structure. en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and robust method to convert sparse 3D joint positions into animation-ready local rotations that can directly drive a rigged avatar or be converted to pose parameters of SMPL-like body models.

idea_spark.idea: Transform the underconstrained IK problem into a graph-structured learning regression task by explicitly parameterizing the rotation axis to resolve ambiguity.
idea_spark.risk: Model performance depends on the quality and diversity of training data; may struggle to generalize to out-of-distribution extreme poses or skeletal structures.
idea_spark.inspiration: This approach can inspire other structured prediction problems (e.g., robotic motion planning, protein structure prediction) to incorporate domain knowledge into network architecture and loss design.

papers.title: Towards a Foundation-Model Paradigm for Aerodynamic Prediction in Three-dimensional Design
papers.summary: Accurate machine-learning models for aerodynamic prediction are essential for accelerating shape optimization, yet remain challenging to develop for complex three-dimensional configurations due to the high cost of generating training data. This work introduces a methodology for efficiently constructing accurate surrogate models for design purposes by first pre-training a large-scale model on diverse geometries and then fine-tuning it with a few more detailed task-specific samples. A Transformer-based architecture, AeroTransformer, is developed and tailored for large-scale training to learn aerodynamics. The methodology is evaluated on transonic wings, where the model is pre-trained on SuperWing, a dataset of nearly 30000 samples with broad geometric diversity, and subsequently fine-tuned to handle specific wing shapes perturbed from the Common Research Model. Results show that, with 450 task-specific samples, the proposed methodology achieves 0.36% error on surface-flow prediction, reducing 84.2% compared to training from scratch. The influence of model configurations and training strategies is also systematically studied to provide guidance on effectively training and deploying such models under limited data and computational budgets. To facilitate reuse, we release the datasets and the pre-trained models at https://github.com/tum-pbs/AeroTransformer. An interactive design tool is also built on the pre-trained model and is available online at https://webwing.pbs.cit.tum.de.

ai.tldr: 本研究提出了一种基于预训练-微调范式的三维气动预测方法AeroTransformer，在广泛几何数据集上预训练后，仅需少量任务样本微调即可实现高精度表面流预测，显著降低数据需求。
ai.motivation: 复杂三维构型的气动预测机器学习模型开发成本高，训练数据生成昂贵，限制了形状优化加速。
ai.method: 开发了基于Transformer的AeroTransformer架构，先在包含近30000个样本的SuperWing多样化几何数据集上进行大规模预训练，然后使用少量特定任务样本（如450个CRM翼型扰动样本）进行微调。
ai.result: 仅用450个任务样本微调后，表面流预测误差达到0.36%，相比从头训练减少84.2%。系统研究了模型配置和训练策略的影响。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了预训练模型、数据集和在线交互设计工具，支持在有限数据和计算预算下高效训练和部署气动预测模型。

idea_spark.idea: 将基础模型思想引入计算流体力学，通过大规模多样化几何预训练获得通用表示，再针对特定设计任务轻量微调。
idea_spark.risk: 预训练数据集的几何多样性可能无法覆盖所有新型设计，微调样本不足时可能泛化不佳。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将Transformer架构与物理先验结合，开发领域专用基础模型，降低高保真仿真依赖。

en.tldr: This work introduces AeroTransformer, a pre-training-fine-tuning paradigm for 3D aerodynamic prediction. After pre-training on diverse geometries, fine-tuning with few task-specific samples achieves high-precision surface flow prediction, significantly reducing data requirements.
en.motivation: Developing accurate ML models for aerodynamic prediction of complex 3D configurations is challenging due to the high cost of generating training data, hindering shape optimization acceleration.
en.method: Developed AeroTransformer, a Transformer-based architecture, pre-trained on SuperWing—a dataset of nearly 30,000 samples with broad geometric diversity—then fine-tuned with few task-specific samples (e.g., 450 CRM wing perturbations).
en.result: With only 450 fine-tuning samples, surface flow prediction error reaches 0.36%, reducing error by 84.2% compared to training from scratch. Model configurations and training strategies are systematically studied.
en.help_to_user: Provides pre-trained models, datasets, and an online interactive design tool to support efficient training and deployment of aerodynamic prediction models under limited data and computational budgets.

idea_spark.idea: Introducing foundation-model thinking to computational fluid dynamics: pre-training on large-scale diverse geometries for general representations, then lightweight fine-tuning for specific design tasks.
idea_spark.risk: Geometric diversity in pre-training data may not cover all novel designs; generalization may suffer with insufficient fine-tuning samples.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining Transformer architectures with physical priors to develop domain-specific foundation models, reducing reliance on high-fidelity simulations.

papers.title: Graph Transformer-Based Pathway Embedding for Cancer Prognosis
papers.summary: Accurate prediction of cancer progression remains a challenge due to the high heterogeneity of molecular omics data across patients. While biologically informed models have improved the interpretability of these predictions, a persistent limitation lies in how they encode individual genes to construct pathway representations. Existing hierarchical models typically derive gene features by directly mapping raw molecular inputs, whereas integration frameworks often rely on simple statistical aggregations of patient-level signals. These approaches often fail to explicitly learn a shared base representation for each gene, thereby limiting the expressiveness and biological accuracy of downstream pathway embeddings. To address this, we introduce PATH, a modulation-based, patient-conditioned gene embedding strategy. PATH represents a paradigm shift by starting from a shared base embedding for each gene, preserving a stable biological identity across the population, and then dynamically adapting it using patient-specific copy number variation (CNV) and mutation signals. This allows the model to capture subtle individual molecular variations while maintaining a consistent latent understanding of the gene itself. We integrate PATH into a graph transformer framework that models interactions among biologically connected pathways through pathway-guided attention. Across pancancer metastasis prediction, PATH achieves an F1 score of 0.8766, representing an 8.8 percent improvement over the current SOTA multi-omics benchmarks. Beyond superior predictive accuracy, our approach identifies biologically meaningful pathways and, crucially, reveals disease-state-specific pathway rewiring, offering new insights into the evolving pathway-pathway interactions that drive cancer progression.

ai.tldr: 提出PATH方法，通过共享基因基础嵌入与患者特异性信号调制，结合图Transformer建模通路交互，显著提升癌症预后预测性能。
ai.motivation: 现有方法在构建通路表示时，未能显式学习基因的共享基础表示，限制了嵌入的表达能力和生物学准确性。
ai.method: 采用调制策略：先为每个基因建立跨人群共享的基础嵌入，再用患者特异的CNV和突变信号动态调整。将此嵌入集成到图Transformer中，通过通路引导的注意力建模通路间交互。
ai.result: 在泛癌转移预测中达到F1分数0.8766，比当前SOTA多组学基准提升8.8%。方法能识别有生物学意义的通路，并揭示疾病状态特异的通路重连。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种更准确、可解释的癌症预后预测框架，特别适用于处理高度异质性的多组学数据。

idea_spark.idea: 在分子网络中，为节点（如基因）建立共享的、跨群体的基础身份表示，再根据个体上下文（如突变）进行条件化调整。
idea_spark.risk: 基础嵌入的稳定性与调制信号的敏感性需要精细平衡，过度调制可能淹没基础生物学身份。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将“稳定核心”与“动态上下文”解耦的表示学习范式，可能适用于其他具有实体和状态分离特性的复杂系统。

en.tldr: Proposes PATH, a modulation-based gene embedding method integrated with a graph transformer, which significantly improves cancer prognosis prediction by modeling pathway interactions.
en.motivation: Existing methods fail to explicitly learn a shared base representation for genes when constructing pathway representations, limiting embedding expressiveness and biological accuracy.
en.method: Employs a modulation strategy: establishes a shared base embedding for each gene across the population, then dynamically adapts it using patient-specific CNV and mutation signals. Integrates this into a graph transformer framework with pathway-guided attention to model interactions.
en.result: Achieves an F1 score of 0.8766 in pancancer metastasis prediction, an 8.8% improvement over current SOTA multi-omics benchmarks. Identifies biologically meaningful pathways and reveals disease-state-specific pathway rewiring.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more accurate and interpretable framework for cancer prognosis prediction, particularly suited for handling highly heterogeneous multi-omics data.

idea_spark.idea: In molecular networks, establish a shared, cross-population base identity representation for nodes (e.g., genes), then conditionally adjust it based on individual context (e.g., mutations).
idea_spark.risk: Requires careful balancing of the stability of the base embedding and the sensitivity of the modulation signal; over-modulation may obscure the core biological identity.
idea_spark.inspiration: The representation learning paradigm of decoupling a 'stable core' from 'dynamic context' may be applicable to other complex systems with separated entity and state characteristics.

papers.title: LayerCache: Exploiting Layer-wise Velocity Heterogeneity for Efficient Flow Matching Inference
papers.summary: Flow Matching models achieve state-of-the-art image generation quality but incur substantial inference cost due to iterative denoising through large Transformer networks. We observe that different layer groups within a Transformer exhibit markedly heterogeneous velocity dynamics: shallow layers are highly stable and amenable to aggressive caching, while deep layers undergo large velocity changes that demand full computation. Existing caching methods, however, treat the entire Transformer as a monolithic unit, applying a single caching decision per timestep and thus failing to exploit this heterogeneity. Based on this finding, we propose LayerCache, a layer-aware caching framework that partitions the Transformer into layer groups and makes independent, per-group caching decisions at each denoising step. LayerCache introduces an adaptive JVP span K selection mechanism that leverages per-group stability measurements to balance estimation accuracy and computational savings. We formulate a three-dimensional scheduling problem over timesteps, layer groups, and JVP span, and solve it with a greedy budget allocation algorithm. On Qwen-Image (1024x1024, 50 steps), LayerCache achieves PSNR 37.46 dB (+5.38 dB over MeanCache), SSIM 0.9834, and LPIPS 0.0178 (a 70% reduction over MeanCache) at 1.37x speedup, dominating all prior caching methods on the quality-speed Pareto frontier.

ai.tldr: LayerCache 通过利用 Transformer 不同层组的速度异质性，实现流匹配模型的高效推理加速。
ai.motivation: 流匹配模型推理成本高，现有缓存方法将整个 Transformer 视为单一单元，无法利用不同层组的速度动态差异。
ai.method: 提出层感知缓存框架，将 Transformer 划分为层组，在每个去噪步骤中为每个层组独立做出缓存决策，并引入自适应 JVP 跨度选择机制和三维调度问题的贪心预算分配算法。
ai.result: 在 Qwen-Image (1024x1024, 50 步) 上，LayerCache 在 1.37 倍加速下实现了 PSNR 37.46 dB、SSIM 0.9834 和 LPIPS 0.0178，在质量-速度帕累托前沿上优于所有现有缓存方法。
ai.help_to_user: 为加速大型生成式 AI 模型（尤其是基于 Transformer 的图像生成模型）的推理提供了新的层粒度优化思路。

idea_spark.idea: 将模型内部结构（如层）的动态行为差异作为优化维度，而非将其视为同质整体。
idea_spark.risk: 层组划分和稳定性测量的开销可能抵消部分加速收益，需在具体硬件和模型上验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从模型内部微观动态而非宏观架构角度寻找优化机会，例如分析不同注意力头或 FFN 层的计算冗余。

en.tldr: LayerCache exploits layer-wise velocity heterogeneity in Transformers for efficient flow matching inference acceleration.
en.motivation: Flow matching models incur high inference costs, and existing caching methods treat the entire Transformer monolithically, failing to exploit heterogeneous velocity dynamics across layer groups.
en.method: Proposes a layer-aware caching framework that partitions the Transformer into layer groups, makes independent per-group caching decisions at each denoising step, introduces an adaptive JVP span selection mechanism, and solves a three-dimensional scheduling problem with a greedy budget allocation algorithm.
en.result: On Qwen-Image (1024x1024, 50 steps), LayerCache achieves PSNR 37.46 dB, SSIM 0.9834, and LPIPS 0.0178 at 1.37x speedup, dominating prior caching methods on the quality-speed Pareto frontier.
en.help_to_user: Provides a novel layer-granularity optimization approach for accelerating the inference of large generative AI models, particularly Transformer-based image generation models.

idea_spark.idea: Treat the dynamic behavioral differences within a model's internal structure (e.g., layers) as an optimization dimension, rather than viewing it as a homogeneous whole.
idea_spark.risk: The overhead of layer-group partitioning and stability measurement may offset some acceleration gains, requiring validation on specific hardware and models.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires searching for optimization opportunities from the microscopic dynamics within a model rather than its macroscopic architecture, such as analyzing computational redundancy in different attention heads or FFN layers.

papers.title: Flow Shop Scheduling with Stochastic Reentry
papers.summary: We study flow shop scheduling with stochastic reentry, where jobs must complete multiple passes through the entire shop, and the number of passes that a job requires for completion is drawn from a discrete probability distribution. The goal is to find policies that minimize performance measures in expectation. Our main contribution is a reduction to a classical parallel machine scheduling problem augmented with machine arrivals. This reduction preserves expected objective values and enables transferring structural results and performance guarantees from the auxiliary problems to the reentrant flow shop setting. We demonstrate the usefulness of this reduction by proving the optimality of simple priority policies for minimizing the makespan and the total completion time in expectation under geometric and, more generally, monotone hazard rate distributions. For minimizing the total weighted completion time, we derive an approximation guarantee that depends only on the squared coefficient of variation of the underlying distributions for a simple priority policy. Our results constitute the first optimality and approximation guarantees for flow shops with stochastic reentry and demonstrate that established scheduling policies naturally extend to this setting through the proposed reduction.

ai.tldr: 研究具有随机重入的流水车间调度问题，其中作业需多次通过整个车间，所需次数服从离散概率分布。通过将问题归约为带机器到达的经典并行机调度问题，证明了简单优先级策略在几何分布和单调失效率分布下对期望完工时间和总完工时间的最优性，并为总加权完工时间提供了近似保证。
ai.motivation: 流水车间调度中，作业可能需要随机次数的重入才能完成，这在实际生产（如返工、多阶段质检）中常见。现有研究缺乏对此类随机重入问题的理论保证。
ai.method: 将随机重入流水车间调度问题归约为带机器到达的经典并行机调度问题，该归约保持期望目标值不变，从而可将辅助问题的结构结果和性能保证迁移到原问题。
ai.result: 在几何分布和单调失效率分布下，证明了简单优先级策略对最小化期望完工时间和总完工时间的最优性；对最小化总加权完工时间，基于分布平方变异系数给出了简单优先级策略的近似保证。
ai.help_to_user: 为具有随机重入的流水车间调度提供了首个最优性和近似保证，表明经典调度策略可通过所提归约自然扩展到此场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将随机重入建模为机器到达过程，从而连接流水车间与并行机调度理论。
idea_spark.risk: 归约依赖于特定的概率分布假设（如几何分布、单调失效率），可能不适用于任意分布。
idea_spark.inspiration: 该归约方法可尝试应用于其他具有随机循环或重复过程的调度场景（如维修、迭代开发）。

en.tldr: Studies flow shop scheduling with stochastic reentry, where jobs require a random number of passes through the shop. By reducing the problem to a classical parallel machine scheduling problem with machine arrivals, we prove optimality of simple priority policies for minimizing expected makespan and total completion time under geometric and monotone hazard rate distributions, and provide approximation guarantees for total weighted completion time.
en.motivation: In flow shop scheduling, jobs may require stochastic reentry (e.g., for rework or multi-stage inspection). Theoretical guarantees for such stochastic reentry problems are lacking in existing literature.
en.method: Reduces the stochastic reentrant flow shop problem to a classical parallel machine scheduling problem augmented with machine arrivals. This reduction preserves expected objective values, enabling transfer of structural results and performance guarantees from the auxiliary problem.
en.result: Proves optimality of simple priority policies for minimizing expected makespan and total completion time under geometric and monotone hazard rate distributions. For minimizing total weighted completion time, derives an approximation guarantee for a simple priority policy based on the squared coefficient of variation of the underlying distributions.
en.help_to_user: Provides the first optimality and approximation guarantees for flow shops with stochastic reentry, demonstrating that established scheduling policies naturally extend to this setting through the proposed reduction.

idea_spark.idea: Model stochastic reentry as a machine arrival process to bridge flow shop and parallel machine scheduling theory.
idea_spark.risk: Reduction relies on specific distributional assumptions (e.g., geometric, monotone hazard rate) and may not hold for arbitrary distributions.
idea_spark.inspiration: The reduction approach could be attempted for other scheduling scenarios with stochastic loops or repetitive processes (e.g., maintenance, iterative development).

papers.title: SAVE: A Generalizable Framework for Multi-Condition Single-Cell Generation with Gene Block Attention
papers.summary: Modeling single-cell gene expression across diverse biological and technical conditions is crucial for characterizing cellular states and simulating unseen scenarios. Existing methods often treat genes as independent tokens, overlooking their high-level biological relationships and leading to poor performance. We introduce SAVE, a unified generative framework based on conditional Transformers for multi-condition single-cell modeling. SAVE leverages a coarse-grained representation by grouping semantically related genes into blocks, capturing higher-order dependencies among gene modules. A Flow Matching mechanism and condition-masking strategy further enhance flexible simulation and enable generalization to unseen condition combinations. We evaluate SAVE on a range of benchmarks, including conditional generation, batch effect correction, and perturbation prediction. SAVE consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in generation fidelity and extrapolative generalization, especially in low-resource or combinatorially held-out settings. Overall, SAVE offers a scalable and generalizable solution for modeling complex single-cell data, with broad utility in virtual cell synthesis and biological interpretation. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/fdu-wangfeilab/sc-save

ai.tldr: SAVE是一个基于条件Transformer的统一生成框架，通过将语义相关的基因分组为块来捕获基因模块间的高阶依赖关系，用于多条件单细胞建模。
ai.motivation: 现有方法通常将基因视为独立标记，忽略了它们的高层生物学关系，导致性能不佳。需要一种能够建模跨多样生物和技术条件的单细胞基因表达的方法。
ai.method: 采用条件Transformer框架，利用粗粒度表示将语义相关基因分组为块，并引入Flow Matching机制和条件掩码策略以增强灵活模拟和泛化能力。
ai.result: 在条件生成、批次效应校正和扰动预测等基准测试中，SAVE在生成保真度和外推泛化方面始终优于最先进方法，尤其在低资源或组合保留设置中。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一个可扩展且可泛化的解决方案，用于建模复杂的单细胞数据，在虚拟细胞合成和生物学解释中具有广泛实用性。

idea_spark.idea: 将细粒度元素（基因）分组为语义块，作为Transformer的输入单元，以捕获高阶依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 基因分块的生物学合理性直接影响模型捕获的关系质量；在数据稀缺条件下，块结构的定义可能具有挑战性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将领域知识（如基因通路）融入表示学习，通过粗粒度化提升模型对模块化结构的建模能力。

en.motivation: Existing methods often treat genes as independent tokens, overlooking their high-level biological relationships and leading to poor performance. A method is needed to model single-cell gene expression across diverse biological and technical conditions.
en.method: Employs a conditional Transformer framework leveraging a coarse-grained representation by grouping semantically related genes into blocks, with a Flow Matching mechanism and condition-masking strategy to enhance flexible simulation and generalization.
en.result: SAVE consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods in generation fidelity and extrapolative generalization across benchmarks including conditional generation, batch effect correction, and perturbation prediction, especially in low-resource or combinatorially held-out settings.

idea_spark.idea: Group fine-grained elements (genes) into semantic blocks as input units for Transformers to capture higher-order dependencies.
idea_spark.risk: The biological plausibility of gene grouping directly affects the quality of relationships captured; defining block structures can be challenging under data scarcity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires incorporating domain knowledge (e.g., gene pathways) into representation learning to enhance modeling of modular structures through coarse-graining.

papers.title: Sessa: Selective State Space Attention
papers.summary: Modern sequence models are dominated by Transformers, where self-attention mixes information from the visible context in an input-dependent way. However, when retrieval is not sharp and attention remains diffuse over an effective support $S_{\mathrm{eff}}(t)$, the influence of any individual token is diluted, typically scaling as $O(1/S_{\mathrm{eff}}(t))$ and reaching $O(1/\ell)$ for old tokens in full-prefix settings. Structured state-space models process sequences recurrently through an explicit feedback path; selective variants such as Mamba make this feedback input-dependent, yet when freeze time cannot be sustained over long intervals, their long-range sensitivity decays exponentially with lag. Existing architectures therefore either retrieve from the past in a single read or propagate information through a single feedback chain. We introduce Sessa, a decoder that places attention inside a feedback path, enabling recurrent many-path aggregation within a layer. Under stated assumptions, Sessa admits regimes with a power-law memory tail in lag $\ell$ of order $O(\ell^{-β})$ for $0<β<1$, which is asymptotically slower than $1/\ell$; moreover, this rate is tight in an explicit diffuse uniform-routing setting where the influence is $Θ(\ell^{-β})$. Under the same conditions, only Sessa among the compared model classes realizes flexible selective retrieval, including non-decaying profiles. Empirically, under matched architectures and training budgets, Sessa achieves the strongest performance on our long-context benchmarks while remaining competitive with Transformer and Mamba style baselines on short-context language modeling.

ai.tldr: Sessa是一种将注意力机制置于反馈路径内的解码器架构，实现了层内的循环多路径聚合，在长上下文任务中表现出色。
ai.motivation: 现有序列模型（如Transformer和Mamba）在长程依赖处理上存在局限：注意力机制在上下文扩散时影响力衰减为O(1/ℓ)，而选择性状态空间模型的长程敏感性随滞后呈指数衰减。
ai.method: 提出Sessa解码器，将注意力机制嵌入反馈路径，支持层内的循环多路径信息聚合。在特定假设下，该架构可实现滞后ℓ的幂律记忆尾O(ℓ^{-β})（0<β<1）。
ai.result: 在匹配架构和训练预算下，Sessa在长上下文基准测试中取得最强性能，同时在短上下文语言建模上与Transformer和Mamba基线保持竞争力。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理长序列依赖问题提供了新架构思路，通过结合注意力与状态空间模型的优势，实现更灵活的选择性检索和非衰减记忆剖面。

idea_spark.risk: 理论假设在实际任务中的普适性需进一步验证
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发混合架构探索，如将注意力机制与其他循环组件结合以优化长程信息流
