zh.motivation: Bartholdi和Eisenstein的开创性工作表明，当工人从最慢到最快排序时，桶队系统稳定并收敛到固定点，但违反该排序时动力学变得复杂。后续研究仅描述了三个工人在稳定状态下的行为。
zh.method: 扩展对三个工人的分析至非稳定状态，特别关注工人从最快到最慢排序的情况。同时，针对任意数量工人，在无站点设置下证明固定点的唯一性。
zh.result: 1. 完整描述了三个工人从最快到最慢排序时的动力学行为；2. 发现先前研究中因稳定性定义限制而需修正的结论；3. 证明了无站点设置下固定点对任意数量工人总是唯一的。
zh.help_to_user: 为生产系统设计者提供了更完整的桶队动力学理解，特别是在非标准工人排序下的行为预测，以及固定点唯一性的理论保证。

idea_spark.idea: 将桶队模型中的工人排序条件松弛，研究混合能力团队在动态任务分配中的涌现行为。
idea_spark.risk: 实际生产线存在离散工作站、随机处理时间等复杂因素，可能影响理论结论的直接应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对多智能体系统中基于简单局部规则产生的全局稳定模式的研究，如机器人协作或分布式计算任务分配。

en.tldr: This work extends the analysis of bucket brigade production lines, proving uniqueness of the fixed point in the no-station setting and providing a complete description of dynamics for three workers ordered from fastest to slowest.
en.motivation: The foundational work by Bartholdi and Eisenstein showed bucket brigades stabilize to a fixed point when workers are ordered slowest-to-fastest, but dynamics become complex when this ordering is violated. Subsequent work only characterized stable behaviors for up to three workers.
en.method: Extend the analysis of three workers beyond the stable regime, particularly when ordered fastest-to-slowest. Additionally, prove fixed point uniqueness for arbitrary numbers of workers in the no-station setting.
en.result: 1. Complete description of dynamics for three workers ordered fastest-to-slowest; 2. Identification of conclusions needing revision due to restrictive stability notions in prior work; 3. Proof that the fixed point is always unique in the no-station setting for any number of workers.
en.help_to_user: Provides production system designers with a more complete understanding of bucket brigade dynamics, particularly for non-standard worker orderings, and theoretical guarantees on fixed point uniqueness.

idea_spark.idea: Relax the worker ordering condition in bucket brigade models to study emergent behaviors in mixed-capability teams with dynamic task allocation.
idea_spark.risk: Real production lines involve complexities like discrete stations and stochastic processing times that may limit direct application of theoretical results.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires research on global stable patterns emerging from simple local rules in multi-agent systems, such as robotic collaboration or distributed computing task allocation.

papers.title: FlowC2S: Flowing from Current to Succeeding Frames for Fast and Memory-Efficient Video Continuation
papers.summary: This paper introduces a novel methodology for generating fast and memory-efficient video continuations. Our method, dubbed FlowC2S, fine-tunes a pre-trained text-to-video flow model to learn a vector field between the current and succeeding video chunks. Two design choices are key. First, we introduce inherent optimal couplings, utilizing temporally adjacent video chunks during training as a practical proxy for true optimal couplings, resulting in straighter flows. Second, we incorporate target inversion, injecting the inverted latent of the target chunk into the input representation to strengthen correspondences and improve visual fidelity. By flowing directly from current to succeeding frames, instead of the common combination of current frames with noise to generate a video continuation, we reduce the dimensionality of the model input by a factor of two. The proposed method, fine-tuned from LTXV and Wan, surpasses the state-of-the-art scores across quantitative evaluations with FID and FVD, with as few as five neural function evaluations.

ai.tldr: FlowC2S是一种快速、内存高效的视频延续方法，通过微调预训练的文本到视频流模型，学习当前与后续视频块之间的向量场，直接生成后续帧。
ai.motivation: 传统视频延续方法通常将当前帧与噪声结合生成视频，计算和内存开销大。需要一种能直接、高效地从当前帧流向后续帧的方法。
ai.method: 1. 引入固有最优耦合，使用时间相邻的视频块作为真实最优耦合的代理，获得更直的流。2. 采用目标反转，将目标块的反转潜在表示注入输入，增强对应关系。3. 直接学习从当前帧到后续帧的向量场，将模型输入维度减半。
ai.result: 基于LTXV和Wan模型微调，在FID和FVD定量评估中超越最先进方法，仅需五次神经函数评估。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要实时或资源受限的视频生成、补全、预测任务提供了高效解决方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将视频延续重构为帧间向量场学习问题，而非从噪声生成。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于预训练流模型的质量；对非相邻帧的长时延续效果待验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将高维生成问题降维为条件流变换，可应用于音频、3D序列等时序数据生成。

zh.result: 基于LTXV和Wan模型微调，在FID和FVD定量评估中超越最先进方法，仅需五次神经函数评估。
zh.help_to_user: 为需要实时或资源受限的视频生成、补全、预测任务提供了高效解决方案。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖于预训练流模型的质量；对非相邻帧的长时延续效果待验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将高维生成问题降维为条件流变换，可应用于音频、3D序列等时序数据生成。

en.tldr: FlowC2S is a fast, memory-efficient video continuation method that fine-tunes a pre-trained text-to-video flow model to learn a vector field between current and succeeding video chunks for direct frame generation.
en.motivation: Conventional video continuation methods often combine current frames with noise, incurring high computational and memory costs. A method is needed to flow directly and efficiently from current to succeeding frames.
en.method: 1. Introduce inherent optimal couplings using temporally adjacent video chunks as a proxy for true optimal couplings, yielding straighter flows. 2. Incorporate target inversion by injecting the inverted latent of the target chunk into the input to strengthen correspondences. 3. Learn a vector field directly from current to succeeding frames, halving the model input dimensionality.
en.result: Fine-tuned from LTXV and Wan, it surpasses state-of-the-art scores in quantitative evaluations (FID, FVD) with as few as five neural function evaluations.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient solution for real-time or resource-constrained video generation, completion, and prediction tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Reformulate video continuation as an inter-frame vector field learning problem rather than generation from noise.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on the quality of the pre-trained flow model; long-term continuation for non-adjacent frames needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires dimensionality reduction of high-dimensional generation problems into conditional flow transformations, applicable to audio, 3D sequences, and other temporal data generation.

papers.title: Symbolic Synthesis for LTLf+ Obligations
papers.summary: We study synthesis for obligation properties expressed in LTLfp, the extension of LTLf to infinite traces. Obligation properties are positive Boolean combinations of safety and guarantee (co-safety) properties and form the second level of the temporal hierarchy of Manna and Pnueli. Although obligation properties are expressed over infinite traces, they retain most of the simplicity of LTLf. In particular, we show that they admit a translation into symbolically represented deterministic weak automata (DWA) obtained directly from the symbolic deterministic finite automata (DFA) for the underlying LTLf properties on trace prefixes. DWA inherit many of the attractive algorithmic features of DFA, including Boolean closure and polynomial-time minimization. Moreover, we show that synthesis for LTLfp obligation properties is theoretically highly efficient - solvable in linear time once the DWA is constructed. We investigate several symbolic algorithms for solving DWA games that arise in the synthesis of obligation properties and evaluate their effectiveness experimentally. Overall, the results indicate that synthesis for LTLfp obligation properties can be performed with virtually the same effectiveness as LTLf synthesis.

ai.tldr: 研究LTLf+义务属性的符号化综合，通过将义务属性转换为符号表示的确定性弱自动机（DWA），实现高效综合。
ai.motivation: 义务属性是安全性与保证性的布尔组合，属于Manna-Pnueli时间层次第二级，虽定义在无限迹上，但保留了LTLf的简洁性，需探索其高效综合方法。
ai.method: 将LTLf+义务属性直接翻译为符号表示的DWA，该DWA继承自底层LTLf属性的符号确定性有限自动机（DFA），并研究解决DWA游戏的多种符号算法。
ai.result: LTLf+义务属性的综合在理论上是高效的（DWA构建后线性时间可解），实验表明其综合效果与LTLf综合几乎相同。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理时间逻辑中的义务属性提供了高效的符号化综合框架，适用于需要形式化验证与自动合成的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 利用DWA的布尔闭包与多项式时间最小化特性，结合符号算法求解自动机游戏，实现高效综合。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于从LTLf DFA到DWA的翻译，若属性超出义务范畴（如更复杂的时间层次），可能不适用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将复杂时间属性分解为安全/保证组件，并通过符号自动机组合与游戏求解来提升综合效率。

zh.tldr: 研究LTLf+义务属性的符号化综合，通过将义务属性转换为符号表示的确定性弱自动机（DWA），实现高效综合。
zh.motivation: 义务属性是安全性与保证性的布尔组合，属于Manna-Pnueli时间层次第二级，虽定义在无限迹上，但保留了LTLf的简洁性，需探索其高效综合方法。

zh.result: LTLf+义务属性的综合在理论上是高效的（DWA构建后线性时间可解），实验表明其综合效果与LTLf综合几乎相同。

idea_spark.idea: 利用DWA的布尔闭包与多项式时间最小化特性，结合符号算法求解自动机游戏，实现高效综合。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于从LTLf DFA到DWA的翻译，若属性超出义务范畴（如更复杂的时间层次），可能不适用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将复杂时间属性分解为安全/保证组件，并通过符号自动机组合与游戏求解来提升综合效率。

en.tldr: Studies symbolic synthesis for LTLf+ obligation properties by translating them into symbolically represented deterministic weak automata (DWA) for efficient synthesis.
en.motivation: Obligation properties are Boolean combinations of safety and guarantee properties, forming the second level of the Manna-Pnueli temporal hierarchy. Although defined over infinite traces, they retain much of LTLf's simplicity, necessitating exploration of efficient synthesis methods.
en.method: Translates LTLf+ obligation properties directly into symbolically represented DWA, derived from symbolic deterministic finite automata (DFA) for underlying LTLf properties, and investigates several symbolic algorithms for solving DWA games.
en.result: Synthesis for LTLf+ obligation properties is theoretically highly efficient (solvable in linear time after DWA construction), with experiments showing effectiveness nearly identical to LTLf synthesis.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient symbolic synthesis framework for obligation properties in temporal logic, applicable to scenarios requiring formal verification and automatic synthesis.

idea_spark.idea: Utilize DWA's Boolean closure and polynomial-time minimization features, combined with symbolic algorithms to solve automata games for efficient synthesis.
idea_spark.risk: The method relies on translation from LTLf DFA to DWA; properties beyond obligations (e.g., higher temporal hierarchy levels) may not be applicable.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires decomposing complex temporal properties into safety/guarantee components and enhancing synthesis efficiency via symbolic automata composition and game solving.

papers.title: Disentangled Robot Learning via Separate Forward and Inverse Dynamics Pretraining
papers.summary: Vision-language-action (VLA) models have shown great potential in building generalist robots, but still face a dilemma-misalignment of 2D image forecasting and 3D action prediction. Besides, such a vision-action entangled training manner limits model learning from large-scale, action-free web video data. To address these issues, we propose DeFI, a novel framework that Decouples visual Forward and Inverse dynamics pretraining to exploit respective data sources, wherein video generation and action prediction are disentangled. We introduce the General Forward Dynamics Model (GFDM), pretrained on diverse human and robot videos for future prediction, and the General Inverse Dynamics Model (GIDM), trained via self-supervised learning to infer latent actions from unlabeled video transitions. These models are then integrated into a unified architecture for end-to-end finetuning on downstream tasks. In this manner, GFDM and GIDM first shine separately and then cooperate for mutual benefit. Extensive experiments on CALVIN ABC-D and SimplerEnv demonstrate state-of-the-art performance, with DeFI achieving an average task length of 4.51 for CALVIN, 51.2% success rate on SimplerEnv-Fractal benchmark and 81.3% success rate in real-world deployment, significantly outperforming prior methods.

ai.tldr: DeFI框架通过分离视觉前向与逆向动力学预训练，解决VLA模型中2D图像预测与3D动作预测的错位问题，提升机器人泛化能力。
ai.motivation: 现有视觉-语言-动作模型存在2D图像预测与3D动作预测的错位问题，且视觉-动作耦合的训练方式限制了从大规模无动作网络视频数据中学习的能力。
ai.method: 提出DeFI框架，分别预训练通用前向动力学模型（GFDM）进行未来帧预测，和通用逆向动力学模型（GIDM）通过自监督学习从视频过渡中推断潜在动作，最后统一架构进行下游任务端到端微调。
ai.result: 在CALVIN ABC-D上平均任务长度达4.51，在SimplerEnv-Fractal基准上成功率51.2%，真实世界部署成功率81.3%，显著优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 为构建通用机器人提供了可扩展的预训练框架，支持利用不同数据源分别优化视觉预测和动作推理模块。

idea_spark.idea: 将复杂动力学分解为前向预测和逆向推理两个可独立优化的子问题
idea_spark.risk: 两阶段训练可能引入模块间协调开销
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类运动学习中观察学习与动作模仿分离的启发

en.tldr: The DeFI framework decouples visual forward and inverse dynamics pretraining to address misalignment between 2D image forecasting and 3D action prediction in VLA models, improving robot generalization.
en.motivation: Current vision-language-action models suffer from misalignment between 2D image forecasting and 3D action prediction, and their vision-action entangled training limits learning from action-free web video data.
en.method: Proposes DeFI with separate pretraining of General Forward Dynamics Model (GFDM) for future prediction and General Inverse Dynamics Model (GIDM) for inferring latent actions via self-supervised learning, followed by unified end-to-end finetuning.
en.result: Achieves average task length of 4.51 on CALVIN ABC-D, 51.2% success rate on SimplerEnv-Fractal benchmark, and 81.3% success rate in real-world deployment, significantly outperforming prior methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable pretraining framework for generalist robots that leverages different data sources to separately optimize visual prediction and action inference modules.

idea_spark.idea: Decompose complex dynamics into forward prediction and inverse inference subproblems that can be optimized independently
idea_spark.risk: Two-stage training may introduce coordination overhead between modules
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by separation of observational learning and action imitation in human motor learning

papers.title: Safety, Security, and Cognitive Risks in State-Space Models: A Systematic Threat Analysis with Spectral, Stateful, and Capacity Attacks
papers.summary: State-Space Models (SSMs) -- structured SSMs (S4, S4D, DSS, S5), selective SSMs (Mamba, Mamba-2), and hybrid architectures (Jamba) -- are deployed in safety-critical long-context applications: genomic analysis, clinical time-series forecasting, and cybersecurity log processing. Their linear-time scaling is compelling, yet the security properties of their compressed-state recurrent architectures remain unstudied.   We present the first systematic treatment of SSM safety, security, and cognitive risks. Seven contributions: (1) Formal threat framework -- SSM Attack Surface (five layers), State Integrity Violation (StIV), Cross-Context Amplification Ratio $\mathcal{X}_\mathcal{S}$, and a Spectral Sensitivity Proposition grounded in the $H_\infty$ norm. (2) Three novel attack classes: spectral adversarial attacks (transfer-function gain exploitation), delayed-trigger stateful backdoors (activate thousands of steps after injection), and state capacity saturation (entropy flooding forces silent forgetting). (3) 14 MITRE ATLAS technique extensions across the full tactic chain. (4) Six-profile attacker taxonomy with kill chains for genomics, clinical, and cybersecurity domains. (5) Four cognitive risk hypotheses grounded in state-compression mechanics. (6) Governance-aligned mitigations mapped to CREST, NIST AI 600-1, and EU AI Act. (7) Empirical evaluation: targeted genomic injection achieves $\mathrm{StIV}=0.519$ vs. $0.086$ random ($6.0\times$, $p<0.001$); PGD state injection achieves $156\times$ output perturbation over random; SSD-structured extraction confirmed at $O(N^2)$ vs. $O(N^3)$ query complexity ($N\times$ speedup). Validation on pretrained checkpoints is detailed in the Appendix.

ai.tldr: 本文首次对状态空间模型（SSM）的安全、安保和认知风险进行了系统性威胁分析，提出了新的攻击类别、威胁框架和缓解措施。
ai.motivation: 状态空间模型（如S4、Mamba）因其线性时间缩放特性被部署于基因组分析、临床时间序列预测等安全关键的长上下文应用中，但其压缩状态循环架构的安全属性尚未被研究。
ai.method: 提出了一个正式的威胁框架（SSM攻击面、状态完整性违规等），并设计了三种新型攻击类别：频谱对抗攻击、延迟触发状态后门和状态容量饱和攻击。
ai.result: 实证评估显示，针对性基因组注入攻击的状态完整性违规值显著高于随机攻击（6.0倍，p<0.001）；PGD状态注入攻击产生的输出扰动是随机攻击的156倍；验证了SSD结构化提取的查询复杂度优势。
ai.help_to_user: 为在安全关键领域部署状态空间模型的研究人员和工程师提供了首个系统性的安全威胁框架、攻击向量分析和符合治理标准的缓解措施映射。

idea_spark.idea: 利用状态空间模型的频谱特性（传递函数增益）设计对抗样本，或通过熵洪水攻击饱和其状态容量以引发静默遗忘。
idea_spark.risk: 攻击可能在注入数千步后延迟触发，或通过跨上下文放大造成隐蔽的损害，对实时检测系统构成挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对模型内部状态动力学进行形式化安全验证的研究，以及开发针对状态压缩架构的主动防御机制。

zh.tldr: 本文首次对状态空间模型（SSM）的安全、安保和认知风险进行了系统性威胁分析，提出了新的攻击类别、威胁框架和缓解措施。
zh.motivation: 状态空间模型（如S4、Mamba）因其线性时间缩放特性被部署于基因组分析、临床时间序列预测等安全关键的长上下文应用中，但其压缩状态循环架构的安全属性尚未被研究。
zh.method: 提出了一个正式的威胁框架（SSM攻击面、状态完整性违规等），并设计了三种新型攻击类别：频谱对抗攻击、延迟触发状态后门和状态容量饱和攻击。
zh.result: 实证评估显示，针对性基因组注入攻击的状态完整性违规值显著高于随机攻击（6.0倍，p<0.001）；PGD状态注入攻击产生的输出扰动是随机攻击的156倍；验证了SSD结构化提取的查询复杂度优势。
zh.help_to_user: 为在安全关键领域部署状态空间模型的研究人员和工程师提供了首个系统性的安全威胁框架、攻击向量分析和符合治理标准的缓解措施映射。

idea_spark.idea: 利用状态空间模型的频谱特性（传递函数增益）设计对抗样本，或通过熵洪水攻击饱和其状态容量以引发静默遗忘。
idea_spark.risk: 攻击可能在注入数千步后延迟触发，或通过跨上下文放大造成隐蔽的损害，对实时检测系统构成挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对模型内部状态动力学进行形式化安全验证的研究，以及开发针对状态压缩架构的主动防御机制。

en.tldr: This paper presents the first systematic threat analysis of safety, security, and cognitive risks in State-Space Models (SSMs), introducing novel attack classes, a threat framework, and mitigations.
en.motivation: State-Space Models (e.g., S4, Mamba) are deployed in safety-critical long-context applications like genomic analysis due to their linear-time scaling, but the security properties of their compressed-state recurrent architectures remain unstudied.
en.method: Proposes a formal threat framework (SSM Attack Surface, State Integrity Violation, etc.) and designs three novel attack classes: spectral adversarial attacks, delayed-trigger stateful backdoors, and state capacity saturation attacks.
en.result: Empirical evaluation shows targeted genomic injection achieves significantly higher State Integrity Violation than random attacks (6.0×, p<0.001); PGD state injection achieves 156× output perturbation over random; confirms query complexity advantage for SSD-structured extraction.
en.help_to_user: Provides the first systematic security threat framework, attack vector analysis, and governance-aligned mitigation mappings for researchers and engineers deploying SSMs in safety-critical domains.

idea_spark.idea: Design adversarial examples by exploiting the spectral properties (transfer-function gain) of SSMs, or saturate their state capacity via entropy flooding to induce silent forgetting.
idea_spark.risk: Attacks may activate thousands of steps after injection or cause covert damage via cross-context amplification, posing challenges for real-time detection systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires research on formal security verification of model internal state dynamics and the development of active defense mechanisms tailored for state-compression architectures.

papers.title: State Transfer Reveals Reuse in Controlled Routing
papers.summary: Prompt-based interventions can change model behavior, but trained success alone does not identify where the behaviorally relevant state is represented. We study this question in controlled routing tasks using interfaces chosen on support data, held-out query evaluation, and matched necessity, sufficiency, and wrong-interface controls. On GPT-2 triop, an early interface supports exact transfer under these tests. On GPT-2 add/sub, zero-retrain compiled transfer at the fixed interface recovers most of donor routing accuracy, while trainable prompt slots can relearn the same behavior at several other positions only after additional support examples and optimization. These results distinguish fixed-interface reuse from prompt relocation in a setting where the two can be tested directly. Qwen routing provides a cross-architecture consistency check for the same matched-interface pattern at the operator token, although donor-specific identity on the local V-path remains unresolved. Generation and reasoning branches are used to map scope: they show broader transport or weaker controller identifiability once control depends on longer trajectories or harder selection. In controlled routing, fixed-interface transfer is therefore stronger evidence of reuse than trained prompt success alone.

ai.tldr: 通过状态转移实验，在受控路由任务中区分了固定接口重用与提示重定位，证明固定接口转移比单纯训练提示成功更能证明模型内部状态的重用。
ai.motivation: 仅凭训练成功的提示干预无法确定行为相关状态在模型中的具体表示位置，需要更严格的测试来识别状态重用。
ai.method: 在GPT-2 triop/add/sub和Qwen模型上设计受控路由任务，使用支持数据选择接口，通过保持查询评估以及匹配的必要性、充分性和错误接口控制进行测试，比较固定接口转移与可训练提示槽的重新学习。
ai.result: GPT-2 triop中早期接口支持精确转移；GPT-2 add/sub中固定接口的零重训练编译转移恢复了大部分供体路由精度，而可训练提示槽需额外样本和优化才能在其它位置重新学习相同行为；Qwen在算子令牌上显示出相同的匹配接口模式。
ai.help_to_user: 为检测语言模型内部状态重用提供了严格的实验框架，区分了真正的状态转移与通过提示重新学习的行为。

idea_spark.idea: 将受控路由任务与生成/推理分支结合，映射状态转移的范围，探索更长轨迹或更复杂选择下的控制器可识别性。
idea_spark.risk: 跨架构一致性检查中，供体特定身份在局部V路径上仍未解决，可能影响泛化结论。
idea_spark.inspiration: 通过匹配接口控制实验，可将行为干预的成功归因于状态重用而非参数重新配置，推动更精细的模型可解释性研究。

zh.motivation: 仅凭训练成功的提示干预无法确定行为相关状态在模型中的具体表示位置，需要更严格的测试来识别状态重用。

zh.result: GPT-2 triop中早期接口支持精确转移；GPT-2 add/sub中固定接口的零重训练编译转移恢复了大部分供体路由精度，而可训练提示槽需额外样本和优化才能在其它位置重新学习相同行为；Qwen在算子令牌上显示出相同的匹配接口模式。

en.tldr: State transfer experiments in controlled routing tasks distinguish fixed-interface reuse from prompt relocation, demonstrating that fixed-interface transfer provides stronger evidence of internal state reuse than trained prompt success alone.
en.motivation: Prompt-based interventions that succeed in training alone cannot identify where behaviorally relevant state is represented; stricter tests are needed to identify state reuse.
en.method: Designed controlled routing tasks on GPT-2 triop/add/sub and Qwen models, using support data to choose interfaces, with held-out query evaluation and matched necessity, sufficiency, and wrong-interface controls to compare fixed-interface transfer against retrainable prompt slots.
en.result: Early interface in GPT-2 triop supports exact transfer; zero-retrain compiled transfer at fixed interface in GPT-2 add/sub recovers most donor routing accuracy, while trainable prompt slots require additional examples and optimization to relearn behavior at other positions; Qwen shows same matched-interface pattern at operator token.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous experimental framework for detecting internal state reuse in language models, distinguishing genuine state transfer from behavior relearned via prompts.

idea_spark.idea: Combine controlled routing tasks with generation/reasoning branches to map scope of state transfer, exploring controller identifiability under longer trajectories or harder selection.
idea_spark.risk: Donor-specific identity on the local V-path remains unresolved in cross-architecture consistency checks, potentially affecting generalization conclusions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Matched-interface control experiments allow attributing behavioral intervention success to state reuse rather than parameter reconfiguration, advancing finer-grained model interpretability research.

papers.title: Multi-stream Quickest Change Detection: Foundations and Recent Advances
papers.summary: This paper provides an overview of recent developments in quickest change detection (QCD) for high-dimensional multi-sensor systems, with an emphasis on settings involving structural constraints and limited sensing resources. Classical QCD methodologies, while well understood in low-dimensional and fully observed regimes, face significant challenges when extended to modern applications characterized by large-scale data, constrained sampling or communication, and heterogeneous signal structures. We review key approaches for handling high dimensionality, including methods that exploit sparsity, and other forms of signal heterogeneity. Additionally, we discuss sampling constraints, where observations must be selected or acquired sequentially under resource limitations. Multi-stream applications can require making multiple detections, for example when detecting changes separately in different streams. The underlying assumptions on probability models, the types of changes taking place, commonly used decision-making criteria, performance indices, and error types are described. We also briefly discuss the application of machine learning in cases where the underlying probability models are not known or there is a need to select which sensors should monitor the phenomena because of the large scale of the system.

ai.tldr: 本文综述了高维多传感器系统中快速变化检测的最新进展，重点关注结构约束和有限感知资源下的方法。
ai.motivation: 经典快速变化检测方法在低维和完全观测场景下成熟，但在大规模数据、受限采样/通信和异构信号结构的现代应用中面临挑战。
ai.method: 回顾了处理高维性的关键方法（如利用稀疏性）、信号异质性处理技术、资源限制下的序贯观测选择策略，以及多流检测场景。
ai.result: 系统梳理了概率模型假设、变化类型、决策准则、性能指标和错误类型，并探讨了模型未知或大规模系统传感器选择时机器学习技术的应用。
ai.help_to_user: 为高维受限感知场景下的变化检测提供了方法框架和前沿方向参考。

idea_spark.idea: 将稀疏感知与自适应采样结合，设计轻量级多流变化检测器。
idea_spark.risk: 高维场景下理论性能保证可能弱化，需谨慎验证实际部署效果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在物联网边缘设备等资源受限环境中实现高效异常监测。

en.tldr: This paper reviews recent advances in quickest change detection for high-dimensional multi-sensor systems, focusing on settings with structural constraints and limited sensing resources.
en.motivation: Classical QCD methods, while well-established for low-dimensional fully observed regimes, struggle in modern applications with large-scale data, constrained sampling/communication, and heterogeneous signal structures.
en.method: Reviews key approaches for high-dimensionality (e.g., exploiting sparsity), handling signal heterogeneity, sequential observation selection under resource constraints, and multi-stream detection scenarios.
en.result: Systematically outlines probability model assumptions, change types, decision criteria, performance metrics, and error types, and discusses machine learning applications when models are unknown or sensor selection is needed in large-scale systems.
en.help_to_user: Provides a methodological framework and frontier directions for change detection in high-dimensional resource-constrained sensing scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Combine sparse sensing with adaptive sampling to design lightweight multi-stream change detectors.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical performance guarantees may weaken in high-dimensional settings, requiring careful validation in real deployments.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires efficient anomaly monitoring in resource-constrained environments like IoT edge devices.

papers.title: Optimizing Memory Allocation in Distributed Clusters with Predictive Modeling
papers.summary: In modern distributed systems, efficient resource allocation is a vital aspect to maintain scalability, reduce operational costs, and ensure fast execution even across heterogeneous workloads. Predictive models for resource usage are essential tools for optimizing allocation and preventing system bottlenecks. Predictive memory allocation has asymmetric costs as a key challenge: underallocation causes failures while overallocation wastes memory.   We propose a regression method based on a LightGBM and XGBoost ensemble trained to predict high conditional quantiles. To further account for the high cost of underallocations we add a multiplicative safety factor. With our method we are able to reduce the number of under-allocated jobs from 4.17% to 2.89% and average overallocation from 148% to 44.51% on a real-world dataset of build jobs provided by SAP. We further explore the pareto frontier between optimization for underallocation and for overallocation.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于LightGBM和XGBoost集成学习的回归方法，通过预测高条件分位数并添加安全系数，优化分布式集群中的内存分配。
ai.motivation: 分布式系统中内存分配存在不对称成本：分配不足导致任务失败，分配过度浪费内存资源。
ai.method: 使用LightGBM和XGBoost集成模型预测高条件分位数，并引入乘法安全系数以应对分配不足的高成本。
ai.result: 在SAP真实构建任务数据集上，将分配不足任务比例从4.17%降至2.89%，平均过度分配从148%降至44.51%。
ai.help_to_user: 为分布式系统管理员提供基于预测模型的内存分配优化方案，平衡分配不足与过度分配的风险。

idea_spark.idea: 将不对称成本建模为分位数回归问题，结合安全系数实现风险可控的分配策略。
idea_spark.risk: 模型对工作负载变化的适应性需要持续验证，安全系数可能引入新的资源浪费。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将经济学中的风险决策框架应用于系统资源管理，实现成本感知的自动化分配。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于LightGBM和XGBoost集成学习的回归方法，通过预测高条件分位数并添加安全系数，优化分布式集群中的内存分配。
zh.motivation: 分布式系统中内存分配存在不对称成本：分配不足导致任务失败，分配过度浪费内存资源。

idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将经济学中的风险决策框架应用于系统资源管理，实现成本感知的自动化分配。

en.tldr: Proposes a regression method using LightGBM and XGBoost ensemble to predict high conditional quantiles with a safety factor, optimizing memory allocation in distributed clusters.
en.motivation: Memory allocation in distributed systems has asymmetric costs: underallocation causes failures while overallocation wastes memory resources.
en.method: Uses LightGBM and XGBoost ensemble to predict high conditional quantiles, incorporating a multiplicative safety factor to address the high cost of underallocations.
en.result: On a real-world SAP build jobs dataset, reduces underallocated jobs from 4.17% to 2.89% and average overallocation from 148% to 44.51%.
en.help_to_user: Provides system administrators with a predictive modeling approach for memory allocation optimization that balances underallocation and overallocation risks.

idea_spark.idea: Model asymmetric costs as a quantile regression problem combined with safety factors for risk-controlled allocation.
idea_spark.risk: Model adaptability to workload changes requires continuous validation; safety factors may introduce new resource waste.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires applying economic risk-decision frameworks to system resource management for cost-aware automated allocation.

papers.title: A Control Architecture for Training-Free Memory Use
papers.summary: Prompt-injected memory can improve reasoning without updating model weights, but it also creates a control problem: retrieved content helps only when it is applied in the right state. We study this problem in a strict training-free setting and formulate it as applicability control: when to trigger a memory-assisted second pass, when to trust it, and how to maintain the memory bank over time. Our method combines uncertainty-based routing, confidence-based selective acceptance, bank selection across rule and exemplar memory, and evidence-based governance of the memory bank over time. Under a locked training-free protocol with compute-matched controls, it improves two core arithmetic benchmarks by +7.0 points on SVAMP and +7.67 points on ASDiv over baseline. The same architecture also transfers to QA and agent benchmarks with smaller positive effects and shows the same positive direction on a second checkpoint for the main arithmetic tasks. On arithmetic, the main empirical pattern is that the control architecture, rather than raw memory exposure, drives the improvements on SVAMP and ASDiv. Mechanistically, confidence separates helpful from harmful rule-bank interventions, and under fixed retrieval the repair-versus-corrupt difference localizes to rows whose retrieved set actually contains the edited entries.

ai.tldr: 提出一种免训练的记忆使用控制架构，通过适用性控制（何时触发记忆辅助、何时信任记忆、如何维护记忆库）来提升大语言模型在算术推理任务上的性能。
ai.motivation: 提示注入的记忆可以在不更新模型权重的情况下辅助推理，但检索到的内容仅在正确状态下应用才有效，这产生了控制问题。
ai.method: 结合基于不确定性的路由、基于置信度的选择性接受、规则与示例记忆的库选择，以及基于证据的记忆库长期治理。
ai.result: 在严格的免训练协议下，该架构将SVAMP和ASDiv算术基准分别提升7.0分和7.67分；控制架构本身（而非单纯记忆暴露）是性能提升的主要驱动力。
ai.help_to_user: 为在推理中动态、安全地利用外部记忆提供了一套可操作的免训练控制框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将记忆辅助视为一个控制问题，并分解为触发、信任、维护三个子问题分别设计机制。
idea_spark.risk: 在任务分布变化或记忆库质量下降时，控制机制可能失效，需设计更鲁棒的适应性策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 对于任何依赖外部知识注入的系统，均可借鉴此“适用性控制”框架来管理知识的使用时机与可信度。

zh.tldr: 提出一种免训练的记忆使用控制架构，通过适用性控制（何时触发记忆辅助、何时信任记忆、如何维护记忆库）来提升大语言模型在算术推理任务上的性能。

zh.help_to_user: 为在推理中动态、安全地利用外部记忆提供了一套可操作的免训练控制框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将记忆辅助视为一个控制问题，并分解为触发、信任、维护三个子问题分别设计机制。
idea_spark.risk: 在任务分布变化或记忆库质量下降时，控制机制可能失效，需设计更鲁棒的适应性策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 对于任何依赖外部知识注入的系统，均可借鉴此“适用性控制”框架来管理知识的使用时机与可信度。

en.tldr: Proposes a training-free control architecture for memory use, employing applicability control (when to trigger memory assistance, when to trust it, how to maintain the memory bank) to improve LLM performance on arithmetic reasoning tasks.
en.motivation: Prompt-injected memory can aid reasoning without weight updates, but retrieved content only helps when applied in the right state, creating a control problem.
en.method: Combines uncertainty-based routing, confidence-based selective acceptance, bank selection across rule and exemplar memory, and evidence-based governance of the memory bank over time.
en.result: Under a strict training-free protocol, the architecture improves SVAMP and ASDiv arithmetic benchmarks by +7.0 and +7.67 points respectively; the control architecture itself, not raw memory exposure, drives the gains.
en.help_to_user: Provides an actionable, training-free control framework for dynamically and safely leveraging external memory during inference.

idea_spark.idea: Frame memory assistance as a control problem and decompose it into sub-problems of triggering, trusting, and maintaining, with dedicated mechanisms for each.
idea_spark.risk: Control mechanisms may fail if task distribution shifts or memory bank quality degrades, necessitating more robust adaptation strategies.
idea_spark.inspiration: For any system relying on external knowledge injection, this 'applicability control' framework can inspire management of knowledge usage timing and credibility.

papers.title: SeekerGym: A Benchmark for Reliable Information Seeking
papers.summary: Despite their substantial successes, AI agents continue to face fundamental challenges in terms of trustworthiness. Consider deep research agents, tasked with searching for information relevant to a given topic-while AI agents can perform effective information retrieval, there is little guarantee regarding the completeness of this information. Gaps in retrieved information can leave biases that mislead users even if the information they are given is correct and relevant. We introduce SeekerGym, a benchmark designed to evaluate the completeness of information retrieved by AI agents. In addition, SeekerGym also measures how well agents quantify their uncertainty in the completeness of their information; if an agent fails to retrieve all relevant information, it is useful for it to at least quantify how much might be missing. At a high level, each task in SeekerGym is a document (e.g., a Wikipedia article), and the AI agent must issue queries to retrieve passages from that document. Intuitively, the document comprehensively covers a topic, so the ability to retrieve its sections directly measures completeness of information retrieval. In addition to Wikipedia, we also consider machine learning survey papers, where the goal is to retrieve relevant sections of a survey paper. We benchmark several models and algorithms; the best approaches retrieve 42.5% of passages on Wikipedia and 29.2% on ML Surveys, leaving substantial room for improvement.

ai.tldr: SeekerGym是一个评估AI智能体信息检索完整性的基准，通过文档段落检索任务衡量信息完备性和不确定性量化能力。
ai.motivation: 现有AI智能体在信息检索时缺乏完整性保证，信息缺口可能导致偏见并误导用户，即使检索到的信息本身正确。
ai.method: 构建基于文档（如维基百科文章和机器学习综述论文）的检索任务，智能体需通过查询检索文档段落，直接衡量信息检索的完整性。
ai.result: 最佳模型在维基百科任务中检索到42.5%的段落，在ML综述任务中检索到29.2%的段落，显示仍有很大改进空间。
ai.help_to_user: 为评估信息检索系统的完整性提供标准化测试框架，帮助识别信息缺口并促进不确定性量化能力的发展。

idea_spark.idea: 将完整性评估与实时查询优化结合，动态调整检索策略以覆盖信息盲区。
idea_spark.risk: 基准任务可能无法完全模拟真实场景中信息分布的复杂性和动态性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发开发能自我评估信息覆盖度的检索系统，在无法获取完整信息时主动提示用户潜在缺失。

en.tldr: SeekerGym is a benchmark for evaluating the completeness of information retrieved by AI agents, measuring both retrieval coverage and uncertainty quantification through document passage retrieval tasks.
en.motivation: AI agents lack guarantees about information retrieval completeness, where gaps can introduce biases that mislead users even with correct retrieved information.
en.method: Construct retrieval tasks based on documents (e.g., Wikipedia articles and ML survey papers) where agents must retrieve passages via queries, directly measuring information retrieval completeness.
en.result: Best approaches retrieve 42.5% of passages on Wikipedia and 29.2% on ML Surveys, indicating substantial room for improvement.
en.help_to_user: Provides a standardized test framework for evaluating information retrieval system completeness, helping identify information gaps and advance uncertainty quantification capabilities.

idea_spark.idea: Combine completeness assessment with real-time query optimization to dynamically adjust retrieval strategies for covering information blind spots.
idea_spark.risk: Benchmark tasks may not fully simulate the complexity and dynamics of information distribution in real-world scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires developing retrieval systems that self-assess information coverage and proactively alert users to potential missing information when completeness cannot be achieved.

papers.title: One Pass for All: A Discrete Diffusion Model for Knowledge Graph Triple Set Prediction
papers.summary: Knowledge Graphs (KGs) are composed of triples, and the goal of Knowledge Graph Completion (KGC) is to infer the missing factual triples. Traditional KGC tasks predict missing elements in a triple given one or two of its elements. As a more realistic task, the Triple Set Prediction (TSP) task aims to infer the set of missing triples conditioned only on the observed knowledge graph, without assuming any partial information about the missing triples. Existing TSP methods predict the set of missing triples in a triple-by-triple manner, falling short in capturing the dependencies among the predicted triples to ensure consistency. To address this issue, we propose a novel discrete diffusion model termed DiffTSP that treats TSP as a generative task. DiffTSP progressively adds noise to the KG through a discrete diffusion process, achieved by masking relational edges. The reverse process then gradually recovers the complete KG conditioned on the incomplete graph. To this end, we design a structure-aware denoising network that integrates a relational context encoder with a relational graph diffusion transformer for knowledge graph generation. DiffTSP can generate the complete set of triples in a one-pass manner while ensuring the dependencies among the predicted triples. Our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on three public datasets. Code: https://github.com/ADMIS-TONGJI/DiffTSP.

ai.tldr: DiffTSP：一种用于知识图谱三元组集合预测的离散扩散模型，通过一次前向过程生成完整三元组集合，并确保预测间的依赖关系。
ai.motivation: 现有的三元组集合预测方法以逐个三元组的方式预测，难以捕捉预测三元组之间的依赖关系以保证一致性。
ai.method: 将TSP视为生成任务，提出离散扩散模型DiffTSP。通过掩码关系边实现离散扩散过程，并设计结构感知去噪网络（集成关系上下文编码器和关系图扩散Transformer）进行知识图谱生成。
ai.result: 在三个公共数据集上取得了最先进的性能。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种能一次性生成完整且内部一致的知识图谱三元组集合的新方法，适用于更现实的知识图谱补全场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将集合预测任务重构为条件图生成问题，利用扩散模型的一次性生成特性来建模元素间的依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 模型性能可能高度依赖于所设计的关系图扩散Transformer对复杂图结构的建模能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 对于其他需要输出结构化集合（如分子图、场景图）的任务，可探索类似的离散扩散生成框架来保证输出元素间的全局一致性。

zh.tldr: DiffTSP：一种用于知识图谱三元组集合预测的离散扩散模型，通过一次前向过程生成完整三元组集合，并确保预测间的依赖关系。
zh.motivation: 现有的三元组集合预测方法以逐个三元组的方式预测，难以捕捉预测三元组之间的依赖关系以保证一致性。

idea_spark.idea: 将集合预测任务重构为条件图生成问题，利用扩散模型的一次性生成特性来建模元素间的依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 模型性能可能高度依赖于所设计的关系图扩散Transformer对复杂图结构的建模能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 对于其他需要输出结构化集合（如分子图、场景图）的任务，可探索类似的离散扩散生成框架来保证输出元素间的全局一致性。

en.tldr: DiffTSP: A discrete diffusion model for Knowledge Graph Triple Set Prediction that generates the complete set of triples in one pass while ensuring dependencies among predictions.
en.motivation: Existing Triple Set Prediction methods predict missing triples in a triple-by-triple manner, failing to capture dependencies among predicted triples to ensure consistency.
en.method: Treats TSP as a generative task and proposes DiffTSP, a discrete diffusion model. It adds noise via a discrete diffusion process by masking relational edges, and designs a structure-aware denoising network integrating a relational context encoder with a relational graph diffusion transformer for KG generation.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on three public datasets.
en.help_to_user: Provides a novel method to generate a complete and internally consistent set of KG triples in one pass, suitable for more realistic Knowledge Graph Completion scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Reframing the set prediction task as a conditional graph generation problem, leveraging the one-pass generation property of diffusion models to model dependencies among elements.
idea_spark.risk: Model performance may heavily depend on the designed relational graph diffusion transformer's ability to model complex graph structures.
idea_spark.inspiration: For other tasks requiring structured set outputs (e.g., molecular graphs, scene graphs), similar discrete diffusion generation frameworks could be explored to ensure global consistency among output elements.

papers.title: Ranking Abuse via Strategic Pairwise Data Perturbations
papers.summary: Pairwise ranking systems based on Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE), such as the Bradley-Terry model, are widely used to aggregate preferences from pairwise comparisons. However, their robustness under strategic data manipulation remains insufficiently understood.   In this paper, we study the vulnerability of MLE-based ranking systems to adversarial perturbations. We formulate the manipulation task as a constrained combinatorial optimization problem and propose an Adaptive Subset Selection Attack (ASSA) to efficiently identify high-impact perturbations.   Experimental results on both synthetic data and real-world election datasets show that MLE-based rankings exhibit a sharp phase-transition behavior: beyond a small perturbation budget, a limited number of strategic voters can significantly alter the global ranking. In particular, our method consistently outperforms random and greedy baselines under constrained budgets.   These findings reveal a fundamental sensitivity of MLE-based ranking mechanisms to structured perturbations and highlight the need for more robust aggregation methods in collective decision-making systems.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了基于最大似然估计的成对排序系统在对抗性数据扰动下的脆弱性，提出了一种自适应子集选择攻击方法，揭示了此类系统在有限扰动下即可发生全局排名剧变的相变行为。
ai.motivation: 基于最大似然估计的成对排序系统（如Bradley-Terry模型）被广泛用于汇总偏好，但其在战略性数据操纵下的鲁棒性尚未得到充分理解。
ai.method: 将操纵任务建模为约束组合优化问题，提出自适应子集选择攻击（ASSA）来高效识别高影响力扰动。
ai.result: 在合成数据和真实选举数据集上的实验表明，MLE排序表现出明显的相变行为：超出小扰动预算后，少量战略投票者即可显著改变全局排名。ASSA在约束预算下持续优于随机和贪婪基线。
ai.help_to_user: 揭示了MLE排序机制对结构化扰动的基本敏感性，为构建更鲁棒的集体决策系统提供了实证依据。

idea_spark.idea: 探索其他排序模型（如Plackett-Luce）的相变脆弱性，或设计基于认证鲁棒性的防御机制。
idea_spark.risk: 攻击方法可能被恶意用于操纵现实中的排名系统（如体育赛事、产品推荐）。
idea_spark.inspiration: 相变现象提示可能存在临界扰动预算的理论阈值，可引导鲁棒性边界的形式化分析。

en.tldr: This paper studies the vulnerability of Maximum Likelihood Estimation-based pairwise ranking systems to adversarial data perturbations, proposes an Adaptive Subset Selection Attack, and reveals a phase-transition behavior where global rankings shift dramatically under limited perturbations.
en.motivation: Pairwise ranking systems based on Maximum Likelihood Estimation (e.g., Bradley-Terry model) are widely used to aggregate preferences, but their robustness under strategic data manipulation remains insufficiently understood.
en.method: Formulates the manipulation task as a constrained combinatorial optimization problem and proposes an Adaptive Subset Selection Attack (ASSA) to efficiently identify high-impact perturbations.
en.result: Experiments on synthetic data and real-world election datasets show that MLE-based rankings exhibit sharp phase-transition behavior: beyond a small perturbation budget, a limited number of strategic voters can significantly alter the global ranking. ASSA consistently outperforms random and greedy baselines under constrained budgets.
en.help_to_user: Reveals a fundamental sensitivity of MLE-based ranking mechanisms to structured perturbations and highlights the need for more robust aggregation methods in collective decision-making systems.

idea_spark.idea: Explore phase-transition vulnerabilities in other ranking models (e.g., Plackett-Luce) or design defenses based on certified robustness.
idea_spark.risk: Attack methods could be maliciously used to manipulate real-world ranking systems (e.g., sports competitions, product recommendations).
idea_spark.inspiration: The phase-transition phenomenon suggests the existence of theoretical thresholds for critical perturbation budgets, which could guide formal analysis of robustness boundaries.

papers.title: Active World-Model with 4D-informed Retrieval for Exploration and Awareness
papers.summary: Physical awareness, especially in a large and dynamic environment, is shaped by sensing decisions that determine observability across space, time, and scale, while observations impact the quality of sensing decisions. This loopy information structure makes physical awareness a fundamentally challenging decision problem with partial observations. While in the past decade we have witnessed the unprecedented success of reinforcement learning (RL) in problems with full observability, decision problems with partial observation, such as POMDPs, remain largely open: real-world explorations are excessively costly, while sim-to-real pipeline suffer from unobserved viewpoints. We introduce AW4RE (Active World-model with 4D-informed Retrieval for Exploration), an awareness-centric generative world model that provides a sensor-native surrogate environment for exploring sensing queries. Conditioned on a queried sensing action, AW4RE estimates the action-conditioned observation process. This is done by combining 4D-informed evidence retrieval, action-conditioned geometric support with temporal coherence, and conditional generative completion. Experiments demonstrate that AW4RE produces more grounded and consistent predictions than geometry-aware generative baselines under extreme viewpoint shifts, temporal gaps, and sparse geometric support.

ai.tldr: AW4RE是一个主动世界模型，通过4D信息检索为部分可观测环境中的感知查询提供传感器原生代理环境。
ai.motivation: 在大型动态环境中实现物理感知是一个具有部分观测的根本性挑战决策问题，现有方法在真实世界探索成本高且存在未观测视角。
ai.method: 结合4D信息证据检索、动作条件几何支持与时间一致性，以及条件生成补全，估计动作条件下的观测过程。
ai.result: 在极端视角变化、时间间隔和稀疏几何支持下，比几何感知生成基线产生更接地气和一致的预测。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究部分可观测决策问题的研究者提供了可探索感知查询的生成世界模型工具。

idea_spark.idea: 将时间维度信息整合到证据检索中，增强世界模型对动态环境的预测一致性
idea_spark.risk: 对4D数据质量和完整性的依赖可能限制在更稀疏或噪声环境中的应用
idea_spark.inspiration: 可考虑将该框架扩展到多模态传感器融合或长期规划任务中

en.tldr: AW4RE is an active world-model that provides a sensor-native surrogate environment for sensing queries in partially observable settings via 4D-informed retrieval.
en.motivation: Physical awareness in large dynamic environments is a fundamentally challenging decision problem with partial observations, where real-world exploration is costly and unobserved viewpoints persist.
en.method: Estimates the action-conditioned observation process by combining 4D-informed evidence retrieval, action-conditioned geometric support with temporal coherence, and conditional generative completion.
en.result: Produces more grounded and consistent predictions than geometry-aware generative baselines under extreme viewpoint shifts, temporal gaps, and sparse geometric support.
en.help_to_user: Provides researchers studying partially observable decision problems with a generative world model tool for exploring sensing queries.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporating temporal dimension information into evidence retrieval to enhance the predictive consistency of world models for dynamic environments
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on 4D data quality and completeness may limit application in sparser or noisier environments
idea_spark.inspiration: Could consider extending this framework to multimodal sensor fusion or long-horizon planning tasks

papers.title: Decentralized Stability-Constrained Optimal Power Flow for Inverter-Based Power Systems
papers.summary: Future inverter-dominated power systems feature higher variability and more stressed operating conditions, which motivates the consideration of stability in operational settings. Existing approaches to stability-constrained OPF often rely on eigenvalue calculation, global model information, or dynamic evaluation inside optimization formulation, which are computationally intensive and difficult to scale. This paper proposes the first decentralized stability-constrained OPF framework for inverter-based power systems. The key novelty lies in the incorporation of a class of algebraic decentralized small-signal stability criteria that admits tractable representations in steady-state variables and is therefore suitable for optimization. The decentralized stability condition is based on local voltage differences and enables clear theoretical and practical economic interpretation of the stability contribution from each inverter. We define a Nodal Stability Shadow Price (NSSP) for each inverter, and characterize the role of these stability constraints through their associated shadow prices, enabling a nodal interpretation of their economic impacts. It is proved that under active-power-only objectives in lossless networks, binding stability constraints may occur but will admit zero shadow prices if all other operational constraints are inactive. Most importantly, we reveal the importance of considering the opportunity cost of reactive power for inverter-based resources (IBRs) that have limited capacity. When reactive power costs are considered, stability constraints can carry strictly positive shadow prices and admit meaningful economic impacts.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了首个用于逆变器主导电力系统的分散式稳定性约束最优潮流框架，通过代数分散小信号稳定性判据和节点稳定性影子价格实现稳定性的经济解释。
ai.motivation: 未来逆变器主导的电力系统具有更高波动性和更严峻运行条件，需在运行中考虑稳定性。现有稳定性约束最优潮流方法依赖特征值计算、全局模型信息或优化内动态评估，计算量大且难以扩展。
ai.method: 提出分散式稳定性约束最优潮流框架，引入一类基于局部电压差的代数分散小信号稳定性判据，该判据可用稳态变量表示并适合优化。定义每个逆变器的节点稳定性影子价格，通过影子价格表征稳定性约束的经济影响。
ai.result: 证明了在无损网络中仅考虑有功功率目标时，绑定稳定性约束可能出现但影子价格为零；当考虑逆变器有限容量的无功功率机会成本时，稳定性约束可具有严格正影子价格和显著经济影响。
ai.help_to_user: 为逆变器主导电网提供可扩展的分散稳定性约束运行框架，通过影子价格量化稳定性贡献的经济价值，支持运行决策。

idea_spark.idea: 将局部电压差作为分散稳定性代理指标，避免全局动态模型，降低计算复杂度。
idea_spark.risk: 假设条件（如无损网络、特定目标函数）可能限制实际适用性；分散判据的保守性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将物理稳定性条件转化为代数约束并嵌入优化，通过影子价格揭示稳定性资源的经济价值，促进稳定与经济的协同设计。

zh.tldr: 本文提出了首个用于逆变器主导电力系统的分散式稳定性约束最优潮流框架，通过代数分散小信号稳定性判据和节点稳定性影子价格实现稳定性的经济解释。
zh.motivation: 未来逆变器主导的电力系统具有更高波动性和更严峻运行条件，需在运行中考虑稳定性。现有稳定性约束最优潮流方法依赖特征值计算、全局模型信息或优化内动态评估，计算量大且难以扩展。
zh.method: 提出分散式稳定性约束最优潮流框架，引入一类基于局部电压差的代数分散小信号稳定性判据，该判据可用稳态变量表示并适合优化。定义每个逆变器的节点稳定性影子价格，通过影子价格表征稳定性约束的经济影响。
zh.result: 证明了在无损网络中仅考虑有功功率目标时，绑定稳定性约束可能出现但影子价格为零；当考虑逆变器有限容量的无功功率机会成本时，稳定性约束可具有严格正影子价格和显著经济影响。
zh.help_to_user: 为逆变器主导电网提供可扩展的分散稳定性约束运行框架，通过影子价格量化稳定性贡献的经济价值，支持运行决策。

idea_spark.idea: 将局部电压差作为分散稳定性代理指标，避免全局动态模型，降低计算复杂度。
idea_spark.risk: 假设条件（如无损网络、特定目标函数）可能限制实际适用性；分散判据的保守性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将物理稳定性条件转化为代数约束并嵌入优化，通过影子价格揭示稳定性资源的经济价值，促进稳定与经济的协同设计。

en.tldr: This paper proposes the first decentralized stability-constrained optimal power flow framework for inverter-based power systems, employing algebraic decentralized small-signal stability criteria and nodal stability shadow prices to enable economic interpretation of stability.
en.motivation: Future inverter-dominated power systems exhibit higher variability and more stressed operating conditions, necessitating the consideration of stability in operational settings. Existing stability-constrained OPF approaches often rely on eigenvalue calculation, global model information, or dynamic evaluation within optimization, which are computationally intensive and difficult to scale.
en.method: A decentralized stability-constrained OPF framework is proposed, incorporating a class of algebraic decentralized small-signal stability criteria based on local voltage differences, which admit tractable representations in steady-state variables and are suitable for optimization. Nodal Stability Shadow Prices (NSSP) are defined for each inverter to characterize the economic impact of stability constraints through their associated shadow prices.
en.result: It is proved that under active-power-only objectives in lossless networks, binding stability constraints may occur but will admit zero shadow prices if all other operational constraints are inactive. Crucially, when considering the opportunity cost of reactive power for inverter-based resources with limited capacity, stability constraints can carry strictly positive shadow prices and admit meaningful economic impacts.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable decentralized operational framework with stability constraints for inverter-dominated grids, quantifying the economic value of stability contributions through shadow prices to support operational decision-making.

idea_spark.idea: Using local voltage differences as a decentralized stability proxy avoids global dynamic models and reduces computational complexity.
idea_spark.risk: Assumptions (e.g., lossless networks, specific objective functions) may limit practical applicability; conservativeness of decentralized criteria requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires transforming physical stability conditions into algebraic constraints embedded in optimization, revealing the economic value of stability resources through shadow prices, promoting co-design of stability and economics.

papers.title: A Generalized Synthetic Control Method for Baseline Estimation in Demand Response Services
papers.summary: Baseline estimation is critical to Demand Response (DR) settlement in electricity markets, yet existing machine learning methods remain limited in predictive performance, while methodologies from causal inference and counterfactual prediction are still underutilized in this domain. We introduce a Generalized Synthetic Control Method that builds on the classical Synthetic Control Method (SCM) from econometrics. While SCM provides a powerful framework for counterfactual estimation, classical SCM remains a static estimator: it fits the treated unit as a combination of contemporaneous donor units and therefore ignores predictable temporal structure in the residual error. We develop a generalized SCM framework that transforms baseline estimation into a dynamic counterfactual prediction problem by augmenting the donor representation with exogenous features, lagged treated load, and selected lagged donor signals. This enriched representation allows the estimator to capture autoregressive dependence, delayed donor-response patterns, and error-correction effects beyond the scope of standard SCM. The framework further accommodates nonlinear predictors when linear weighting is inadequate, with the greatest benefit arising in limited-data settings. Experiments on the Ausgrid smart-meter dataset show consistent improvements over classical SCM and strong benchmark methods, with the dominant performance gains driven by dynamic augmentation.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种广义合成控制方法，用于电力需求响应服务中的基线估计，通过动态增强捐赠单元表示来改进经典合成控制法的预测性能。
ai.motivation: 需求响应结算中的基线估计至关重要，现有机器学习方法预测性能有限，而因果推断和反事实预测方法在该领域尚未充分利用。
ai.method: 扩展经典合成控制法，通过引入外生特征、滞后处理负荷和滞后捐赠信号来增强捐赠表示，将基线估计转化为动态反事实预测问题，并可容纳非线性预测器。
ai.result: 在Ausgrid智能电表数据集上的实验表明，该方法相比经典合成控制法和强基准方法具有一致的性能提升，主要增益来自动态增强。
ai.help_to_user: 为电力市场需求响应结算提供了一个更准确的基线估计框架，特别适用于数据有限的情况。

idea_spark.idea: 在合成控制法中引入时间序列结构（自回归依赖、延迟响应模式、误差修正效应）来提升反事实预测精度。
idea_spark.risk: 动态增强可能增加模型复杂度，在数据极度稀缺时可能导致过拟合；需要仔细选择滞后阶数和外生特征。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将计量经济学中的经典方法与现代预测问题结合，通过特征工程和表示学习来释放传统方法的潜力。

en.tldr: This paper introduces a Generalized Synthetic Control Method for baseline estimation in demand response services, improving predictive performance over classical SCM through dynamic augmentation of donor representations.
en.motivation: Baseline estimation is critical for demand response settlement, yet existing machine learning methods have limited predictive performance, and methodologies from causal inference remain underutilized in this domain.
en.method: Extends the classical Synthetic Control Method by augmenting donor representation with exogenous features, lagged treated load, and lagged donor signals, transforming baseline estimation into a dynamic counterfactual prediction problem that can accommodate nonlinear predictors.
en.result: Experiments on the Ausgrid smart-meter dataset show consistent improvements over classical SCM and strong benchmark methods, with dominant performance gains driven by dynamic augmentation.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more accurate baseline estimation framework for demand response settlement in electricity markets, particularly beneficial in limited-data settings.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate time-series structures (autoregressive dependence, delayed donor-response patterns, error-correction effects) into synthetic control methods to improve counterfactual prediction accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: Dynamic augmentation may increase model complexity and could lead to overfitting in extremely data-scarce scenarios; careful selection of lag orders and exogenous features is required.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine classical econometric methods with modern prediction problems, unlocking the potential of traditional approaches through feature engineering and representation learning.

papers.title: CDSA-Net:Collaborative Decoupling of Vascular Structure and Background for High-Fidelity Coronary Digital Subtraction Angiography
papers.summary: Digital subtraction angiography (DSA) in coronary imaging is fundamentally challenged by physiological motion, forcing reliance on raw angiograms cluttered with anatomical noise. Existing deep learning methods often produced images with two critical clinically unacceptable flaws: persistent boundary artifacts and a loss of native tissue grayscale fidelity that undermined diagnostic confidence. We propose a novel framework termed as CDSA-Net that for the first time explicitly decouples and jointly optimizes vascular structure preservation and realistic background restoration. CDSA-Net introduces two core innovations: (i) A hierarchical geometric prior guidance (HGPG) mechanism, embedded in our coronary structure extraction network (CSENet). It synergistically combines integrated geometric prior (IGP) with gated spatial modulation (GSM) and centerline-aware topology (CAT) loss supervision, ensuring structural continuity. (ii) An adaptive noise module (ANM) within our coronary background restoration network (CBResNet). Unlike standard restoration, ANM uniquely models the stochastic nature of clinical X-ray noise, bridging the domain gap to enable seamless background intensity estimation and the complete elimination of boundary artifacts. The final subtraction is obtained by removing the restored background from the raw angiogram. Quantitatively, it significantly outperformed state-of-the-art methods in vascular intensity correlation and perceptual quality. A 25.6% improvement in morphology assessment efficiency and a 42.9% gain in hemodynamic evaluation speed set a new benchmark for utility in interventional cardiology, while maintaining diagnostic results consistent with raw angiograms. The project code is available at https://github.com/DrThink-ai/CDSA-Net.

ai.tldr: CDSA-Net 是一种用于冠状动脉数字减影血管造影的新框架，通过协同解耦血管结构和背景，实现高保真图像重建，消除边界伪影并保持组织灰度保真度。
ai.motivation: 传统DSA受生理运动影响，现有深度学习方法存在边界伪影和灰度保真度丢失问题，影响临床诊断信心。
ai.method: 提出CDSA-Net框架，包含冠状动脉结构提取网络（CSENet）和冠状动脉背景恢复网络（CBResNet）。CSENet采用分层几何先验引导机制，结合集成几何先验、门控空间调制和中心线感知拓扑损失；CBResNet引入自适应噪声模块，建模临床X射线噪声的随机性。
ai.result: 在血管强度相关性和感知质量上显著优于现有方法，形态评估效率提升25.6%，血流动力学评估速度提升42.9%，诊断结果与原始血管造影一致。
ai.help_to_user: 为介入心脏病学提供高保真冠状动脉DSA图像，提升诊断效率和信心。

idea_spark.idea: 将血管结构与背景噪声解耦并联合优化，而非端到端直接生成减影图像。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于临床X射线噪声的准确建模，在不同设备或采集协议下可能需重新适配。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将解剖结构先验与随机噪声特性分离处理，以提升医学影像重建的临床可接受性。

en.tldr: CDSA-Net is a novel framework for coronary digital subtraction angiography that collaboratively decouples vascular structure and background for high-fidelity image reconstruction, eliminating boundary artifacts while preserving native tissue grayscale fidelity.
en.motivation: Conventional DSA is challenged by physiological motion, and existing deep learning methods suffer from persistent boundary artifacts and loss of grayscale fidelity, undermining diagnostic confidence.
en.method: Proposes CDSA-Net with two core components: Coronary Structure Extraction Network (CSENet) using Hierarchical Geometric Prior Guidance (HGPG) combining Integrated Geometric Prior, Gated Spatial Modulation, and Centerline-Aware Topology loss; and Coronary Background Restoration Network (CBResNet) with an Adaptive Noise Module (ANM) that models the stochastic nature of clinical X-ray noise.
en.result: Significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in vascular intensity correlation and perceptual quality, with a 25.6% improvement in morphology assessment efficiency and 42.9% gain in hemodynamic evaluation speed, while maintaining diagnostic consistency with raw angiograms.
en.help_to_user: Provides high-fidelity coronary DSA images for interventional cardiology, enhancing diagnostic efficiency and confidence.

idea_spark.idea: Decouple vascular structure and background noise for joint optimization, rather than end-to-end direct subtraction image generation.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on accurate modeling of clinical X-ray noise, which may require adaptation across different devices or acquisition protocols.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires separation of anatomical structure priors from stochastic noise characteristics to improve clinical acceptability of medical image reconstruction.

papers.title: A Unified Compliance Aggregator Framework for Automated Multi-Tool Security Assessment of Linux Systems
papers.summary: Assessing the security posture of modern computing systems typically requires the use of multiple specialized tools. These tools focus on different aspects such as configuration compliance, file integrity, and vulnerability exposure, and their outputs are often difficult to interpret collectively. This paper introduces the Unified Compliance Aggregator (UCA), a framework that integrates several open-source security tools into a single composite score representing overall system security. The proposed framework combines outputs from Lynis, OpenSCAP (STIG and CIS profiles), AIDE, Tripwire, and Nmap NSE. A normalization process converts heterogeneous outputs into a consistent 0 to 100 scale, followed by weighted aggregation. We also introduce a logarithmic scoring model for file integrity measurements to address limitations observed in prior linear approaches. Experiments were conducted on Ubuntu 22.04 across different hardening levels and environments. Results show consistent improvement in composite scores as systems are hardened, while also revealing contrasting behavior between compliance and file integrity tools. Two case studies, a basic web server and a DVWA-based system illustrate how the framework can be applied in practical scenarios.

ai.tldr: 本文提出统一合规聚合器框架，集成多种开源安全工具，通过归一化和加权聚合生成0-100综合安全评分，用于Linux系统自动化多工具安全评估。
ai.motivation: 现代计算系统安全评估需使用多种专用工具，这些工具输出异构且难以统一解读，缺乏整体安全态势的综合度量。
ai.method: 集成Lynis、OpenSCAP、AIDE、Tripwire、Nmap NSE等工具输出，通过归一化将异构结果转换为0-100统一尺度，采用加权聚合生成综合评分，并为文件完整性监测引入对数评分模型。
ai.result: 在Ubuntu 22.04不同加固级别和环境中的实验表明，综合评分随系统加固而持续提升，同时揭示了合规工具与文件完整性工具间的对比行为。
ai.help_to_user: 为安全工程师提供自动化多工具集成评估框架，通过统一评分直观反映系统整体安全态势，支持实际场景应用。

idea_spark.idea: 将对数评分模型应用于其他具有指数特征的安全度量指标，如入侵检测告警频率或漏洞风险评分。
idea_spark.risk: 权重设置依赖领域专家经验，可能引入主观偏差；工具覆盖范围有限，可能遗漏特定安全维度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发跨领域多源异构数据的统一度量与可视化呈现研究，如医疗多模态数据或环境监测指标聚合。

en.tldr: This paper proposes the Unified Compliance Aggregator framework that integrates multiple open-source security tools to generate a 0-100 composite security score through normalization and weighted aggregation for automated multi-tool security assessment of Linux systems.
en.motivation: Assessing security posture of modern computing systems requires multiple specialized tools with heterogeneous outputs that are difficult to interpret collectively, lacking comprehensive metrics for overall security态势.
en.method: Integrates outputs from Lynis, OpenSCAP, AIDE, Tripwire, and Nmap NSE, normalizes heterogeneous results to a consistent 0-100 scale, applies weighted aggregation to produce composite scores, and introduces a logarithmic scoring model for file integrity measurements.
en.result: Experiments on Ubuntu 22.04 across different hardening levels show consistent improvement in composite scores as systems are hardened, while revealing contrasting behavior between compliance and file integrity tools.
en.help_to_user: Provides security engineers with an automated multi-tool integration framework that直观 reflects overall system security posture through unified scoring, supporting practical scenario applications.

idea_spark.idea: Apply logarithmic scoring models to other security metrics with exponential characteristics, such as intrusion detection alert frequency or vulnerability risk scoring.
idea_spark.risk: Weight settings rely on domain expert experience, potentially introducing subjective bias; tool coverage is limited and may miss specific security dimensions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires research on unified measurement and visualization of cross-domain multi-source heterogeneous data, such as medical multimodal data or environmental monitoring indicator aggregation.

papers.title: Quantum channel tomography: optimal bounds and a Heisenberg-to-classical phase transition
papers.summary: How many black-box queries to a quantum channel are needed to learn its full classical description? This question lies at the heart of quantum channel tomography (also known as quantum process tomography), a fundamental task in the characterization and validation of quantum hardware. Despite extensive prior work, the optimal query complexity for quantum channel tomography is far from fully understood.   In this paper, we study tomography of an unknown quantum channel with input dimension $d_1$, output dimension $d_2$, and Kraus rank at most $r$, to within error $\varepsilon$. We identify the dilation rate $τ= r d_2 / d_1$ (which always satisfies $τ\geq 1$ due to the trace preservation of quantum channels) as a key parameter, and establish that the optimal query complexity of channel tomography exhibits distinct scaling laws across three regimes of $τ$.   - In the boundary regime ($τ= 1$): we show that the query complexity is $Θ(r d_1 d_2/\varepsilon)$ for Choi trace norm error $\varepsilon$, and is upper bounded by $O(\min\{r d_1^{1.5} d_2/\varepsilon, r d_1 d_2/\varepsilon^2\})$ and lower bounded by $Ω(r d_1 d_2/\varepsilon)$ for diamond norm error $\varepsilon$.   - In the away-from-boundary regime ($τ\geq 1+Ω(1)$): we show that the query complexity is $Θ(r d_1 d_2/\varepsilon^2)$ for both Choi trace norm and diamond norm errors $\varepsilon$.   Our results uncover a sharp Heisenberg-to-classical phase transition in the query complexity of quantum channel tomography: at $τ=1$, the optimal query complexity exhibits Heisenberg scaling $1/\varepsilon$, whereas for $τ\geq 1+Ω(1)$, it exhibits classical scaling $1/\varepsilon^2$. In addition, we show that in the near-boundary regime ($1< τ< 1+o(1)$), the query complexity exhibits a mixture of Heisenberg and classical scaling behaviors.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了量子信道层析的查询复杂度，揭示了其随膨胀率τ变化的三个不同标度律，并发现了一个从海森堡标度到经典标度的尖锐相变。
ai.motivation: 量子信道层析是表征和验证量子硬件的核心任务，但其最优查询复杂度尚未被完全理解。
ai.method: 研究未知量子信道的层析问题，该信道具有输入维度d₁、输出维度d₂和至多为r的Kraus秩。以膨胀率τ = r d₂ / d₁为关键参数，分析在不同τ区间内达到误差ε所需的最优查询复杂度。
ai.result: 在边界区间(τ=1)，查询复杂度对Choi迹范数误差为Θ(r d₁ d₂/ε)，对钻石范数误差介于Ω(r d₁ d₂/ε)和O(min{r d₁¹·⁵ d₂/ε, r d₁ d₂/ε²})之间。在远离边界区间(τ≥1+Ω(1))，两种范数误差下的查询复杂度均为Θ(r d₁ d₂/ε²)。这揭示了一个尖锐的相变。
ai.help_to_user: 为量子硬件表征中的信道层析任务提供了精确的复杂度界限，明确了不同参数区间下的资源需求。

idea_spark.idea: 将膨胀率τ作为关键参数来统一分析量子信道层析的复杂度。
idea_spark.risk: 理论分析基于特定模型假设，实际实验中的噪声和误差可能影响标度律的观测。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在量子信息处理的其他任务中寻找类似的标度律相变或关键控制参数。

zh.tldr: 本文研究了量子信道层析的查询复杂度，揭示了其随膨胀率τ变化的三个不同标度律，并发现了一个从海森堡标度到经典标度的尖锐相变。
zh.motivation: 量子信道层析是表征和验证量子硬件的核心任务，但其最优查询复杂度尚未被完全理解。
zh.method: 研究未知量子信道的层析问题，该信道具有输入维度d₁、输出维度d₂和至多为r的Kraus秩。以膨胀率τ = r d₂ / d₁为关键参数，分析在不同τ区间内达到误差ε所需的最优查询复杂度。
zh.result: 在边界区间(τ=1)，查询复杂度对Choi迹范数误差为Θ(r d₁ d₂/ε)，对钻石范数误差介于Ω(r d₁ d₂/ε)和O(min{r d₁¹·⁵ d₂/ε, r d₁ d₂/ε²})之间。在远离边界区间(τ≥1+Ω(1))，两种范数误差下的查询复杂度均为Θ(r d₁ d₂/ε²)。这揭示了一个尖锐的相变。
zh.help_to_user: 为量子硬件表征中的信道层析任务提供了精确的复杂度界限，明确了不同参数区间下的资源需求。

en.tldr: This paper studies the query complexity of quantum channel tomography, revealing three distinct scaling laws depending on the dilation rate τ, and uncovers a sharp phase transition from Heisenberg to classical scaling.
en.motivation: Quantum channel tomography is a fundamental task for characterizing and validating quantum hardware, yet its optimal query complexity remains poorly understood.
en.method: Studies tomography of an unknown quantum channel with input dimension d₁, output dimension d₂, and Kraus rank at most r. Identifies the dilation rate τ = r d₂ / d₁ as a key parameter and establishes the optimal query complexity to within error ε across different regimes of τ.
en.result: In the boundary regime (τ=1), query complexity is Θ(r d₁ d₂/ε) for Choi trace norm error, and is between Ω(r d₁ d₂/ε) and O(min{r d₁¹·⁵ d₂/ε, r d₁ d₂/ε²}) for diamond norm error. In the away-from-boundary regime (τ≥1+Ω(1)), it is Θ(r d₁ d₂/ε²) for both norms. This reveals a sharp Heisenberg-to-classical phase transition.
en.help_to_user: Provides precise complexity bounds for channel tomography in quantum hardware characterization, clarifying resource requirements in different parameter regimes.

idea_spark.idea: Using the dilation rate τ as a unifying parameter to analyze the complexity of quantum channel tomography.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical analysis relies on specific model assumptions; practical experimental noise and errors may affect the observation of scaling laws.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires searching for similar scaling-law phase transitions or key control parameters in other quantum information processing tasks.

papers.title: Low-noise Pauli-consistent ensemble Monte Carlo for graphene with electron-electron scattering
papers.summary: We investigate Pauli-consistent ensemble Monte Carlo simulations of graphene with explicit intraband electron-electron scattering. To reduce the cost of electron-electron proposal-rate evaluation, we introduce a sampled-partner approximation that replaces the full partner-cell sum by uniform sampling from the instantaneous ensemble, while leaving the event-level collision step unchanged. Comparison with the full-sum reference shows close agreement together with a substantial reduction in computational cost, enabling large-ensemble low-noise simulations. In this regime, systematic oscillatory components become clearly resolved in ensemble-averaged time traces. We show that these oscillations are numerical and originate from deterministic drift on the discretized momentum-space grid. We also discuss a procedure for reducing their impact in recorded observables without modifying the underlying Monte Carlo dynamics.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于石墨烯电子-电子散射的低噪声、保泡利性系综蒙特卡洛方法，通过采样伙伴近似大幅降低计算成本，并分析了离散动量空间网格引起的数值振荡及其抑制方法。
ai.motivation: 在石墨烯的电子-电子散射模拟中，完整的伙伴单元求和计算成本高昂，限制了大规模低噪声系综模拟的实现。
ai.method: 引入采样伙伴近似，用从瞬时系综中均匀采样的方式替代完整的伙伴单元求和，同时保持事件级碰撞步骤不变。
ai.result: 与完整求和参考结果高度一致，计算成本显著降低，实现了大规模低噪声模拟，并清晰解析出由离散动量空间网格确定性漂移引起的数值振荡。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种高效的石墨烯电子-电子散射蒙特卡洛模拟框架，适用于需要高精度、低噪声的大规模载流子动力学研究。

idea_spark.idea: 在保持物理碰撞过程不变的前提下，通过统计采样降低相互作用配对的计算复杂度。
idea_spark.risk: 采样不足可能导致统计偏差，需确保采样数量与系统尺度匹配。
idea_spark.inspiration: 数值振荡的识别与处理方案启示了离散化网格引入的人为效应在其它粒子模拟中也可能存在，需系统诊断。

en.tldr: This work presents a low-noise, Pauli-consistent ensemble Monte Carlo method for graphene with electron-electron scattering, using a sampled-partner approximation to significantly reduce computational cost, and analyzes numerical oscillations arising from discretized momentum-space grids along with mitigation strategies.
en.motivation: The full partner-cell summation in electron-electron scattering simulations for graphene is computationally expensive, limiting large-ensemble low-noise simulations.
en.method: Introduce a sampled-partner approximation that replaces the full partner-cell sum with uniform sampling from the instantaneous ensemble, while leaving the event-level collision step unchanged.
en.result: Close agreement with the full-sum reference is achieved with substantial computational cost reduction, enabling large-ensemble low-noise simulations and clear resolution of systematic oscillations originating from deterministic drift on the discretized momentum-space grid.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient Monte Carlo simulation framework for electron-electron scattering in graphene, suitable for high-precision, low-noise large-scale carrier dynamics studies.

idea_spark.idea: Reduce the computational complexity of interaction pairing through statistical sampling while preserving the physical collision process.
idea_spark.risk: Insufficient sampling may introduce statistical bias; sampling size must be matched to system scale.
idea_spark.inspiration: The identification and handling of numerical oscillations suggest that discretization-grid artifacts may also appear in other particle simulations, requiring systematic diagnosis.

papers.title: An Existence Proof for Neural Language Models That Can Explain Garden-Path Effects via Surprisal
papers.summary: Surprisal theory hypothesizes that the difficulty of human sentence processing increases linearly with surprisal, the negative log-probability of a word given its context. Computational psycholinguistics has tested this hypothesis using language models (LMs) as proxies for human prediction. While surprisal derived from recent neural LMs generally captures human processing difficulty on naturalistic corpora that predominantly consist of simple sentences, it severely underestimates processing difficulty on sentences that require syntactic disambiguation (garden-path effects). This leads to the claim that the processing difficulty of such sentences cannot be reduced to surprisal, although it remains possible that neural LMs simply differ from humans in next-word prediction. In this paper, we investigate whether it is truly impossible to construct a neural LM that can explain garden-path effects via surprisal. Specifically, instead of evaluating off-the-shelf neural LMs, we fine-tune these LMs on garden-path sentences so as to better align surprisal-based reading-time estimates with actual human reading times. Our results show that fine-tuned LMs do not overfit and successfully capture human reading slowdowns on held-out garden-path items; they even improve predictive power for human reading times on naturalistic corpora and preserve their general LM capabilities. These results provide an existence proof for a neural LM that can explain both garden-path effects and naturalistic reading times via surprisal, but also raise a theoretical question: what kind of evidence can truly falsify surprisal theory?

ai.tldr: 本文通过微调神经语言模型，使其基于惊奇度理论成功预测花园路径句的人类阅读困难，证明了存在能够统一解释花园路径效应和自然阅读时间的神经语言模型。
ai.motivation: 现有神经语言模型基于惊奇度理论能预测简单句的阅读困难，但严重低估花园路径句的处理难度，导致有观点认为这类句子的处理困难无法简化为惊奇度。本文探究是否存在神经语言模型能通过惊奇度解释花园路径效应。
ai.method: 不直接评估现成模型，而是对神经语言模型在花园路径句上进行微调，使基于惊奇度的阅读时间估计更贴合人类实际阅读时间，并评估模型在未见花园路径项目上的表现、对自然语料库阅读时间的预测能力以及通用语言模型能力的保持情况。
ai.result: 微调后的模型未出现过拟合，成功捕捉了未见花园路径项目上的人类阅读减速现象，甚至提升了对自然语料库人类阅读时间的预测能力，同时保持了通用语言模型能力。
ai.help_to_user: 为计算心理语言学领域的研究者提供了一个方法论案例，展示了如何通过针对性微调使语言模型更好地模拟人类句子处理过程，并证明了惊奇度理论解释花园路径效应的可能性。

idea_spark.idea: 构建一个‘认知对齐’的模型评估框架，系统测试不同认知约束（如句法、语义、语用）下模型预测与人类行为的一致性。
idea_spark.risk: 微调可能使模型过度适应特定认知效应数据集，降低其语言建模的普遍性；惊奇度理论本身可能无法完全捕捉句子处理的所有认知机制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发对理论可证伪性的思考：当模型可通过调整不断拟合数据时，什么证据才能真正证伪一个理论（如惊奇度理论）？这指向了模型评估中需要更严格的独立测试集和理论边界界定。

en.tldr: This paper demonstrates, via fine-tuning neural language models, that surprisal can successfully predict human reading difficulty on garden-path sentences, providing an existence proof for a neural LM that unifies the explanation of garden-path effects and naturalistic reading times.
en.motivation: Current neural LMs based on surprisal theory can predict reading difficulty for simple sentences but severely underestimate processing difficulty on garden-path sentences requiring syntactic disambiguation, leading to the claim that such difficulty cannot be reduced to surprisal. This paper investigates whether it is possible to construct a neural LM that can explain garden-path effects via surprisal.
en.method: Instead of evaluating off-the-shelf LMs, we fine-tune neural LMs on garden-path sentences to better align surprisal-based reading-time estimates with actual human reading times. We evaluate the models on held-out garden-path items, their predictive power for human reading times on naturalistic corpora, and the preservation of their general LM capabilities.
en.result: Fine-tuned LMs do not overfit and successfully capture human reading slowdowns on held-out garden-path items; they even improve predictive power for human reading times on naturalistic corpora while preserving their general LM capabilities.
en.help_to_user: Provides a methodological case for researchers in computational psycholinguistics, demonstrating how targeted fine-tuning can align language models with human sentence processing and proving the possibility of explaining garden-path effects via surprisal theory.

idea_spark.idea: Construct a 'cognitively-aligned' model evaluation framework to systematically test model predictions against human behavior under different cognitive constraints (e.g., syntactic, semantic, pragmatic).
idea_spark.risk: Fine-tuning may cause the model to overfit to specific cognitive effect datasets, reducing its generality for language modeling; surprisal theory itself may not fully capture all cognitive mechanisms of sentence processing.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires reflection on the falsifiability of theories: when models can be continuously adjusted to fit data, what evidence can truly falsify a theory (like surprisal theory)? This points to the need for stricter independent test sets and clearer theoretical boundaries in model evaluation.

papers.title: Mutually-commuting von Neumann algebra models of quantum networks and violation of Bell-type inequalities
papers.summary: Employing mutually-commuting von Neumann algebras to represent the algebra of observables on quantum systems provides a framework for studying quantum information theory in systems with infinite degrees of freedom and quantum field theory, yielding many profound results that differ from non-relativistic quantum systems. In this paper, we establish a mutually-commuting von Neumann algebra model of quantum networks with arbitrary structures. We derive Bell-type inequalities on this model, and determine various bounds for Bell-type inequalities based on the structure of underline von Neumann algebras, and identify the algebraic structural conditions required for their violation. The conditions on the algebraic structure of observables for maximal violation of Bell-type inequalities, which we discovered in the context of von Neumann algebra models, can in turn guide the search for measurements in the non-relativistic setting.

ai.tldr: 本文建立了任意结构量子网络的互易冯·诺依曼代数模型，推导了贝尔型不等式，确定了其违反的代数结构条件，并将结果反哺非相对论性测量搜索。
ai.motivation: 利用互易冯·诺依曼代数表示可观测量代数，为研究无限自由度系统和量子场论中的量子信息理论提供了框架，但需要建立适用于任意网络结构的模型并探索贝尔型不等式的违反条件。
ai.method: 构建任意结构的互易冯·诺依曼代数量子网络模型，在其上推导贝尔型不等式，根据底层代数结构确定不等式界限，并找出违反不等式所需的代数结构条件。
ai.result: 建立了通用量子网络代数模型，得到了贝尔型不等式的多种界限，发现了最大违反贝尔型不等式所需的可观测量代数结构条件，这些条件可指导非相对论性场景中的测量选择。
ai.help_to_user: 为无限自由度量子系统的信息理论研究提供了严格的代数框架，将贝尔非定域性的分析从有限系统推广到代数量子理论范畴。

idea_spark.idea: 将网络拓扑编码为冯·诺依曼代数的互易关系，从而用代数约束刻画分布式量子关联。
idea_spark.risk: 模型高度抽象，需进一步与具体物理实现（如量子光学实验）建立对应。
idea_spark.inspiration: 代数结构条件可能启发新型贝尔实验方案或量子网络协议的安全性分析。
