en.tldr: This paper establishes a mutually-commuting von Neumann algebra model for quantum networks of arbitrary structure, derives Bell-type inequalities, identifies algebraic conditions for their violation, and applies these insights to guide measurement choices in non-relativistic settings.
en.motivation: Using mutually-commuting von Neumann algebras to represent observable algebras provides a framework for quantum information theory in systems with infinite degrees of freedom and quantum field theory, but requires models for arbitrary network structures and an understanding of conditions for violating Bell-type inequalities.
en.method: Construct a mutually-commuting von Neumann algebra model for quantum networks with arbitrary structure, derive Bell-type inequalities on this model, determine bounds based on the underlying algebra structure, and identify the algebraic conditions required for violation.
en.result: Developed a general algebraic model for quantum networks, obtained various bounds for Bell-type inequalities, discovered algebraic structural conditions on observables for maximal violation of Bell-type inequalities, which can guide the search for measurements in non-relativistic quantum settings.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous algebraic framework for studying quantum information in infinite-degree-of-freedom systems, extending Bell nonlocality analysis from finite systems to algebraic quantum theory.

idea_spark.idea: Encode network topology into the commuting relations of von Neumann algebras, using algebraic constraints to characterize distributed quantum correlations.
idea_spark.risk: The model is highly abstract and requires further mapping to specific physical implementations (e.g., quantum optics experiments).
idea_spark.inspiration: Algebraic structural conditions may inspire new Bell experiment designs or security analysis for quantum network protocols.

papers.title: Forecast-Enhanced Lyapunov Optimization for Real-Time EV Charging Scheduling
papers.summary: Electric vehicles (EVs) play a vital role in achieving carbon neutrality. Various approaches have been developed for online optimal EV charging scheduling to maximize their environmental and economic benefits. Among them, Lyapunov optimization has gained wide adoption due to its ease of implementation, no need for predictions, and rigorous performance guarantees. However, this prediction-free nature also limits the performance of Lyapunov optimization, as it cannot fully leverage the relatively accurate short-term forecasts often available in practice. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes a forecast-enhanced Lyapunov optimization method for real-time EV charging scheduling. Specifically, we design novel virtual queues and embed the traditional Lyapunov optimization within a receding horizon control framework to incorporate short-term predictions. The proposed algorithm is further extended by introducing heterogeneous penalty parameters to reduce the optimality gap. We prove that the proposed algorithm achieves bounded charging delay and a bounded optimality gap between online and offline solutions, both depending on the prediction window length. Numerical experiments demonstrate that the proposed method reduces operational costs compared to the traditional prediction-free Lyapunov optimization algorithm, while still satisfying all charging requirements.

ai.tldr: 提出一种融合短期预测的Lyapunov优化方法，用于实时电动汽车充电调度，在保证充电延迟有界的同时降低运营成本。
ai.motivation: 传统Lyapunov优化无需预测、易于实现，但无法利用实践中可获得的较准确短期预测，限制了性能提升。
ai.method: 设计新型虚拟队列，将传统Lyapunov优化嵌入滚动时域控制框架以纳入短期预测，并引入异质惩罚参数减小最优性间隙。
ai.result: 理论证明充电延迟和在线-离线最优性间隙均有界（取决于预测窗口长度）；实验表明相比无预测方法降低了运营成本，且满足所有充电需求。
ai.help_to_user: 为实时能源调度提供了可融合预测信息的优化框架，平衡了理论保证与实际性能。

idea_spark.idea: 在保证算法鲁棒性的前提下，通过设计辅助变量（如虚拟队列）和混合控制架构（滚动时域）引入预测信息，提升在线决策质量。
idea_spark.risk: 预测误差可能导致虚拟队列不稳定，需严格证明有界性；异质参数设计需依赖问题结构先验。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发思考：如何在其他无预测在线算法（如在线凸优化、强化学习）中安全地嵌入外部预测模块，以兼顾鲁棒性与性能。

en.tldr: Proposes a forecast-enhanced Lyapunov optimization method for real-time EV charging scheduling, reducing operational costs while guaranteeing bounded charging delay.
en.motivation: Traditional Lyapunov optimization is prediction-free and easy to implement, but cannot leverage relatively accurate short-term forecasts available in practice, limiting performance.
en.method: Designs novel virtual queues and embeds traditional Lyapunov optimization within a receding horizon control framework to incorporate short-term predictions, with heterogeneous penalty parameters introduced to reduce the optimality gap.
en.result: Theoretically proves bounded charging delay and bounded optimality gap between online and offline solutions (depending on prediction window length); experiments show reduced operational costs compared to prediction-free Lyapunov optimization while satisfying all charging requirements.
en.help_to_user: Provides an optimization framework that incorporates forecast information for real-time energy scheduling, balancing theoretical guarantees and practical performance.

idea_spark.idea: Enhance online decision quality by incorporating forecast information through auxiliary variables (e.g., virtual queues) and hybrid control architectures (receding horizon) while maintaining algorithmic robustness.
idea_spark.risk: Prediction errors may destabilize virtual queues, requiring rigorous boundedness proofs; heterogeneous parameter design relies on prior knowledge of problem structure.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires consideration of how to safely embed external forecast modules into other prediction-free online algorithms (e.g., online convex optimization, reinforcement learning) to balance robustness and performance.

papers.title: C-GenReg: Training-Free 3D Point Cloud Registration by Multi-View-Consistent Geometry-to-Image Generation with Probabilistic Modalities Fusion
papers.summary: We introduce C-GenReg, a training-free framework for 3D point cloud registration that leverages the complementary strengths of world-scale generative priors and registration-oriented Vision Foundation Models (VFMs). Current learning-based 3D point cloud registration methods struggle to generalize across sensing modalities, sampling differences, and environments. Hence, C-GenReg augments the geometric point cloud registration branch by transferring the matching problem into an auxiliary image domain, where VFMs excel, using a World Foundation Model to synthesize multi-view-consistent RGB representations from the input geometry. This generative transfer, preserves spatial coherence across source and target views without any fine-tuning. From these generated views, a VFM pretrained for finding dense correspondences extracts matches. The resulting pixel correspondences are lifted back to 3D via the original depth maps. To further enhance robustness, we introduce a "Match-then-Fuse" probabilistic cold-fusion scheme that combines two independent correspondence posteriors, that of the generated-RGB branch with that of the raw geometric branch. This principled fusion preserves each modality inductive bias and provides calibrated confidence without any additional learning. C-GenReg is zero-shot and plug-and-play: all modules are pretrained and operate without fine-tuning. Extensive experiments on indoor (3DMatch, ScanNet) and outdoor (Waymo) benchmarks demonstrate strong zero-shot performance and superior cross-domain generalization. For the first time, we demonstrate a generative registration framework that operates successfully on real outdoor LiDAR data, where no imagery data is available.

ai.tldr: C-GenReg是一个无需训练的3D点云配准框架，通过多视角一致的几何到图像生成与概率模态融合，实现零样本跨域泛化。
ai.motivation: 现有基于学习的3D点云配准方法难以泛化到不同传感模态、采样差异和环境。
ai.method: 利用世界基础模型从输入几何生成多视角一致的RGB表示，通过视觉基础模型提取密集对应，再通过原始深度图映射回3D，最后与原始几何分支进行概率冷融合。
ai.result: 在室内（3DMatch、ScanNet）和室外（Waymo）基准测试中展现出强大的零样本性能和卓越的跨域泛化能力，首次在无图像数据的真实室外LiDAR数据上成功运行。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种无需训练、即插即用的3D点云配准解决方案，特别适用于跨模态、跨环境的零样本泛化场景。

idea_spark.idea: 通过生成多视角一致的RGB表示作为中间桥梁，结合概率融合保留各模态归纳偏置。
idea_spark.risk: 生成图像的质量和一致性对最终配准精度有直接影响，在极端几何条件下可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 为其他3D视觉任务（如分割、检测）的零样本泛化提供了生成式跨模态表示的新范式。

en.tldr: C-GenReg is a training-free 3D point cloud registration framework that achieves zero-shot cross-domain generalization through multi-view-consistent geometry-to-image generation with probabilistic modalities fusion.
en.motivation: Current learning-based 3D point cloud registration methods struggle to generalize across sensing modalities, sampling differences, and environments.
en.method: Uses a World Foundation Model to synthesize multi-view-consistent RGB representations from input geometry, extracts matches via a Vision Foundation Model, lifts correspondences back to 3D via original depth maps, and fuses with the raw geometric branch through a probabilistic cold-fusion scheme.
en.result: Demonstrates strong zero-shot performance and superior cross-domain generalization on indoor (3DMatch, ScanNet) and outdoor (Waymo) benchmarks, successfully operating on real outdoor LiDAR data without imagery for the first time.
en.help_to_user: Provides a plug-and-play, training-free solution for 3D point cloud registration, particularly valuable for zero-shot generalization across modalities and environments.

idea_spark.idea: Using generated multi-view-consistent RGB representations as an intermediate bridge, combined with probabilistic fusion to preserve each modality's inductive bias.
idea_spark.risk: Registration accuracy depends heavily on the quality and consistency of generated images, which may fail under extreme geometric conditions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Offers a new generative cross-modal representation paradigm for zero-shot generalization in other 3D vision tasks like segmentation and detection.

papers.title: Morita equivalence for quantum graphs
papers.summary: We introduce an operator-algebraic framework for Morita equivalence of quantum graphs based on $Δ$-equivalence of operator systems introduced by Eleftherakis, Kakariadis and Todorov. Adopting the perspective of Weaver, we view quantum graphs as quantum relations, that is, operator systems endowed with a bimodule structure over the commutant of a von Neumann algebra. Within this framework, we show that two irreducibly acting quantum graphs are Morita equivalent if and only if they are both full pullbacks of a common quantum graph. This extends a result of Eleftherakis, Kakariadis and Todorov for graph operator systems to the quantum graph setting. In passing we construct a true-twin reduction analogue for an irreducibly acting quantum graph. We further characterise the case where we have simultaneous TRO-equivalence of the quantum graphs and their associated algebras, thus giving a second, stronger notion of Morita equivalence. In the special case of noncommutative graphs, corresponding to the zero-error quantum communication setting, the two notions coincide and we obtain a characterisation in terms of strong co-homomorphisms of noncommutative graphs. Finally, we show that connectivity, the independence number, Shannon capacity, quantum complexity and subcomplexity, Haemers bound, and the Lovász number are invariant under Morita equivalence.

ai.tldr: 本文为量子图引入了基于算子系统的Morita等价算子代数框架，证明了两个不可约量子图Morita等价当且仅当它们是同一量子图的满拉回，并证明了连通性、独立数等图论量在Morita等价下不变。
ai.motivation: 将Eleftherakis等人关于图算子系统的Δ-等价结果推广到量子图设置，建立量子图的Morita等价理论框架。
ai.method: 采用Weaver的量子关系视角，将量子图视为具有双模结构的算子系统，基于算子代数框架分析Morita等价条件。
ai.result: 证明了不可约量子图Morita等价的充要条件；构造了真孪生约化类似物；在非交换图情形下，通过强共同态刻画等价性；证明了多个图论不变量在Morita等价下保持不变。
ai.help_to_user: 为量子图的分类和比较提供了严格的代数框架，将经典图论概念推广到量子设置，并建立了与零误差量子通信的联系。

idea_spark.idea: 将Morita等价概念从代数几何移植到量子图论，建立不同量子图之间的结构对应关系。
idea_spark.risk: 框架依赖于特定的算子代数假设，可能不适用于所有类型的量子图变体。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将经典图论不变量系统性地量子化，并研究它们在各种等价关系下的行为。

en.tldr: This paper introduces an operator-algebraic framework for Morita equivalence of quantum graphs, proving that two irreducibly acting quantum graphs are Morita equivalent if and only if they are full pullbacks of a common quantum graph, and showing that graph-theoretic quantities like connectivity and independence number are invariant under Morita equivalence.
en.motivation: To extend the Δ-equivalence results of Eleftherakis et al. for graph operator systems to the quantum graph setting, establishing a theoretical framework for Morita equivalence of quantum graphs.
en.method: Adopting Weaver's perspective of quantum relations, viewing quantum graphs as operator systems with bimodule structure, and analyzing Morita equivalence conditions within an operator-algebraic framework.
en.result: Characterized Morita equivalence for irreducibly acting quantum graphs; constructed a true-twin reduction analogue; characterized equivalence via strong co-homomorphisms in the noncommutative graph case; proved invariance of multiple graph invariants under Morita equivalence.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous algebraic framework for classifying and comparing quantum graphs, extends classical graph concepts to quantum settings, and establishes connections with zero-error quantum communication.

idea_spark.idea: Transplanting Morita equivalence concepts from algebraic geometry to quantum graph theory to establish structural correspondences between different quantum graphs.
idea_spark.risk: The framework relies on specific operator-algebraic assumptions that may not apply to all variants of quantum graphs.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires systematic quantization of classical graph invariants and study of their behavior under various equivalence relations.

papers.title: Classification of systolic murmurs in heart sounds using multiresolution complex Gabor dictionary and vision transformer
papers.summary: Systolic murmurs are extra heart sounds that occur during the contraction phase of the cardiac cycle, often indicating heart abnormalities caused by turbulent blood flow. Their intensity, pitch, and quality vary, requiring precise identification for the accurate diagnosis of cardiac disorders. This study presents an automatic classification system for systolic murmurs using a feature extraction module, followed by a classification model. The feature extraction module employs complex orthogonal matching pursuit to project single or multiple murmur segments onto a redundant dictionary composed of multiresolution complex Gabor basis functions (GBFs). The resulting projection weights are split and reshaped into variable-resolution time--frequency feature matrices. Processing multiple segments of a single recording using a shared dictionary mitigates murmur variability. This is achieved by learning the weights for each segment while enforcing that they correspond to the same set of basis functions in the dictionary, promoting consistent time--frequency feature matrices. The classification model is built based on a vision transformer to process multiple input matrices of different resolutions by passing each through a convolutional neural network for patch tokenization. All embedding tokens are then concatenated to form a matrix and forwarded to an encoder layer that includes multihead attention, residual connections, and a convolutional network with a kernel size of one. This integration of multiresolution feature extraction with transformer-based feature classification enhances the accuracy and reliability of heart murmur identification. An experimental analysis of four types of systolic murmurs from the CirCor DigiScope dataset demonstrates the effectiveness of the system, achieving a classification accuracy of $95.96\%$.

ai.tldr: 本研究提出了一种基于多分辨率复Gabor字典和视觉Transformer的心音收缩期杂音自动分类系统，在CirCor DigiScope数据集上达到95.96%的分类准确率。
ai.motivation: 收缩期杂音是心脏收缩期出现的异常心音，其强度、音调和性质多变，精确识别对心脏疾病诊断至关重要。传统方法难以有效处理杂音变异性。
ai.method: 特征提取模块使用复正交匹配追踪将杂音频段投影到多分辨率复Gabor基函数构成的冗余字典上，生成变分辨率时频特征矩阵。分类模型采用视觉Transformer处理多分辨率输入矩阵，通过CNN进行块标记化，再经编码器层（多头注意力、残差连接、卷积网络）进行分类。
ai.result: 在CirCor DigiScope数据集的四类收缩期杂音分类任务中，系统达到95.96%的分类准确率，证明了多分辨率特征提取与Transformer分类结合的有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 为心脏杂音自动诊断提供了高精度的端到端解决方案，特别通过共享字典处理多段信号来减少杂音变异性影响。

idea_spark.idea: 将信号投影到共享字典生成一致性特征矩阵，再用视觉Transformer融合多分辨率信息
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于特定数据集和杂音类型，泛化到其他心音异常或噪声环境需验证
idea_spark.inspiration: 该框架可迁移至其他生物信号（如脑电、肌电）的时变模式分类任务

en.tldr: This study presents an automatic systolic murmur classification system using multiresolution complex Gabor dictionary and vision transformer, achieving 95.96% accuracy on the CirCor DigiScope dataset.
en.motivation: Systolic murmurs are extra heart sounds with variable intensity, pitch, and quality, requiring precise identification for cardiac disorder diagnosis. Traditional methods struggle with murmur variability.
en.method: Feature extraction uses complex orthogonal matching pursuit to project murmur segments onto a redundant dictionary of multiresolution complex Gabor basis functions, producing variable-resolution time-frequency feature matrices. Classification employs a vision transformer: each matrix is tokenized via CNN, embeddings are concatenated, and processed through an encoder layer with multihead attention, residual connections, and 1D convolution.
en.result: The system achieves 95.96% classification accuracy for four types of systolic murmurs from the CirCor DigiScope dataset, demonstrating effectiveness of integrating multiresolution feature extraction with transformer-based classification.
en.help_to_user: Provides a high-accuracy end-to-end solution for automatic heart murmur diagnosis, particularly by using a shared dictionary across multiple segments to mitigate murmur variability.

idea_spark.idea: Project signals onto a shared dictionary to generate consistent feature matrices, then fuse multiresolution information using vision transformer
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on specific dataset and murmur types; generalization to other cardiac abnormalities or noisy environments requires validation
idea_spark.inspiration: The framework could be transferred to other biosignal (e.g., EEG, EMG) classification tasks with time-varying patterns

papers.title: Brain-Inspired Capture: Evidence-Driven Neuromimetic Perceptual Simulation for Visual Decoding
papers.summary: Visual decoding of neurophysiological signals is a critical challenge for brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) and computational neuroscience. However, current approaches are often constrained by the systematic and stochastic gaps between neural and visual modalities, largely neglecting the intrinsic computational mechanisms of the Human Visual System (HVS). To address this, we propose Brain-Inspired Capture (BI-Cap), a neuromimetic perceptual simulation paradigm that aligns these modalities by emulating HVS processing. Specifically, we construct a neuromimetic pipeline comprising four biologically plausible dynamic and static transformations, coupled with Mutual Information (MI)-guided dynamic blur regulation to simulate adaptive visual processing. Furthermore, to mitigate the inherent non-stationarity of neural activity, we introduce an evidence-driven latent space representation. This formulation explicitly models uncertainty, thereby ensuring robust neural embeddings. Extensive evaluations on zero-shot brain-to-image retrieval across two public benchmarks demonstrate that BI-Cap substantially outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving relative gains of 9.2\% and 8.0\%, respectively. We have released the source code on GitHub through the link https://github.com/flysnow1024/BI-Cap.

ai.tldr: 提出BI-Cap，一种受大脑启发的神经拟态感知模拟范式，通过模拟人类视觉系统处理来对齐神经与视觉模态，显著提升零样本脑到图像检索性能。
ai.motivation: 当前视觉解码方法受限于神经与视觉模态间的系统性和随机性差距，且常忽略人类视觉系统的内在计算机制。
ai.method: 构建包含四个生物合理动态与静态变换的神经拟态流水线，结合互信息引导的动态模糊调节来模拟自适应视觉处理，并引入证据驱动的潜在空间表示以建模不确定性。
ai.result: 在两个公共基准测试的零样本脑到图像检索任务中，BI-Cap显著优于现有最先进方法，分别实现9.2%和8.0%的相对性能提升。
ai.help_to_user: 为脑机接口和计算神经科学领域提供了一种更贴近生物视觉机制、鲁棒性更强的视觉解码新范式。

idea_spark.idea: 将人类视觉系统的自适应处理机制（如动态模糊调节）显式建模到解码流程中。
idea_spark.risk: 生物合理性假设可能过于简化，且方法依赖于特定神经数据集的特征。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将更多感知系统的分层、动态特性引入跨模态对齐任务，如听觉或触觉解码。

zh.tldr: 提出BI-Cap，一种受大脑启发的神经拟态感知模拟范式，通过模拟人类视觉系统处理来对齐神经与视觉模态，显著提升零样本脑到图像检索性能。
zh.motivation: 当前视觉解码方法受限于神经与视觉模态间的系统性和随机性差距，且常忽略人类视觉系统的内在计算机制。
zh.method: 构建包含四个生物合理动态与静态变换的神经拟态流水线，结合互信息引导的动态模糊调节来模拟自适应视觉处理，并引入证据驱动的潜在空间表示以建模不确定性。
zh.result: 在两个公共基准测试的零样本脑到图像检索任务中，BI-Cap显著优于现有最先进方法，分别实现9.2%和8.0%的相对性能提升。
zh.help_to_user: 为脑机接口和计算神经科学领域提供了一种更贴近生物视觉机制、鲁棒性更强的视觉解码新范式。

idea_spark.idea: 将人类视觉系统的自适应处理机制（如动态模糊调节）显式建模到解码流程中。
idea_spark.risk: 生物合理性假设可能过于简化，且方法依赖于特定神经数据集的特征。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将更多感知系统的分层、动态特性引入跨模态对齐任务，如听觉或触觉解码。

en.tldr: Proposes BI-Cap, a brain-inspired neuromimetic perceptual simulation paradigm that aligns neural and visual modalities by emulating Human Visual System processing, significantly improving zero-shot brain-to-image retrieval performance.
en.motivation: Current visual decoding approaches are constrained by systematic and stochastic gaps between neural and visual modalities, often neglecting the intrinsic computational mechanisms of the Human Visual System.
en.method: Constructs a neuromimetic pipeline with four biologically plausible dynamic and static transformations, coupled with Mutual Information-guided dynamic blur regulation to simulate adaptive visual processing, and introduces an evidence-driven latent space representation to model uncertainty.
en.result: Extensive evaluations on zero-shot brain-to-image retrieval across two public benchmarks show BI-Cap substantially outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving relative gains of 9.2% and 8.0%, respectively.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new paradigm for visual decoding in BCIs and computational neuroscience that is more aligned with biological visual mechanisms and offers improved robustness.

idea_spark.idea: Explicitly modeling adaptive processing mechanisms of the Human Visual System (e.g., dynamic blur regulation) into the decoding pipeline.
idea_spark.risk: Biological plausibility assumptions might be oversimplified, and the method relies on features from specific neural datasets.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires incorporating hierarchical, dynamic properties of other perceptual systems into cross-modal alignment tasks, such as auditory or tactile decoding.

papers.title: On-Trip Matching and Pricing for Shared Rides
papers.summary: Although shared rides have the potential to increase vehicle utilization and reduce congestion and emissions, these benefits depend heavily on ridesharing platforms' ability to match riders effectively. As such, shared rides have seen limited success outside of dense urban areas -- the sparse outskirts of greater metropolitan areas remain underserved. In the literature, the dominant matching model involves collecting rider requests in a batch interval and solving a non-bipartite matching problem on the requests. However, this model neglects the ability of a rider to be matched to a future arriving rider even after she is initially dispatched solo; namely, matching is only modeled pre-trip, and the value of on-trip matching is not explicitly accounted for. We develop a dynamic, stochastic matching model, where the platform makes both pre-trip and on-trip matching decisions, and contrast the behavior of each phase of matching. Using both synthetic and real-world data from Chicago, we find that whereas pre-trip matching is well-suited to dense downtown areas with concentrated demand, on-trip matching is critical in sparser outskirts where demand is spatially dispersed, and manages a tradeoff between matching opportunity and value. We also embed the matching model in an outer pricing optimization problem to study the interaction of matching with pricing, and find that the addition of on-trip matching increases profitability and efficiency for the platform and lowers prices for riders. These effects are particularly pronounced in the sparse outskirts, where operating shared rides -- even providing access to any form of transportation -- has historically been most challenging.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一种动态随机匹配模型，支持行程前和行程中匹配，解决了稀疏郊区需求分散的共享出行匹配难题，并通过定价优化提升了平台盈利和乘客体验。
ai.motivation: 现有共享出行匹配模型主要在行程前进行批量匹配，忽略了行程中匹配未来乘客的潜力，导致在需求空间分散的郊区区域服务效果不佳。
ai.method: 开发了一个动态随机匹配模型，平台可做出行程前和行程中两阶段的匹配决策，并将该模型嵌入外层定价优化问题中，使用合成数据和芝加哥真实数据进行验证。
ai.result: 行程前匹配适合需求密集的市中心，行程中匹配对需求分散的郊区至关重要，能在匹配机会与价值间取得平衡；加入行程中匹配可提高平台盈利和效率，降低乘客价格，在郊区效果尤为显著。
ai.help_to_user: 为共享出行平台提供了在低密度区域运营的有效匹配与定价策略框架。

idea_spark.idea: 在移动资源（如车辆）执行任务过程中，持续探测并匹配新出现的需求点。
idea_spark.risk: 模型对需求预测准确性、实时计算延迟及乘客对行程中匹配的接受度较为敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发于服务系统在资源已启程后仍可优化后续任务分配的普遍现象。

en.tldr: This paper proposes a dynamic stochastic matching model supporting both pre-trip and on-trip matching, addressing the shared ride matching challenge in sparse suburbs with dispersed demand, and enhances platform profitability and rider experience through pricing optimization.
en.motivation: Existing shared ride matching models primarily perform batch matching pre-trip, neglecting the potential to match with future riders during a trip, resulting in poor service performance in suburban areas with spatially dispersed demand.
en.method: Developed a dynamic stochastic matching model where the platform makes both pre-trip and on-trip matching decisions, embedded this model within an outer pricing optimization problem, and validated it using synthetic data and real-world data from Chicago.
en.result: Pre-trip matching suits dense downtown areas, while on-trip matching is critical for sparse outskirts, balancing matching opportunity and value; incorporating on-trip matching increases platform profitability and efficiency, lowers rider prices, with effects most pronounced in suburbs.
en.help_to_user: Provides an effective matching and pricing strategy framework for ridesharing platforms operating in low-density areas.

idea_spark.idea: Continuously probe and match newly emerging demand points while a mobile resource (e.g., a vehicle) is already executing a task.
idea_spark.risk: The model is sensitive to demand forecast accuracy, real-time computational latency, and rider acceptance of on-trip matching.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the general phenomenon in service systems where resources can be further optimized after departure.

papers.title: Enabling Lie-Algebraic Classical Simulation beyond Free Fermions
papers.summary: Efficient classical simulation has matured to a critical component of the quantum computing stack, driving hardware validation, algorithm design, and the study of structured quantum dynamics. Lie-algebraic simulation ($\mathfrak{g}$-sim) is a compelling approach: it replaces exponentially large Hilbert-space evolution by dynamics in a reduced adjoint space whose dimension is set by the dynamical Lie algebra (DLA) of the circuit, enabling efficient simulation whenever the DLA grows only polynomially with system size. In practice, however, existing applications of $\mathfrak{g}$-sim have been confined to free-fermionic (matchgate) regimes, and it has been unclear how to extend the paradigm to other structured circuits whose generators may have large Pauli expansions. Here we enable Lie-algebraic classical simulation beyond free fermions by identifying additional non-trivial families of polynomial-dimensional DLAs and introducing symmetry-adapted basis representations that make the adjoint space mapping tractable. In particular, we develop an explicit Pauli orbit basis for permutation-equivariant dynamics, supporting cubic-dimensional algebras despite exponential Pauli support, and a subspace-adapted (modified) generalized Gell-Mann basis for bounded Hamming-weight ($U(1)$-equivariant) dynamics, yielding polynomial costs on fixed excitation sectors. Together with streamlined routines for free-fermionic Pauli algebras and translation-invariant variants thereof, these constructions significantly broaden the practical scope of $\mathfrak{g}$-sim as a unifying simulation tool for structured quantum dynamics. Numerical benchmarks confirm favorable preprocessing scaling and validate large-scale proof-of-concept simulations.

ai.tldr: 本文扩展了李代数经典模拟（𝔤-sim）的应用范围，使其超越自由费米子体系，通过识别新的多项式维数动态李代数家族并引入对称性适应的基表示，实现了对具有大Pauli展开的结构化量子电路的高效模拟。
ai.motivation: 现有的李代数经典模拟方法主要局限于自由费米子（匹配门）体系，对于生成元具有大Pauli展开的其他结构化电路，如何扩展该模拟范式尚不明确。
ai.method: 识别了新的多项式维数动态李代数家族，并引入了两种对称性适应的基表示：针对置换等变动力学的显式Pauli轨道基，以及针对有界汉明权重（U(1)-等变）动力学的子空间适应广义Gell-Mann基。
ai.result: 这些构造显著拓宽了𝔤-sim作为结构化量子动力学统一模拟工具的实际适用范围。数值基准测试证实了有利的预处理缩放，并验证了大规模概念验证模拟。
ai.help_to_user: 为模拟具有特定对称性（如置换对称性、粒子数守恒）的量子电路动力学提供了新的高效经典算法框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将特定对称性（如置换、U(1)）编码到基的构造中，以压缩表示并简化动力学计算。
idea_spark.risk: 对新识别代数族的普适性及对更复杂对称性的扩展能力有待进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将群表示论与李代数方法结合，用于其他具有组合对称性的量子多体系统的模拟。

en.tldr: This work extends Lie-algebraic classical simulation (𝔤-sim) beyond free-fermionic regimes by identifying new families of polynomial-dimensional dynamical Lie algebras and introducing symmetry-adapted basis representations, enabling efficient simulation of structured quantum circuits with large Pauli expansions.
en.motivation: Existing applications of 𝔤-sim have been confined to free-fermionic (matchgate) regimes, and it has been unclear how to extend the paradigm to other structured circuits whose generators may have large Pauli expansions.
en.method: Identified additional non-trivial families of polynomial-dimensional DLAs and introduced symmetry-adapted basis representations: an explicit Pauli orbit basis for permutation-equivariant dynamics and a subspace-adapted generalized Gell-Mann basis for bounded Hamming-weight (U(1)-equivariant) dynamics.
en.result: These constructions significantly broaden the practical scope of 𝔤-sim as a unifying simulation tool for structured quantum dynamics. Numerical benchmarks confirm favorable preprocessing scaling and validate large-scale proof-of-concept simulations.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new efficient classical algorithmic framework for simulating the dynamics of quantum circuits with specific symmetries (e.g., permutation symmetry, particle number conservation).

idea_spark.idea: Encoding specific symmetries (e.g., permutation, U(1)) into the construction of bases to compress representation and simplify dynamics computation.
idea_spark.risk: The generality of the newly identified algebra families and their extensibility to more complex symmetries require further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining group representation theory with Lie-algebraic methods for simulating other quantum many-body systems with combinatorial symmetries.

papers.title: AstroSURE: Learning to Remove Noise from Astronomical Images Without Ground Truth Data
papers.summary: In astronomical imaging, the low photon count of exposures necessitates extensive post-processing steps, including contamination removal and denoising. This paper evaluates deep-learning denoising methods that can be trained without clean ground-truth images and assesses their utility for detection11 oriented analysis of astronomical data. We adapt and compare Noise2Noise, Stein's Unbiased Risk Estimator, and blind-spot-based methods using synthetic data and real observations from the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) and the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope (CFHT). Performance is evaluated using object-detection metrics, including correct detection rate and false alarm rate, together with image-based metrics and pixel-distribution diagnostics. The results show that these methods can improve faint-source detectability relative to the original noisy images, with encouraging gains on HST data after domain-consistent initialization, while transfer to CFHT data is more limited, highlighting the importance of instrument/domain similarity for unsupervised adaptation.

ai.tldr: 本文评估了无需干净地面真值图像即可训练的深度学习去噪方法，并测试了它们在天文数据检测分析中的实用性。
ai.motivation: 天文成像中光子计数低，需要复杂的后处理步骤，包括去噪。传统方法依赖干净地面真值数据，但实际中难以获取。
ai.method: 采用并比较了Noise2Noise、Stein无偏风险估计器和盲点方法，使用合成数据以及哈勃太空望远镜和加拿大-法国-夏威夷望远镜的真实观测数据进行评估。
ai.result: 这些方法能提高微弱天体的可检测性，在哈勃数据上经过域一致初始化后效果显著，但在CFHT数据上迁移效果有限，凸显了仪器/域相似性对无监督适应的重要性。
ai.help_to_user: 为天文图像处理提供了无需干净地面真值的去噪解决方案，并评估了不同无监督方法在真实观测数据上的性能。

idea_spark.idea: 利用域一致初始化提升跨仪器数据迁移效果，或结合合成数据与真实数据混合训练。
idea_spark.risk: 仪器差异可能导致性能下降，需谨慎处理域适应问题。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在缺乏地面真值的数据领域探索无监督或自监督去噪技术。

en.tldr: This paper evaluates deep-learning denoising methods trainable without clean ground-truth images and assesses their utility for detection-oriented analysis of astronomical data.
en.motivation: Low photon count in astronomical imaging necessitates extensive post-processing, including denoising. Traditional methods rely on clean ground truth, which is often unavailable in practice.
en.method: Adapts and compares Noise2Noise, Stein's Unbiased Risk Estimator, and blind-spot-based methods using synthetic data and real observations from HST and CFHT.
en.result: These methods improve faint-source detectability relative to noisy images, with encouraging gains on HST data after domain-consistent initialization, while transfer to CFHT data is more limited, highlighting the importance of instrument/domain similarity.
en.help_to_user: Provides denoising solutions for astronomical images without requiring clean ground truth and evaluates the performance of different unsupervised methods on real observational data.

idea_spark.idea: Use domain-consistent initialization to enhance cross-instrument data transfer, or combine synthetic and real data for hybrid training.
idea_spark.risk: Instrument differences may lead to performance degradation, requiring careful handling of domain adaptation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires exploration of unsupervised or self-supervised denoising techniques in data domains lacking ground truth.

papers.title: From Program Slices to Causal Clarity: Evaluating Faithful, Actionable LLM-Generated Failure Explanations via Context Partitioning and LLM-as-a-Judge
papers.summary: Large language model (LLM)-based debugging systems can generate failure explanations, but these explanations may be incomplete or incorrect. Misleading explanations are harmful for downstream tasks (e.g., bug triage, bug fixing). We investigate how explanation quality is affected by various LLM context configurations. Existing work predominantly treats LLM-generated failure explanations as an ad hoc by-product of debugging or repair workflows, using generic prompting over undifferentiated artifacts such as code, tests, and error messages rather than targeting explanations as a first-class output with dedicated quality assessment. Consequently, existing approaches provide limited support for assessing whether these explanations capture the underlying fault-error-failure mechanism and for actionable next steps, and most techniques instead prioritize task success (e.g., patch correctness or review quality) over the explicit causal explanation quality. We systematically vary the debugging information to study how distinct context compositions affect the quality of LLM-generated failure explanations. Across 93 context configurations on real bugs and three economically viable models (gpt-5-mini, DeepSeek-V3.2, and Grok-4.1-fast), we evaluate explanations with six criteria and validate the LLM-as-a-judge scores against human ratings in a user study. Our results indicate that explanation quality is causally affected by context composition. Evidence-rich, failure-specific artifacts improve causal and action-oriented quality, whereas overly large contexts tend to yield vague explanations. Higher explanation-score quartiles are associated with higher downstream repair pass rates and, for some models, with fixes that are closer to the reference minimal fixes. In contrast, low-score quartiles can even underperform the no-explanation baseline. Reproduction package is publicly available.

ai.tldr: 本研究系统评估了不同上下文配置如何影响LLM生成的故障解释质量，发现证据丰富、故障特定的上下文能提升解释的因果性和可操作性，而过大上下文则导致解释模糊。
ai.motivation: 现有LLM调试系统生成的故障解释可能不完整或不正确，误导性解释对下游任务（如缺陷分类、修复）有害，且缺乏对解释质量作为首要输出的专门评估。
ai.method: 在真实缺陷上系统性地变化调试信息（93种上下文配置），使用三种经济可行模型（gpt-5-mini、DeepSeek-V3.2、Grok-4.1-fast），通过六项标准评估解释，并用LLM-as-a-Judge评分与人工评分进行验证。
ai.result: 解释质量受上下文组成因果影响。证据丰富、故障特定的工件能提升因果性和面向行动的质量；过大上下文易产生模糊解释。高解释分数四分位数与更高的下游修复通过率相关，且某些模型的修复更接近参考最小修复。
ai.help_to_user: 为开发者提供了优化LLM调试提示中上下文配置的实证依据，帮助生成更忠实、可操作的故障解释，从而提升调试效率。

idea_spark.idea: 构建动态上下文选择器，根据故障类型自动优化输入工件的组合与粒度。
idea_spark.risk: LLM-as-a-Judge的评分可能受模型偏见影响，需多模型交叉验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将解释质量与下游任务性能关联，为解释评估提供客观指标。

zh.motivation: 现有LLM调试系统生成的故障解释可能不完整或不正确，误导性解释对下游任务（如缺陷分类、修复）有害，且缺乏对解释质量作为首要输出的专门评估。
zh.method: 在真实缺陷上系统性地变化调试信息（93种上下文配置），使用三种经济可行模型（gpt-5-mini、DeepSeek-V3.2、Grok-4.1-fast），通过六项标准评估解释，并用LLM-as-a-Judge评分与人工评分进行验证。
zh.result: 解释质量受上下文组成因果影响。证据丰富、故障特定的工件能提升因果性和面向行动的质量；过大上下文易产生模糊解释。高解释分数四分位数与更高的下游修复通过率相关，且某些模型的修复更接近参考最小修复。
zh.help_to_user: 为开发者提供了优化LLM调试提示中上下文配置的实证依据，帮助生成更忠实、可操作的故障解释，从而提升调试效率。

idea_spark.idea: 构建动态上下文选择器，根据故障类型自动优化输入工件的组合与粒度。
idea_spark.risk: LLM-as-a-Judge的评分可能受模型偏见影响，需多模型交叉验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将解释质量与下游任务性能关联，为解释评估提供客观指标。

en.tldr: This study systematically evaluates how different context configurations affect the quality of LLM-generated failure explanations, finding that evidence-rich, failure-specific contexts improve causal and actionable quality, while overly large contexts yield vague explanations.
en.motivation: Existing LLM-based debugging systems may produce incomplete or incorrect failure explanations, which are harmful for downstream tasks (e.g., bug triage, fixing). Explanation quality is often treated as a by-product rather than a first-class output with dedicated assessment.
en.method: Systematically varied debugging information across 93 context configurations on real bugs using three economically viable models (gpt-5-mini, DeepSeek-V3.2, Grok-4.1-fast). Evaluated explanations with six criteria and validated LLM-as-a-Judge scores against human ratings.
en.result: Explanation quality is causally affected by context composition. Evidence-rich, failure-specific artifacts improve causal and action-oriented quality; overly large contexts tend to yield vague explanations. Higher explanation-score quartiles correlate with higher downstream repair pass rates and, for some models, fixes closer to minimal reference fixes.
en.help_to_user: Provides empirical guidance for optimizing context configurations in LLM debugging prompts to generate more faithful and actionable failure explanations, improving debugging efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Build a dynamic context selector that automatically optimizes the composition and granularity of input artifacts based on failure type.
idea_spark.risk: LLM-as-a-Judge scores may be influenced by model biases, requiring cross-model validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Linking explanation quality to downstream task performance provides objective metrics for explanation evaluation.

papers.title: Voronoi-guided Bilateral 2D Gaussian Splatting for Arbitrary-Scale Hyperspectral Image Super-Resolution
papers.summary: Most existing hyperspectral image super-resolution methods require modifications for different scales, limiting their flexibility in arbitrary-scale reconstruction. 2D Gaussian splatting provides a continuous representation that is compatible with arbitrary-scale super-resolution. Existing methods often rely on rasterization strategies, which may limit flexible spatial modeling. Extending them to hyperspectral image super-resolution remains challenging, as the task requires adaptive spatial reconstruction while preserving spectral fidelity. This paper proposes GaussianHSI, a Gaussian-Splatting-based framework for arbitrary-scale hyperspectral image super-resolution. We develop a Voronoi-Guided Bilateral 2D Gaussian Splatting for spatial reconstruction. After predicting a set of Gaussian functions to represent the input, it associates each target pixel with relevant Gaussian functions through Voronoi-guided selection. The target pixel is then reconstructed by aggregating the selected Gaussian functions with reference-aware bilateral weighting, which considers both geometric relevance and consistency with low-resolution features. We further introduce a Spectral Detail Enhancement module to improve spectral reconstruction. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of GaussianHSI over state-of-the-art methods for arbitrary-scale hyperspectral image super-resolution.

ai.tldr: 提出GaussianHSI框架，基于Voronoi引导的双边2D高斯泼溅实现任意尺度高光谱图像超分辨率，通过空间重建与光谱增强模块提升重建质量。
ai.motivation: 现有高光谱图像超分辨率方法通常需要针对不同尺度进行调整，缺乏任意尺度重建的灵活性；传统光栅化策略限制了空间建模能力，且同时保持空间与光谱保真度具有挑战。
ai.method: 使用2D高斯函数集合表示输入图像；通过Voronoi引导选择关联目标像素与相关高斯函数；采用参考感知双边加权聚合所选函数，兼顾几何相关性与低分辨率特征一致性；引入光谱细节增强模块改善光谱重建。
ai.result: 在基准数据集上的大量实验表明，GaussianHSI在任意尺度高光谱图像超分辨率任务上优于现有先进方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种无需针对不同尺度调整的灵活高光谱图像超分辨率框架，实现了连续表示与空间-光谱保真度的平衡。

idea_spark.idea: 将几何引导的局部关联与特征一致性加权结合，增强空间建模的适应性与保真度。
idea_spark.risk: 高斯函数预测与Voronoi划分的计算复杂度可能较高，影响实时性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将连续表示方法与自适应局部聚合策略结合，用于多模态或多尺度图像重建问题。

en.tldr: Proposes GaussianHSI, a Voronoi-guided bilateral 2D Gaussian splatting framework for arbitrary-scale hyperspectral image super-resolution, enhancing reconstruction through spatial modeling and spectral detail enhancement.
en.motivation: Existing hyperspectral image super-resolution methods often require modifications for different scales, lacking flexibility for arbitrary-scale reconstruction; rasterization strategies limit spatial modeling, and preserving both spatial and spectral fidelity is challenging.
en.method: Represents input with a set of 2D Gaussian functions; associates target pixels with relevant Gaussians via Voronoi-guided selection; reconstructs pixels by aggregating selected Gaussians with reference-aware bilateral weighting considering geometric relevance and low-resolution feature consistency; introduces a Spectral Detail Enhancement module for spectral reconstruction.
en.result: Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate GaussianHSI's superiority over state-of-the-art methods for arbitrary-scale hyperspectral image super-resolution.
en.help_to_user: Provides a flexible hyperspectral image super-resolution framework that does not require scale-specific modifications, balancing continuous representation with spatial-spectral fidelity.

idea_spark.idea: Combining geometry-guided local association with feature-consistency weighting to enhance adaptive spatial modeling and fidelity.
idea_spark.risk: Computational complexity of Gaussian prediction and Voronoi partitioning may be high, affecting real-time performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining continuous representation methods with adaptive local aggregation strategies for multimodal or multiscale image reconstruction problems.

papers.title: Understanding Human Actions through the Lens of Executable Models
papers.summary: Human-centred systems require an understanding of human actions in the physical world. Temporally extended sequences of actions are intentional and structured, yet existing methods for recognising what actions are performed often do not attempt to capture their structure, particularly how the actions are executed. This, however, is crucial for assessing the quality of the action's execution and its differences from other actions. To capture the internal mechanics of actions, we introduce a domain-specific language EXACT that represents human motions as underspecified motion programs, interpreted as reward-generating functions for zero-shot policy inference using forward-backwards representations. By leveraging the compositional nature of EXACT motion programs, we combine individual policies into an executable neuro-symbolic model that uses program structure for compositional modelling. We evaluate the utility of the proposed pipeline for creating executable action models by analysing motion-capture data to understand human actions, for the tasks of human action segmentation and action anomaly detection. Our results show that the use of executable action models improves data efficiency and captures intuitive relationships between actions compared with monolithic, task-specific approaches.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于可执行动作模型的方法，通过将人类动作表示为运动程序来理解其内部结构和执行质量。
ai.motivation: 现有动作识别方法常忽略动作的内部执行结构，而这对于评估动作执行质量及区分不同动作至关重要。
ai.method: 引入领域特定语言EXACT，将人类动作表示为未完全指定的运动程序，并构建可执行的神经符号模型进行组合建模。
ai.result: 在动作分割和异常检测任务中，该方法提高了数据效率，并能捕捉动作间的直观关系。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要理解人类动作结构和执行质量的研究者提供了一种可解释的建模框架。

idea_spark.idea: 用领域特定语言描述动作，再通过神经符号系统执行推理
idea_spark.risk: 对复杂、非结构化动作的泛化能力可能有限
idea_spark.inspiration: 可将类似框架用于机器人技能学习或体育动作分析

en.tldr: Proposes an executable action model approach that represents human actions as motion programs to understand their internal structure and execution quality.
en.motivation: Existing action recognition methods often overlook the internal execution structure of actions, which is crucial for assessing execution quality and differentiating between actions.
en.method: Introduces a domain-specific language EXACT to represent human actions as underspecified motion programs, and constructs an executable neuro-symbolic model for compositional modeling.
en.result: The method improves data efficiency and captures intuitive relationships between actions in action segmentation and anomaly detection tasks.
en.help_to_user: Provides an interpretable modeling framework for researchers needing to understand human action structure and execution quality.

idea_spark.idea: Describe actions with a domain-specific language, then execute inference through a neuro-symbolic system
idea_spark.risk: Generalization to complex, unstructured actions may be limited
idea_spark.inspiration: Could adapt similar frameworks for robot skill learning or sports motion analysis

papers.title: QuickScope: Certifying Hard Questions in Dynamic LLM Benchmarks
papers.summary: LLM benchmarks are increasingly dynamic: instead of containing a fixed set of questions, they define templates and parameters that can generate an effectively unlimited number of question variants. This flexibility is valuable, but it makes evaluation expensive -- especially when the goal is not just determining an average score, but reliably identifying a model's weak spots. This paper introduces a new methodology for identifying hard questions in dynamic benchmarks. It leverages COUP, a recent Bayesian optimization algorithm (Graham, Velez & Leyton-Brown, 2026), after introducing several substantive modifications to make the algorithm suitable for practical LLM pipelines. We also wrap it in a tool that supports flexible choices of datasets and utility functions, enabling users to target the kinds of questions they care about (e.g., low-accuracy questions; questions that are unusually hard relative to their measured complexity). In experiments across a range of benchmarks, we show that our method, dubbed $\texttt{QuickScope}$, discovers truly difficult questions more sample efficiently than standard baselines, while also reducing false positives from noisy outcomes.

ai.tldr: QuickScope 是一种用于动态 LLM 基准测试中高效识别困难问题的新方法，它基于改进的贝叶斯优化算法，能减少误报并提高样本效率。
ai.motivation: 动态 LLM 基准测试能生成无限多的问题变体，但全面评估成本高昂，尤其是需要可靠识别模型弱点时。
ai.method: 引入对 COUP 贝叶斯优化算法的实质性修改，使其适用于实际 LLM 流程，并封装成支持灵活选择数据集和效用函数的工具。
ai.result: 在多个基准测试中，QuickScope 比标准基线方法更高效地发现真正困难的问题，同时减少了噪声结果导致的误报。
ai.help_to_user: 该方法帮助用户针对性地寻找他们关心的特定类型问题（如低准确率问题、相对于测量复杂度异常困难的问题）。

idea_spark.idea: 将动态基准测试中的“困难问题识别”定义为一个优化问题，利用先验信息和主动学习策略来指导搜索。
idea_spark.risk: 算法的性能可能依赖于问题模板和参数空间的特性，以及所选择的效用函数是否能准确反映用户对“困难”的定义。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将主动学习或优化框架应用于其他需要从大规模生成空间中高效采样特定模式任务的场景，如测试用例生成或对抗样本挖掘。

en.tldr: QuickScope is a new method for efficiently identifying hard questions in dynamic LLM benchmarks, based on a modified Bayesian optimization algorithm, reducing false positives and improving sample efficiency.
en.motivation: Dynamic LLM benchmarks can generate an unlimited number of question variants, but comprehensive evaluation is expensive, especially when reliably identifying a model's weak spots is the goal.
en.method: Introduces substantive modifications to the COUP Bayesian optimization algorithm to make it suitable for practical LLM pipelines, and wraps it in a tool supporting flexible choices of datasets and utility functions.
en.result: Across a range of benchmarks, QuickScope discovers truly difficult questions more sample efficiently than standard baselines while reducing false positives from noisy outcomes.
en.help_to_user: The method enables users to target the kinds of questions they care about (e.g., low-accuracy questions, questions unusually hard relative to their measured complexity).

idea_spark.idea: Framing 'hard question identification' in dynamic benchmarks as an optimization problem, using prior information and active learning strategies to guide the search.
idea_spark.risk: Algorithm performance may depend on the characteristics of the question templates and parameter space, and whether the chosen utility function accurately reflects the user's definition of 'hard'.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires applying active learning or optimization frameworks to other scenarios requiring efficient sampling of specific patterns from large generative spaces, such as test case generation or adversarial example mining.

papers.title: Copy-as-Decode: Grammar-Constrained Parallel Prefill for LLM Editing
papers.summary: LLMs edit text and code by autoregressively regenerating the full output, even when most tokens appear verbatim in the input. We study Copy-as-Decode, a decoding-layer mechanism that recasts edit generation as structured decoding over a two-primitive grammar: <copy lines="i-j"/> references an input line range, <gen>...</gen> emits new content. A token-level FSM guarantees syntactic validity, and a serving-layer primitive updates the KV cache for each copy span via a single parallel-prefill forward rather than $N$ autoregressive steps -- sharing the parallel-forward kernel of speculative decoding but with input tokens as the draft and program-enforced acceptance replacing probabilistic verification. We report an upper-bound analysis that requires no end-to-end training. (i) Kernel speedup: on Qwen2.5-{1.5B, 7B}, copying $N$ tokens via parallel prefill is $6.8\times$--$303\times$ faster than autoregressive ($N \in [8, 512]$, A100 80GB bf16). (ii) Copy ceiling: on ProbeEdit and HumanEvalPack-Fix (Py/JS), $74$--$98\%$ of gold tokens are reachable under the line-level primitive; composed with the empirical kernel over each corpus's span histogram this yields a closed-form wall-clock bound of $29.0\times / 3.4\times / 4.2\times$ ($13.0\times$ pooled). A token-level extension reaches $91$--$99\%$ coverage with $4.5\times$--$6.5\times$ floors. (iii) Pipeline losslessness: oracle programs round-trip through the deterministic resolver on all $482$ cases, localizing any downstream failure to span selection rather than the mechanism. A perturbation study shows pooled EM drops from $100\%$ to $15.48\%$ under off-by-one noise. A fine-tuning pilot on Qwen2.5-Coder-1.5B lifts HEvalFix-Py EM from $0/33$ (untrained) to $12$--$17\%$, a learnability signal, not a production selector. Batched-serving integration and multi-file coverage are scoped as follow-up.

ai.tldr: Copy-as-Decode 是一种解码层机制，将编辑生成重新定义为基于双原语语法的结构化解码，通过并行预填充而非自回归步骤加速LLM编辑。
ai.motivation: LLM编辑文本和代码时通常需要自回归地重新生成整个输出，即使大部分标记在输入中已存在，这造成了效率低下。
ai.method: 设计了一个包含<copy>和<gen>原语的语法，通过令牌级有限状态机保证语法有效性，并使用并行预填充单次前向更新KV缓存，替代N次自回归步骤。
ai.result: 在Qwen2.5模型上，并行预填充复制N个令牌比自回归快6.8-303倍；在编辑任务中，74-98%的黄金令牌可通过行级原语覆盖；确定性解析器在所有482个案例中实现无损往返。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要高效编辑文本/代码的LLM应用开发者提供了一种加速机制，减少计算开销。

idea_spark.idea: 将复制操作抽象为语法原语，通过程序强制接受替代概率验证，实现确定性加速。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于准确的跨度选择，扰动研究表明跨度偏移会导致性能显著下降；需要特定语法约束。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将其他重复性任务（如代码重构、文档修订）转化为类似的语法约束解码问题，以利用并行计算优势。

zh.tldr: Copy-as-Decode 是一种解码层机制，将编辑生成重新定义为基于双原语语法的结构化解码，通过并行预填充而非自回归步骤加速LLM编辑。
zh.motivation: LLM编辑文本和代码时通常需要自回归地重新生成整个输出，即使大部分标记在输入中已存在，这造成了效率低下。
zh.method: 设计了一个包含<copy>和<gen>原语的语法，通过令牌级有限状态机保证语法有效性，并使用并行预填充单次前向更新KV缓存，替代N次自回归步骤。
zh.result: 在Qwen2.5模型上，并行预填充复制N个令牌比自回归快6.8-303倍；在编辑任务中，74-98%的黄金令牌可通过行级原语覆盖；确定性解析器在所有482个案例中实现无损往返。
zh.help_to_user: 为需要高效编辑文本/代码的LLM应用开发者提供了一种加速机制，减少计算开销。

idea_spark.idea: 将复制操作抽象为语法原语，通过程序强制接受替代概率验证，实现确定性加速。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于准确的跨度选择，扰动研究表明跨度偏移会导致性能显著下降；需要特定语法约束。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将其他重复性任务（如代码重构、文档修订）转化为类似的语法约束解码问题，以利用并行计算优势。

en.tldr: Copy-as-Decode is a decoding-layer mechanism that recasts edit generation as structured decoding over a two-primitive grammar, accelerating LLM editing via parallel prefill instead of autoregressive steps.
en.motivation: LLMs edit text and code by autoregressively regenerating the full output even when most tokens appear verbatim in the input, leading to inefficiency.
en.method: Designed a grammar with <copy> and <gen> primitives, enforced syntactic validity via a token-level FSM, and updated KV cache for copy spans via a single parallel-prefill forward rather than N autoregressive steps.
en.result: On Qwen2.5 models, copying N tokens via parallel prefill is 6.8-303× faster than autoregressive; 74-98% of gold tokens are reachable under line-level primitive in editing tasks; deterministic resolver achieves lossless round-trip on all 482 cases.
en.help_to_user: Provides an acceleration mechanism for developers building LLM applications that require efficient text/code editing, reducing computational overhead.

idea_spark.idea: Abstract copy operations as grammar primitives with program-enforced acceptance replacing probabilistic verification for deterministic acceleration.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on accurate span selection—perturbation study shows significant performance drop with off-by-one noise; requires specific grammar constraints.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires transforming other repetitive tasks (e.g., code refactoring, document revision) into similar grammar-constrained decoding problems to leverage parallel computation benefits.

papers.title: Rethinking Cross-Dose PET Denoising: Mitigating Averaging Effects via Residual Noise Learning
papers.summary: Cross-dose denoising for low-dose positron emission tomography (LDPET) has been proposed to address the limited generalization of models trained at a single noise level. In practice, neural networks trained on a specific dose level often fail to generalize to other dose conditions due to variations in noise magnitude and statistical properties. Conventional "one-size-for-all" models attempt to handle this variability but tend to learn averaged representations across noise levels, resulting in degraded performance. In this work, we analyze this limitation and show that standard training formulations implicitly optimize an expectation over heterogeneous noise distributions. To this end, we propose a unified residual noise learning framework that estimates noise directly from low-dose PET images rather than predicting full-dose images. Experiments on large-scale multi-dose PET datasets from two medical centers demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the "one-size-for-all" model, individual dose-specific U-Net models, and dose-conditioned approaches, achieving improved denoising performance. These results indicate that residual noise learning effectively mitigates the averaging effect and enhances generalization for cross-dose PET denoising.

ai.tldr: 提出一种残差噪声学习框架，通过直接从低剂量PET图像估计噪声来缓解跨剂量去噪中的平均效应，提升模型泛化能力。
ai.motivation: 针对低剂量PET图像的跨剂量去噪问题，传统单一模型或‘一刀切’模型在处理不同噪声水平的图像时，会学习到噪声的平均表示，导致性能下降和泛化能力不足。
ai.method: 提出统一的残差噪声学习框架。该方法不直接预测全剂量图像，而是专注于从低剂量PET图像中直接估计噪声。通过这种方式，模型避免了学习跨噪声水平的平均表示。
ai.result: 在两个医疗中心的大规模多剂量PET数据集上的实验表明，该方法在去噪性能上超越了‘一刀切’模型、针对单个剂量训练的U-Net模型以及剂量条件化方法。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理医学图像中因剂量变化导致的噪声特性差异提供了一种新思路，通过专注于噪声本身而非目标图像，可能改善模型在未见噪声水平上的泛化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 在存在多种干扰条件（如不同噪声水平）的任务中，让模型直接学习干扰（噪声）的残差，而非学习一个平均化的干净目标，可能避免模型性能的‘平均化’下降。
idea_spark.risk: 该方法假设噪声是可分离且可估计的。对于噪声与信号高度耦合或噪声模型未知的场景，直接估计噪声可能面临挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将问题重构为‘估计干扰’而非‘恢复目标’，为解决模型在多变条件下的泛化问题提供了一个不同的视角。

en.tldr: Proposes a residual noise learning framework that mitigates the averaging effect in cross-dose PET denoising by directly estimating noise from low-dose images, thereby improving model generalization.
en.motivation: For cross-dose denoising of low-dose PET images, conventional single models or 'one-size-fits-all' models learn averaged representations across different noise levels when processing images with varying noise magnitudes, leading to degraded performance and poor generalization.
en.method: Introduces a unified residual noise learning framework. Instead of directly predicting full-dose images, the method focuses on estimating noise directly from the low-dose PET images. This approach prevents the model from learning averaged representations across heterogeneous noise distributions.
en.result: Experiments on large-scale multi-dose PET datasets from two medical centers demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the 'one-size-fits-all' model, individual dose-specific U-Net models, and dose-conditioned approaches in denoising performance.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new perspective for handling noise characteristic variations due to dose changes in medical imaging. By focusing on the noise itself rather than the target image, it may improve model generalization to unseen noise levels.

idea_spark.idea: In tasks with multiple interference conditions (e.g., different noise levels), having the model directly learn the residual of the interference (noise), rather than learning an averaged clean target, may avoid the 'averaging' degradation in model performance.
idea_spark.risk: This method assumes the noise is separable and estimable. For scenarios where noise is highly coupled with the signal or the noise model is unknown, direct noise estimation could be challenging.
idea_spark.inspiration: Reframing the problem as 'estimating the disturbance' instead of 'restoring the target' offers a different perspective for addressing generalization issues under varying conditions.

papers.title: GeGS-PCR: Effective and Robust 3D Point Cloud Registration with Two-Stage Color-Enhanced Geometric-3DGS Fusion
papers.summary: We address the challenge of point cloud registration using color information, where traditional methods relying solely on geometric features often struggle in low-overlap and incomplete scenarios. To overcome these limitations, we propose GeGS-PCR, a novel two-stage method that combines geometric, color, and Gaussian information for robust registration. Our approach incorporates a dedicated color encoder that enhances color features by extracting multi-level geometric and color data from the original point cloud. We introduce the \textbf{Ge}ometric-3D\textbf{GS} module, which encodes the local neighborhood information of colored superpoints to ensure a globally invariant geometric-color context. Leveraging LORA optimization, we maintain high performance while preserving the expressiveness of 3DGS. Additionally, fast differentiable rendering is utilized to refine the registration process, leading to improved convergence. To further enhance performance, we propose a joint photometric loss that exploits both geometric and color features. This enables strong performance in challenging conditions with extremely low point cloud overlap. We validate our method by colorizing the Kitti dataset as ColorKitti and testing on both Color3DMatch and Color3DLoMatch datasets. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance with \textit{Registration Recall} at 99.9\%, \textit{Relative Rotation Error} as low as 0.013, and \textit{Relative Translation Error} as low as 0.024, improving precision by at least a factor of 2.

ai.tldr: GeGS-PCR是一种两阶段点云配准方法，通过融合几何、颜色和高斯信息，在低重叠和不完整场景中实现鲁棒配准。
ai.motivation: 传统仅依赖几何特征的点云配准方法在低重叠和不完整场景中表现不佳，需要利用颜色信息提升鲁棒性。
ai.method: 提出两阶段方法：1) 专用颜色编码器提取多级几何与颜色特征；2) Geometric-3DGS模块编码彩色超点的局部邻域信息，结合LORA优化和快速可微渲染，使用联合光度损失进行精炼。
ai.result: 在Color3DMatch和Color3DLoMatch数据集上达到SOTA：配准召回率99.9%，相对旋转误差0.013，相对平移误差0.024，精度提升至少2倍。
ai.help_to_user: 为低重叠、不完整点云的鲁棒配准提供了有效的颜色-几何融合解决方案，适用于自动驾驶、三维重建等实际场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将高斯溅射(3DGS)的局部邻域编码与颜色增强结合，构建全局不变的几何-颜色上下文。
idea_spark.risk: 方法依赖于颜色信息的质量，在单色或光照剧烈变化场景中性能可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发于利用可微渲染和LORA优化在保持表达力的同时提升配准收敛性。

idea_spark.idea: 将高斯溅射(3DGS)的局部邻域编码与颜色增强结合，构建全局不变的几何-颜色上下文。

en.tldr: GeGS-PCR is a two-stage point cloud registration method that achieves robust performance in low-overlap and incomplete scenarios by fusing geometric, color, and Gaussian information.
en.motivation: Traditional point cloud registration methods relying solely on geometric features struggle in low-overlap and incomplete scenarios, necessitating the incorporation of color information for improved robustness.
en.method: Proposes a two-stage approach: 1) a dedicated color encoder extracts multi-level geometric and color features; 2) the Geometric-3DGS module encodes local neighborhood information of colored superpoints, combined with LORA optimization and fast differentiable rendering, refined using a joint photometric loss.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on Color3DMatch and Color3DLoMatch datasets: Registration Recall 99.9%, Relative Rotation Error 0.013, Relative Translation Error 0.024, improving precision by at least a factor of 2.
en.help_to_user: Provides an effective color-geometric fusion solution for robust registration of low-overlap and incomplete point clouds, applicable to real-world scenarios like autonomous driving and 3D reconstruction.

idea_spark.idea: Combine Gaussian splatting (3DGS) local neighborhood encoding with color enhancement to build a globally invariant geometric-color context.
idea_spark.risk: Performance may degrade in monochromatic or scenes with drastic illumination changes due to reliance on color information quality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by leveraging differentiable rendering and LORA optimization to improve registration convergence while preserving expressiveness.

papers.title: On the asymptotic duality of spectral variances in random matrix theory and the "1/6" formula
papers.summary: A "mysterious" relation between the number variance and the variance of the $L$-th ordered eigenvalue, first suggested by French et al. [Ann. Phys. 113, 277 (1978)], is revisited and proven to be asymptotically exact for the $β=2$ Dyson symmetry class. Central to the proof is a previously unknown sum rule for the level spacing auto-covariances. Its derivation hinges on our previous work on the power spectrum description of eigenvalue fluctuations in random matrix theory. Analytical results for $β=2$ are complemented by conjectural extensions to the $β=1$ and $β=4$ symmetry classes. Our findings are corroborated by a comprehensive numerical analysis.

ai.tldr: 本文证明了随机矩阵理论中谱方差与第L个有序特征值方差之间的渐近对偶关系，即'1/6'公式，对β=2对称类严格成立，并推测了β=1和β=4类的扩展。
ai.motivation: 重新审视French等人提出的谱方差与有序特征值方差之间的'神秘'关系，旨在严格证明其渐近精确性并扩展至其他对称类。
ai.method: 利用先前关于特征值涨落功率谱描述的工作，推导了能级间距自协方差的新求和规则，作为证明的核心工具。
ai.result: 严格证明了该对偶关系对β=2 Dyson对称类渐近精确成立，提出了对β=1和β=4类的推测性扩展，并通过全面数值分析验证。
ai.help_to_user: 为随机矩阵谱统计中的经典经验关系提供了严格数学基础，揭示了不同方差度量间的深层联系。

idea_spark.idea: 利用自协方差求和规则作为桥梁，连接宏观方差与微观有序统计量。
idea_spark.risk: 对β=1和β=4类的扩展仍为推测，需进一步严格证明。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发探索其他对称类或非平衡系统中类似的对偶关系与普适常数。

en.tldr: This paper proves the asymptotic duality between spectral variances and the variance of the L-th ordered eigenvalue in random matrix theory, known as the '1/6' formula, for the β=2 symmetry class, with conjectural extensions to β=1 and β=4 classes.
en.motivation: To revisit the 'mysterious' relation between number variance and ordered eigenvalue variance suggested by French et al., aiming to prove its asymptotic exactness and extend it to other symmetry classes.
en.method: Leveraging previous work on the power spectrum description of eigenvalue fluctuations, a previously unknown sum rule for level spacing auto-covariances is derived as the central proof tool.
en.result: The duality is proven asymptotically exact for the β=2 Dyson symmetry class, conjectural extensions to β=1 and β=4 are proposed, and results are corroborated by comprehensive numerical analysis.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous mathematical foundation for a classical empirical relation in random matrix spectral statistics, revealing deep connections between different variance measures.

idea_spark.idea: Using the auto-covariance sum rule as a bridge to connect macroscopic variance with microscopic ordered statistics.
idea_spark.risk: Extensions to β=1 and β=4 classes remain conjectural and require further rigorous proof.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires exploration of similar dual relations and universal constants in other symmetry classes or non-equilibrium systems.

papers.title: Nesterov Accelerated Distributed Optimization with Efficient Quantized Communication
papers.summary: In modern large-scale networked systems, rapidly solving optimization problems while utilizing communication resources efficiently is critical for addressing complex tasks. In this paper, we consider an unconstrained distributed optimization problem in which information exchange among nodes is governed by a directed communication graph. In our setup we focus on two key challenges. The first is the zigzag phenomenon caused by the objective functions of individual nodes having significantly different curvature along different directions. The second is that the communication channels among nodes are subject to limited bandwidth, which motivates the use of compressed (quantized) messages. To address both challenges simultaneously, we propose QANM, a distributed optimization algorithm that combines Nesterov-accelerated gradient descent with a distributed finite-time quantized consensus protocol, enabling accelerated convergence. Under strong convexity and smoothness assumptions, we show that our proposed algorithm converges linearly to a neighborhood of the optimal solution. Finally, we validate our algorithm on a distributed sensor fusion application for multi-dimensional target parameter estimation, where simulations across two distinct scenarios confirm the convergence guarantees and demonstrate clear acceleration benefits over non-momentum baselines.

ai.tldr: 提出QANM算法，结合Nesterov加速梯度下降与分布式有限时间量化共识协议，解决具有不同曲率函数和有限带宽通信的分布式优化问题，实现线性收敛。
ai.motivation: 大规模网络系统中，节点间目标函数曲率差异导致优化震荡，且通信带宽有限，需同时解决收敛速度与通信效率问题。
ai.method: 在强凸光滑假设下，将Nesterov加速梯度下降与分布式有限时间量化共识协议结合，节点交换量化信息以减少通信负载。
ai.result: 算法线性收敛至最优解邻域，在分布式传感器融合的多维目标参数估计仿真中，相比无动量基线显示出明显加速优势。
ai.help_to_user: 为带宽受限的分布式网络提供了一种加速优化方案，适用于传感器网络、多智能体协同等场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将有限时间共识的确定性收敛特性与Nesterov动量结合，抵消量化误差对加速过程的影响。
idea_spark.risk: 强凸光滑假设在实际非凸问题中可能不成立，且定向通信图的拓扑变化可能影响共识性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索自适应量化精度或事件触发通信机制，进一步平衡收敛精度与通信成本。

zh.tldr: 提出QANM算法，结合Nesterov加速梯度下降与分布式有限时间量化共识协议，解决具有不同曲率函数和有限带宽通信的分布式优化问题，实现线性收敛。

idea_spark.idea: 将有限时间共识的确定性收敛特性与Nesterov动量结合，抵消量化误差对加速过程的影响。
idea_spark.risk: 强凸光滑假设在实际非凸问题中可能不成立，且定向通信图的拓扑变化可能影响共识性能。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索自适应量化精度或事件触发通信机制，进一步平衡收敛精度与通信成本。

en.tldr: Proposes QANM, combining Nesterov-accelerated gradient descent with a distributed finite-time quantized consensus protocol to address distributed optimization with heterogeneous curvature and limited bandwidth, achieving linear convergence.
en.motivation: In large-scale networked systems, zigzagging due to heterogeneous curvature across nodes and limited communication bandwidth necessitate simultaneous improvement of convergence speed and communication efficiency.
en.method: Under strong convexity and smoothness assumptions, integrates Nesterov acceleration with a distributed finite-time quantized consensus protocol, where nodes exchange quantized messages to reduce communication load.
en.result: The algorithm converges linearly to a neighborhood of the optimal solution. Simulations on distributed sensor fusion for multi‑dimensional target parameter estimation demonstrate clear acceleration benefits over non‑momentum baselines.
en.help_to_user: Provides an accelerated optimization solution for bandwidth‑constrained distributed networks, applicable to sensor networks, multi‑agent coordination, etc.

idea_spark.idea: Combining the deterministic convergence of finite‑time consensus with Nesterov momentum to counteract the impact of quantization errors on acceleration.
idea_spark.risk: Strong convexity and smoothness assumptions may not hold in practical non‑convex problems, and topology changes in directed communication graphs could affect consensus performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore adaptive quantization precision or event‑triggered communication mechanisms to further balance convergence accuracy and communication cost.

papers.title: What Is Actually Being Annotated? Inter-Prompt Reliability as a Measurement Problem in LLM-Based Social Science Labeling
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for annotation in computational social science, yet their methodological reliability under prompt variation remains unclear. This paper introduces Inter-Prompt Reliability (IPR), a framework for evaluating the stability of LLM outputs across semantically equivalent but linguistically varied prompts. Drawing on Inter-Rater Reliability, IPR is measured by Pairwise Agreement Rate (PAR) and its distribution to capture both consistency and stochasticity in model behavior. We evaluate this framework on two tasks with distinct properties: TREC (interpretative) and Politifact (knowledge-anchored). Results show that LLM annotation exhibits substantial stochastic variation in interpretative tasks, while appearing more stable in knowledge-based tasks. We further show that majority voting across prompts significantly improves reproducibility and reduces variance. These findings suggest that LLM prompt acts as an instrumental measurement while its wording exhibits methodological uncertainty. For future LLM-based CSS studies, we suggest that researchers move beyond single-prompt evaluation toward distributional stability and prompt aggregation within our IPR framework.

ai.tldr: 本文提出跨提示可靠性（IPR）框架，用于评估大语言模型在语义相同但措辞不同的提示下输出的一致性，发现解释性任务中模型行为存在显著随机波动，而知识性任务更稳定，多数投票可显著提升可复现性。
ai.motivation: 大语言模型越来越多地用于计算社会科学标注，但其在不同提示下的方法可靠性尚不明确，需要量化评估提示措辞变化带来的测量不确定性。
ai.method: 引入跨提示可靠性（IPR）框架，借鉴评分者间信度概念，使用配对一致率（PAR）及其分布来衡量模型输出的一致性和随机性，并在TREC（解释性）和Politifact（知识锚定）两个任务上进行评估。
ai.result: LLM在解释性任务中表现出显著的随机变异，而在知识性任务中更稳定；跨提示的多数投票能显著提高可复现性并减少方差。
ai.help_to_user: 为LLM在社会科学标注中的应用提供了可靠性评估框架，建议研究者超越单提示评估，转向分布稳定性和提示聚合。

zh.method: 引入跨提示可靠性（IPR）框架，借鉴评分者间信度概念，使用配对一致率（PAR）及其分布来衡量模型输出的一致性和随机性，并在TREC（解释性）和Politifact（知识锚定）两个任务上进行评估。

en.tldr: This paper introduces Inter-Prompt Reliability (IPR), a framework to evaluate the consistency of LLM outputs across semantically equivalent but linguistically varied prompts, finding substantial stochastic variation in interpretative tasks, greater stability in knowledge-based tasks, and that majority voting across prompts significantly improves reproducibility.
en.motivation: LLMs are increasingly used for annotation in computational social science, yet their methodological reliability under prompt variation remains unclear, necessitating quantification of measurement uncertainty introduced by prompt wording.
en.method: Proposes the Inter-Prompt Reliability (IPR) framework, drawing on Inter-Rater Reliability, measured by Pairwise Agreement Rate (PAR) and its distribution to capture consistency and stochasticity, evaluated on TREC (interpretative) and Politifact (knowledge-anchored) tasks.
en.result: LLM annotation exhibits substantial stochastic variation in interpretative tasks but appears more stable in knowledge-based tasks; majority voting across prompts significantly improves reproducibility and reduces variance.
en.help_to_user: Provides a reliability assessment framework for LLM-based social science labeling, suggesting researchers move beyond single-prompt evaluation toward distributional stability and prompt aggregation.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a visualization tool that displays the distribution and consistency of LLM outputs across different prompt variants in real-time, aiding researchers in optimizing prompt design and evaluating labeling quality.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on majority voting may mask systematic biases in the model under specific prompts, and IPR metrics themselves may be influenced by task type and prompt design space.
idea_spark.inspiration: Systematically introduce psychometric reliability concepts (e.g., test-retest reliability, parallel-forms reliability) into LLM evaluation to build a more comprehensive reliability indicator system for model behavior.

papers.title: Causality from Projection and Hardy-Space Analyticity of Non-Markovian Memory Kernels
papers.summary: The Kramers-Kronig (KK) dispersion relations connect the real and imaginary parts of a causal response function through a Hilbert transform. While standard for linear-response theory, their validity for non-Markovian memory kernels -- the central object governing reduced dynamics in open quantum systems -- has not been rigorously established. We prove that the Nakajima-Zwanzig memory kernel belongs to the vector-valued Hardy space H+p(B) for every p>1, provided the initial system-bath state is factorized and the bath has a continuous spectral density. This membership yields rigorous KK reconstruction formulas. We establish three new results, including (i) a CP-Hardy obstruction theorem showing that upper-half-plane poles in approximate kernels imply non-physical (non-CPTP) dynamics; (ii) a passivity-analyticity theorem connecting dissipative kernels to the Herglotz-Nevanlinna class, realizing Gavassino's stability-causality chain for open quantum systems; and (iii) a moment-based Carleman criterion guaranteeing that Pade-reconstructed kernels from Memory Kernel Coupling Theory automatically satisfy KK. We validate the framework on solvable models and construct a counterexample demonstrating that correlated initial states break the Hardy property -- the quantum analogue of Gavassino's mechanism for how acausal macroscopic equations emerge from causal microscopic dynamics.

ai.tldr: 本文严格证明了在因子化初始态和连续谱密度条件下，Nakajima-Zwanzig非马尔可夫记忆核属于Hardy空间，从而建立了Kramers-Kronig重构公式的数学基础，并揭示了初始关联如何破坏因果性。
ai.motivation: Kramers-Kronig色散关系在线性响应理论中已成熟，但对于开放量子系统中描述约化动力学的非马尔可夫记忆核，其因果性与解析性的严格关系尚未建立。
ai.method: 通过证明记忆核属于向量值Hardy空间H+p(B) (p>1)，推导出严格的Kramers-Kronig重构公式，并建立了三个新定理：CP-Hardy阻碍定理、被动性-解析性定理和基于矩的Carleman判据。
ai.result: 获得了记忆核的Hardy空间性质，使得Kramers-Kronig关系严格成立；发现初始系统-浴关联会破坏Hardy性质，导致非因果宏观方程的出现；验证了Pade重构核自动满足Kramers-Kronig关系。
ai.help_to_user: 为开放量子系统中非马尔可夫记忆核的因果性提供了严格的数学框架，明确了Kramers-Kronig关系适用的条件，并揭示了初始关联对动力学的根本影响。

idea_spark.idea: 将记忆核的因果性验证转化为Hardy空间隶属性问题，用泛函分析工具处理开放量子系统的非马尔可夫动力学。
idea_spark.risk: 结论强烈依赖于因子化初始态和浴谱连续性的假设，实际物理系统可能不满足这些条件。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发思考：如何将解析函数论中的经典结果（如Carleman判据）系统性地应用于量子动力学的正则化与重构问题？

en.tldr: This work rigorously proves that the Nakajima-Zwanzig non-Markovian memory kernel belongs to the Hardy space under factorized initial states and continuous spectral density, establishing the mathematical foundation for Kramers-Kronig reconstruction formulas and revealing how initial correlations break causality.
en.motivation: While Kramers-Kronig dispersion relations are standard in linear-response theory, their validity for non-Markovian memory kernels—central to reduced dynamics in open quantum systems—has not been rigorously established.
en.method: By proving the memory kernel belongs to the vector-valued Hardy space H+p(B) for p>1, rigorous Kramers-Kronig reconstruction formulas are derived. Three new theorems are established: a CP-Hardy obstruction theorem, a passivity-analyticity theorem, and a moment-based Carleman criterion.
en.result: Obtained Hardy-space properties of memory kernels, making Kramers-Kronig relations strictly valid; discovered that initial system-bath correlations break the Hardy property, leading to acausal macroscopic equations; validated that Pade-reconstructed kernels automatically satisfy Kramers-Kronig relations.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous mathematical framework for causality of non-Markovian memory kernels in open quantum systems, clarifies conditions for Kramers-Kronig applicability, and reveals fundamental impact of initial correlations on dynamics.

idea_spark.idea: Transform causality verification of memory kernels into a Hardy-space membership problem, using functional analysis tools to treat non-Markovian dynamics in open quantum systems.
idea_spark.risk: Conclusions strongly depend on assumptions of factorized initial states and continuous bath spectrum, which may not hold in realistic physical systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspiration: How can classical results from analytic function theory (e.g., Carleman criterion) be systematically applied to regularization and reconstruction problems in quantum dynamics?

papers.title: Playing Psychic: Using Thought Trees to Predict Reasoning Models Accuracy on Coding Tasks
papers.summary: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have shown that test-time scaling can substantially improve model performance on complex tasks, particularly in the coding domain. Under this paradigm, models use a larger token budget during inference to generate intermediate reasoning traces before producing a final answer. However, current evaluations primarily rely on competitive programming benchmarks, which may not capture the full range of reasoning abilities. In this work, we perform a systematic study of frontier reasoning models to understand their performance on real-world coding benchmarks. To gain more insights into the performance of such models, we devise a programmatic way to {\em automatically generate} coding tasks of arbitrary difficulty and structure from existing benchmarks. Using this framework, our analysis reveals that the structure of a reasoning trace, not just its contents, is a strong predictor of correctness. Motivated by this, we propose structured thought-trees as means to represent reasoning traces. To illustrate their use, we train a lightweight classifier on features extracted from thought-trees to predict trace correctness, and demonstrate that flagging and retrying structurally anomalous traces based on the extracted features yields consistent gains at lower complexity levels.

ai.tldr: 本研究提出使用结构化思维树来表示大语言模型的推理轨迹，并训练轻量级分类器基于思维树特征预测轨迹正确性，从而在较低复杂度任务上实现性能提升。
ai.motivation: 当前基于竞争性编程基准的评估可能无法全面捕捉模型的推理能力，且推理轨迹的结构（而不仅仅是内容）对预测答案正确性有重要作用。
ai.method: 设计了一种从现有基准自动生成任意难度和结构编码任务的程序化方法；提出结构化思维树来表示推理轨迹；提取思维树特征训练轻量级分类器来预测轨迹正确性。
ai.result: 分析表明推理轨迹的结构是答案正确性的强预测指标；基于提取的特征标记和重试结构异常的轨迹，在较低复杂度级别上获得了稳定的性能提升。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种通过分析推理轨迹结构来预测和提升大语言模型在编码任务上性能的系统化方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将模型的内部推理过程显式地结构化为树，并定义可量化的结构特征（如分支模式、深度一致性）作为性能诊断指标。
idea_spark.risk: 生成的任务可能无法完全代表真实世界的复杂性；结构特征的定义可能过于任务特定。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们不仅关注模型输出的最终答案，更应关注其得出答案的推理过程的结构完整性，这可能是评估和提升模型可靠性的新维度。
