idea_spark.idea: 将时间难度作为课程变量，结合少样本提示进行微调，以增强模型对历史上下文的利用。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于特定纵向数据集的构建，可能对任务多样性敏感；课程设计需要人工定义难度梯度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将时序结构显式编码到指令中，用于改善模型对演变模式的理解和预测。

en.tldr: LiFT is a longitudinal instruction fine-tuning framework that unifies diverse longitudinal modeling tasks under a shared instruction schema, using a progressive curriculum to enhance temporal reasoning, consistently outperforming base-model ICL across multiple datasets and model scales.
en.motivation: Longitudinal NLP tasks require reasoning over temporally ordered text to detect persistence and change in human behavior and opinions. However, in-context learning with large language models struggles on tasks requiring integration of historical context, tracking of evolving interactions, and handling of rare change events.
en.method: Introduces the LiFT framework, employing a shared instruction schema to unify diverse longitudinal modeling tasks. Uses a curriculum that progressively increases temporal difficulty while incorporating few-shot structure and temporal conditioning to encourage effective use of past context.
en.result: Evaluated across five datasets. Models trained on tasks with different temporal granularity are tested for generalizability on two separate datasets. Across models of varying parameter sizes, LiFT consistently outperforms base-model ICL, with strong gains on out-of-distribution data and minority change events.
en.help_to_user: Provides an effective instruction fine-tuning framework for researchers dealing with time-series text and detecting behavioral changes, improving model performance in temporal reasoning and generalization for longitudinal tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Use temporal difficulty as a curriculum variable combined with few-shot prompting during fine-tuning to enhance the model's utilization of historical context.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on the construction of specific longitudinal datasets and may be sensitive to task diversity; curriculum design requires manual definition of difficulty progression.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires the explicit encoding of temporal structure into instructions to improve model understanding and prediction of evolving patterns.

papers.title: SegTTA: Training-Free Test-Time Augmentation for Zero-Shot Medical Imaging Segmentation
papers.summary: Increasingly advanced data augmentation techniques have greatly aided clinical medical research, increasing data diversity and improving model generalization capabilities. Although most current basic models exhibit strong generalization abilities, image quality varies due to differences in equipment and operators. To address these challenges, we present SegTTA, a framework that improves medical image segmentation without model retraining by combining four augmentations (Gamma correction, Contrast enhancement, Gaussian blur, Gaussian noise) with weighted voting across multiple MedSAM2 checkpoints. Experiments demonstrate consistent improvements across three diverse datasets: healthy uterus segmentation, uterine myoma detection, and multi class hepatic structure segmentation. Ablation studies reveal that large organs benefit from intensity augmentations while small lesions require noise augmentations. The voting threshold controls the coverage precision trade off, enabling task specific optimization for different clinical requirements. Ultimately, on a multiclass hepatic vessel dataset, compared to MedSAM2 baselines, our method achieves an increase of 1.6 in mIoU and 1.9 in aIoU, along with a reduction of approximately 2.0 in HD95. Code will be available at https://github.com/AIGeeksGroup/SegTTA.

ai.tldr: SegTTA是一种无需训练、基于测试时增强的零样本医学图像分割框架，通过四种图像增强与多检查点加权投票提升分割性能。
ai.motivation: 医学图像质量因设备和操作者差异而不同，现有基础模型虽泛化能力强，但仍需适应这种变化以提升分割精度。
ai.method: 结合伽马校正、对比度增强、高斯模糊和高斯噪声四种增强，在多个MedSAM2检查点上进行加权投票，投票阈值可调节覆盖度与精度的平衡。
ai.result: 在健康子宫分割、子宫肌瘤检测和多类肝结构分割三个数据集上均取得稳定提升；在肝血管数据集上，mIoU提升1.6，aIoU提升1.9，HD95降低约2.0。
ai.help_to_user: 为临床医生和研究者提供一种无需重新训练模型即可提升不同质量医学图像分割效果的工具，尤其适用于设备或操作条件多变的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 针对不同尺寸的解剖结构（大器官 vs. 小病灶）设计差异化的增强偏好策略。
idea_spark.risk: 增强类型与权重的选择可能依赖于特定数据集，需谨慎验证其泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发在资源受限的临床环境中，通过轻量级后处理而非重新训练来适配模型。

idea_spark.risk: 增强类型与权重的选择可能依赖于特定数据集，需谨慎验证其泛化性。

en.tldr: SegTTA is a training-free test-time augmentation framework for zero-shot medical image segmentation, improving performance via four augmentations and weighted voting across multiple MedSAM2 checkpoints.
en.motivation: Medical image quality varies due to differences in equipment and operators, requiring adaptation of foundational models to enhance segmentation accuracy despite their strong generalization.
en.method: Combines four augmentations (Gamma correction, Contrast enhancement, Gaussian blur, Gaussian noise) with weighted voting across multiple MedSAM2 checkpoints; a voting threshold controls the coverage-precision trade-off.
en.result: Consistent improvements across three diverse datasets: healthy uterus segmentation, uterine myoma detection, and multi-class hepatic structure segmentation. On a hepatic vessel dataset, mIoU increased by 1.6, aIoU by 1.9, and HD95 reduced by ~2.0 compared to MedSAM2 baselines.
en.help_to_user: Provides clinicians and researchers a tool to enhance segmentation of medical images with varying quality without model retraining, particularly useful in settings with diverse equipment or operators.

idea_spark.idea: Design differentiated augmentation preferences for anatomical structures of different sizes (large organs vs. small lesions).
idea_spark.risk: The choice of augmentation types and weights may be dataset-dependent, requiring careful validation for generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires lightweight post-processing adaptation instead of retraining in resource-constrained clinical environments.

papers.title: Beyond Single-Score Ranking: Facet-Aware Reranking for Controllable Diversity in Paper Recommendation
papers.summary: Current paper recommendation systems output a single similarity score that mixes different notions of relatedness, so users cannot specify why papers should be similar. We present SciFACE (Scientific Faceted Cross-Encoder), a reranking framework that models two independent facets: Background (what problem is studied) and Method (how it is solved). SciFACE trains two separate cross-encoders on 5,891 real seed-candidate paper pairs labeled by GPT-4o-mini with facet-specific criteria and validated against human judgments. On CSFCube, SciFACE reaches 70.63 NDCG@20 on Background (5.9 points above SPECTER) and 49.06 NDCG@20 on Method (31.1 points above SPECTER), competitive with state-of-the-art results. Compared with FaBLE without citation pre-training, SciFACE improves Method NDCG@20 by 4.1 points while using 5,891 labeled pairs versus 40K synthetic augmentations. These results show that high-quality grounded facet labels can be more data-efficient than large-scale synthetic augmentation for learning fine-grained scientific similarity.

ai.tldr: 提出SciFACE框架，通过背景和方法两个独立维度对论文进行重排序，实现可控多样性的推荐。
ai.motivation: 现有论文推荐系统仅输出单一相似度分数，无法让用户指定相似的具体方面（如研究问题或解决方法）。
ai.method: 训练两个独立的交叉编码器（背景和方法），使用GPT-4o-mini标注的5,891个真实论文对进行训练，并在CSFCube数据集上评估。
ai.result: 在背景维度NDCG@20达到70.63（比SPECTER高5.9分），方法维度达到49.06（比SPECTER高31.1分），且比无引用预训练的FaBLE方法在方法维度提升4.1分。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究人员提供可控制相似性维度的论文推荐，帮助更精准地发现相关文献。

idea_spark.idea: 利用大模型标注高质量细分维度标签，替代大规模合成数据增强。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖大模型标注质量；双编码器可能增加计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 细分相似性维度可提升推荐系统的可解释性和可控性。

zh.result: 在背景维度NDCG@20达到70.63（比SPECTER高5.9分），方法维度达到49.06（比SPECTER高31.1分），且比无引用预训练的FaBLE方法在方法维度提升4.1分。
zh.help_to_user: 为研究人员提供可控制相似性维度的论文推荐，帮助更精准地发现相关文献。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖大模型标注质量；双编码器可能增加计算开销。
idea_spark.inspiration: 细分相似性维度可提升推荐系统的可解释性和可控性。

en.tldr: Proposes SciFACE, a reranking framework that models Background and Method facets independently for controllable diversity in paper recommendation.
en.motivation: Current paper recommendation systems output a single similarity score, preventing users from specifying why papers should be similar (e.g., problem studied vs. solution approach).
en.method: Trains two separate cross-encoders (Background and Method) on 5,891 real seed-candidate paper pairs labeled by GPT-4o-mini with facet-specific criteria, evaluated on CSFCube.
en.result: Achieves 70.63 NDCG@20 on Background (5.9 points above SPECTER) and 49.06 NDCG@20 on Method (31.1 points above SPECTER); outperforms FaBLE without citation pre-training by 4.1 points on Method NDCG@20.
en.help_to_user: Enables researchers to receive paper recommendations with controllable similarity dimensions, facilitating more precise literature discovery.

idea_spark.idea: Use LLM-generated high-quality facet labels as a data-efficient alternative to large-scale synthetic augmentation.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on LLM annotation quality; dual encoders may increase computational cost.
idea_spark.inspiration: Granular similarity facets improve interpretability and controllability of recommender systems.

papers.title: Why Training-Free Token Reduction Collapses: The Inherent Instability of Pairwise Scoring Signals
papers.summary: Training-free token reduction methods for Vision Transformers (ToMe, ToFu, PiToMe, and MCTF) employ different scoring mechanisms, yet they share a closely matched cliff-like collapse at high compression. This paper explains \emph{why}. We develop a diagnostic framework with two tools, ranking consistency $ρ_s$ and off-diagonal correlation $ρ_\text{off}$, that decomposes the collapse into (1)a signal-agnostic error amplifier inherent to layer-wise reduction, predicting convex Pareto curves and $r_{\text{crit}} \propto 1/L$; and (2)shared reliance on \emph{pairwise} similarity signals whose ranking consistency degrades from $ρ_s{=}0.88$ to $0.27$ in deep layers. Pairwise rankings are inherently unstable ($O(N_p^2)$ joint perturbations) while unary signals enjoy greater stability ($O(N_p)$ perturbations, CLT). From three design principles derived from this diagnosis, we construct CATIS as a constructive validation: unary signals raise the trigger threshold, triage suppresses the gain. On ViT-Large at 63% FLOPs reduction, CATIS retains 96.9% of vanilla accuracy (81.0%) on ImageNet-1K where all baselines collapse to 43--65%.

ai.tldr: 本文揭示了训练无关的视觉Transformer令牌缩减方法在高压缩下崩溃的根本原因：层间缩减固有的误差放大效应与成对相似性信号的不稳定性。
ai.motivation: 现有训练无关令牌缩减方法（如ToMe、ToFu等）在不同评分机制下均在高压缩时出现相似的悬崖式性能崩溃，需要探究其内在机理。
ai.method: 提出诊断框架，使用排序一致性ρ_s和非对角相关性ρ_off分解崩溃原因；推导出三条设计原则，并构建CATIS方法进行验证。
ai.result: 在ViT-Large上实现63% FLOPs缩减时，CATIS在ImageNet-1K上保持96.9%的原始准确率（81.0%），而基线方法崩溃至43-65%。
ai.help_to_user: 为训练无关的令牌压缩方法提供了稳定性诊断工具和设计原则，帮助避免高压缩下的性能崩溃。

idea_spark.idea: 将成对相似性信号替换为单元信号，结合分级处理机制提升压缩稳定性。
idea_spark.risk: 单元信号的设计需要与模型架构和任务特性深度耦合。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从信号扰动理论角度分析算法稳定性，为轻量化方法提供新的理论视角。

en.tldr: This paper reveals the root cause of collapse in training-free token reduction for Vision Transformers at high compression: inherent error amplification in layer-wise reduction and instability of pairwise similarity signals.
en.motivation: Existing training-free token reduction methods (e.g., ToMe, ToFu) show closely matched cliff-like collapse at high compression despite different scoring mechanisms, requiring investigation into inherent mechanisms.
en.method: Develop a diagnostic framework using ranking consistency ρ_s and off-diagonal correlation ρ_off to decompose collapse; derive three design principles and construct CATIS as validation.
en.result: At 63% FLOPs reduction on ViT-Large, CATIS retains 96.9% of vanilla accuracy (81.0%) on ImageNet-1K where all baselines collapse to 43–65%.
en.help_to_user: Provides stability diagnostic tools and design principles for training-free token compression, helping avoid performance collapse at high compression ratios.

idea_spark.idea: Replace pairwise similarity signals with unary signals combined with triage mechanisms to enhance compression stability.
idea_spark.risk: Design of unary signals requires deep coupling with model architecture and task characteristics.
idea_spark.inspiration: Analyzing algorithm stability from signal perturbation theory offers new theoretical perspectives for lightweight methods.

papers.title: HyKey: Hyperspectral Keypoint Detection and Matching in Minimally Invasive Surgery
papers.summary: Purpose: 3D reconstruction in minimally invasive surgery (MIS) enables enhanced surgical guidance through improved visualisation, tool tracking, and augmented reality. However, traditional RGB-based keypoint detection and matching pipelines struggle with surgical challenges, such as poor texture and complex illumination. We investigate whether using snapshot hyperspectral imaging (HSI) can provide improved results on keypoint detection and matching surgical scenes. Methods: We developed HyKey, a HYperspectral KEYpoint detection and description model made up of a hybrid 3D-2D convolutional neural network that jointly extracts spatial-spectral features from HSI. The model was trained using synthetic homographic augmentation and epipolar geometry constraints on a robotically-acquired dual-camera RGB-HSI laparoscopic dataset of ex-vivo organs with calibrated camera poses. We benchmarked performance against established RGB-based methods, including SuperPoint and ALIKE. Results: Our HSI-based model outperformed RGB baselines on registered RGB frames, achieving 96.62% mean matching accuracy and 67.18% mean average accuracy at 10 degree on pose estimation, demonstrating consistent improvements across multiple evaluation metrics. Conclusion: Integrating spectral information from an HSI cube offers a promising approach for robust monocular 3D reconstruction in MIS, addressing limitations of texture-poor surgical environments through enhanced spectral-spatial feature discrimination. Our model and dataset are available at https://github.com/alexsaikia/HyKey-Hyperspectral-Keypoint-Detection

ai.tldr: HyKey模型利用高光谱成像（HSI）在纹理贫乏的手术场景中实现更稳健的关键点检测与匹配，提升单目3D重建性能。
ai.motivation: 传统RGB关键点检测在纹理贫乏、光照复杂的手术场景中表现不佳，高光谱成像可能提供更丰富的空间-光谱特征以应对这些挑战。
ai.method: 开发HyKey模型，采用混合3D-2D卷积神经网络从HSI立方体中联合提取空间-光谱特征，使用合成单应性增强和极几何约束在机器人采集的双摄像头RGB-HSI腹腔镜数据集上进行训练。
ai.result: HSI模型在配准的RGB帧上优于RGB基线方法（如SuperPoint、ALIKE），平均匹配准确率达96.62%，姿态估计在10度阈值下的平均准确率达67.18%。
ai.help_to_user: 为纹理贫乏的手术环境提供了一种基于高光谱成像的稳健关键点检测与匹配方案，可增强手术导航、工具跟踪和增强现实应用。

idea_spark.idea: 将高光谱成像与深度学习结合，利用光谱维度信息增强传统视觉任务的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 高光谱设备成本较高，实时数据处理计算需求大，临床部署可能存在硬件集成挑战。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发探索多模态成像（如RGB+HSI）在医疗或其他专业视觉领域的融合，利用互补信息解决单一模态的局限性。

en.tldr: The HyKey model uses hyperspectral imaging (HSI) to achieve more robust keypoint detection and matching in texture-poor surgical scenes, improving monocular 3D reconstruction performance.
en.motivation: Traditional RGB-based keypoint detection struggles in surgical environments with poor texture and complex illumination; hyperspectral imaging may provide richer spatial-spectral features to address these challenges.
en.method: Developed HyKey, a hybrid 3D-2D convolutional neural network that jointly extracts spatial-spectral features from HSI cubes, trained using synthetic homographic augmentation and epipolar geometry constraints on a robotically-acquired dual-camera RGB-HSI laparoscopic dataset.
en.result: The HSI-based model outperformed RGB baselines (e.g., SuperPoint, ALIKE) on registered RGB frames, achieving 96.62% mean matching accuracy and 67.18% mean average accuracy at 10 degrees for pose estimation.
en.help_to_user: Provides a robust keypoint detection and matching solution based on hyperspectral imaging for texture-poor surgical environments, enhancing surgical guidance, tool tracking, and augmented reality applications.

idea_spark.idea: Combine hyperspectral imaging with deep learning to leverage spectral dimension information for enhancing robustness in traditional vision tasks.
idea_spark.risk: High cost of hyperspectral equipment, substantial computational demands for real-time data processing, and potential hardware integration challenges for clinical deployment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires exploration of multimodal imaging (e.g., RGB+HSI) fusion in medical or other specialized vision domains, leveraging complementary information to overcome limitations of single modalities.

papers.title: OptiMVMap: Offline Vectorized Map Construction via Optimal Multi-vehicle Perspectives
papers.summary: Offline vectorized maps constitute critical infrastructure for high-precision autonomous driving and mapping services. Existing approaches rely predominantly on single ego-vehicle trajectories, which fundamentally suffer from viewpoint insufficiency: while memory-based methods extend observation time by aggregating ego-trajectory frames, they lack the spatial diversity needed to reveal occluded regions. Incorporating views from surrounding vehicles offers complementary perspectives, yet naive fusion introduces three key challenges: computational cost from large candidate pools, redundancy from near-collinear viewpoints, and noise from pose errors and occlusion artifacts.   We present OptiMVMap, which reformulates multi-vehicle mapping as a select-then-fuse problem to address these challenges systematically. An Optimal Vehicle Selection (OVS) module strategically identifies a compact subset of helpers that maximally reduce ego-centric uncertainty in occluded regions, addressing computation and redundancy challenges. Cross-Vehicle Attention (CVA) and Semantic-aware Noise Filter (SNF) then perform pose-tolerant alignment and artifact suppression before BEV-level fusion, addressing the noise challenge. This targeted pipeline yields more complete and topologically faithful maps with substantially fewer views than indiscriminate aggregation. On nuScenes and Argoverse2, OptiMVMap improves MapTRv2 by +10.5 mAP and +9.3 mAP, respectively, and surpasses memory-augmented baselines MVMap and HRMapNet by +6.2 mAP and +3.8 mAP on nuScenes. These results demonstrate that uncertainty-guided selection of helper vehicles is essential for efficient and accurate multi-vehicle vectorized mapping. The code is released at https://github.com/DanZeDong/OptiMVMap.

ai.tldr: OptiMVMap通过最优多车视角选择与融合，解决离线矢量化地图构建中的视角不足、计算冗余和噪声问题，显著提升地图完整性与拓扑精度。
ai.motivation: 现有单车主车轨迹方法存在视角不足的根本缺陷，无法有效揭示被遮挡区域。引入周围车辆视角虽能提供互补信息，但直接融合面临计算成本高、视角冗余和传感器噪声三大挑战。
ai.method: 将多车建图重构为“先选择后融合”问题：1) 最优车辆选择模块基于不确定性减少策略，紧凑选取辅助车辆；2) 跨车辆注意力模块进行位姿容忍对齐；3) 语义感知噪声滤波器抑制伪影；最后进行BEV级融合。
ai.result: 在nuScenes和Argoverse2数据集上，分别将MapTRv2的mAP提升10.5和9.3，并超越MVMap和HRMapNet等记忆增强基线6.2和3.8 mAP，证明不确定性引导的车辆选择对高效准确的多车矢量化建图至关重要。
ai.help_to_user: 为高精自动驾驶和地图服务提供更完整、拓扑准确的离线矢量化地图构建方案，通过智能选择辅助车辆视角，以更少视图实现更优重建效果。

idea_spark.idea: 将多源数据融合问题重构为“选择-融合”两阶段流程，先通过信息增益最大化选择最具互补性的数据源，再进行鲁棒融合。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖车辆间相对位姿估计精度；在极端遮挡或车辆密度过低场景下效果可能受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发从“信息价值”而非“数据量”角度设计融合系统，为资源受限的边缘计算或实时应用提供优化思路。

en.tldr: OptiMVMap addresses viewpoint insufficiency, computational redundancy, and noise in offline vectorized map construction through optimal multi-vehicle perspective selection and fusion, significantly improving map completeness and topological accuracy.
en.motivation: Existing single-ego-vehicle trajectory methods fundamentally suffer from viewpoint insufficiency, failing to reveal occluded regions effectively. While incorporating surrounding vehicle views offers complementary perspectives, naive fusion introduces three key challenges: high computational cost, viewpoint redundancy, and sensor noise.
en.method: Reformulates multi-vehicle mapping as a select-then-fuse problem: 1) Optimal Vehicle Selection module strategically picks a compact subset of helper vehicles to maximally reduce ego-centric uncertainty; 2) Cross-Vehicle Attention performs pose-tolerant alignment; 3) Semantic-aware Noise Filter suppresses artifacts before BEV-level fusion.
en.result: Improves MapTRv2 by +10.5 mAP and +9.3 mAP on nuScenes and Argoverse2 respectively, and surpasses memory-augmented baselines MVMap and HRMapNet by +6.2 mAP and +3.8 mAP on nuScenes, demonstrating that uncertainty-guided vehicle selection is essential for efficient and accurate multi-vehicle vectorized mapping.
en.help_to_user: Provides a solution for constructing more complete and topologically faithful offline vectorized maps for high-precision autonomous driving and mapping services, achieving better reconstruction with substantially fewer views through intelligent helper vehicle selection.

idea_spark.idea: Reformulate multi-source data fusion as a two-stage 'select-then-fuse' pipeline: first select the most complementary data sources by maximizing information gain, then perform robust fusion.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on the accuracy of inter-vehicle relative pose estimation; effectiveness may be limited in scenarios with extreme occlusion or very low vehicle density.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires designing fusion systems from the perspective of 'information value' rather than 'data volume', offering optimization ideas for resource-constrained edge computing or real-time applications.

papers.title: DuConTE: Dual-Granularity Text Encoder with Topology-Constrained Attention for Text-attributed Graphs
papers.summary: Text-attributed graphs integrate semantic information of node texts with topological structure, offering significant value in various applications such as document classification and information extraction. Existing approaches typically encode textual content using language models (LMs), followed by graph neural networks (GNNs) to process structural information. However, during the LM-based text encoding phase, most methods not only perform semantic interaction solely at the word-token granularity, but also neglect the structural dependencies among texts from different nodes. In this work, we propose DuConTE, a dual-granularity text encoder with topology-constrained attention. The model employs a cascaded architecture of two pretrained LMs, encoding semantics first at the word-token granularity and then at the node granularity. During the self-attention computation in each LM, we dynamically adjust the attention mask matrix based on node connectivity, guiding the model to learn semantic correlations informed by the graph structure. Furthermore, when composing node representations from word-token embeddings, we separately evaluate the importance of tokens under the center-node context and the neighborhood context, enabling the capture of more contextually relevant semantic information. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that DuConTE achieves state-of-the-art performance on the majority of them.

ai.tldr: DuConTE是一种用于文本属性图的双粒度文本编码器，通过拓扑约束注意力机制，在词粒度和节点粒度上编码语义，并利用图结构指导语义交互。
ai.motivation: 现有方法在文本编码阶段通常仅在词粒度进行语义交互，且忽略了不同节点文本之间的结构依赖关系。
ai.method: 采用两个预训练语言模型的级联架构，先在词粒度编码语义，后在节点粒度编码。在自注意力计算中，根据节点连接性动态调整注意力掩码矩阵，并在组合节点表示时分别评估中心节点上下文和邻居上下文中词元的重要性。
ai.result: 在多个基准数据集上的实验表明，DuConTE在大多数数据集上达到了最先进的性能。
ai.help_to_user: 为处理文本属性图提供了一种新的编码框架，能够更好地融合文本语义与图结构信息。

idea_spark.idea: 利用图结构动态调整语言模型的注意力模式，引导语义学习。
idea_spark.risk: 对图结构的质量依赖较强，在稀疏或噪声较大的图中效果可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将图拓扑作为先验知识注入预训练模型的注意力机制，以增强结构感知的语义表示。

idea_spark.risk: 对图结构的质量依赖较强，在稀疏或噪声较大的图中效果可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将图拓扑作为先验知识注入预训练模型的注意力机制，以增强结构感知的语义表示。

en.tldr: DuConTE is a dual-granularity text encoder for text-attributed graphs that employs topology-constrained attention to encode semantics at both word-token and node granularities, guided by graph structure.
en.motivation: Existing methods typically perform semantic interaction only at the word-token granularity during text encoding and neglect structural dependencies among texts from different nodes.
en.method: Uses a cascaded architecture of two pretrained language models to encode semantics first at word-token granularity, then at node granularity. Dynamically adjusts the attention mask matrix based on node connectivity during self-attention, and separately evaluates token importance under center-node and neighborhood contexts when composing node representations.
en.result: Extensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that DuConTE achieves state-of-the-art performance on the majority of them.
en.help_to_user: Provides a novel encoding framework for text-attributed graphs that better integrates textual semantics with graph structural information.

idea_spark.idea: Use graph structure to dynamically adjust the attention patterns of language models, guiding semantic learning.
idea_spark.risk: Performance may degrade in sparse or noisy graphs due to strong reliance on graph structure quality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires injecting graph topology as prior knowledge into the attention mechanisms of pretrained models to enhance structure-aware semantic representations.

papers.title: Small Model as Master Orchestrator: Learning Unified Agent-Tool Orchestration with Parallel Subtask Decomposition
papers.summary: Multi-agent systems (MAS) demonstrate clear advantages in tackling complex problems by coordinating diverse agents and external tools. However, most existing orchestration methods rely on static workflows or serial agent scheduling, and are further constrained by heterogeneous interface protocols between tools and agents. This leads to high system complexity and poor extensibility. To mitigate these issues, we propose Agent-as-Tool, a unified parallel orchestration paradigm that abstracts both agents and tools into a standardized, learnable action space with protocol normalization and explicit state feedback. Building on this paradigm, we train a lightweight orchestrator, ParaManager, which decouples planning decisions from subtask solving, enabling state-aware parallel subtask decomposition, delegation, and asynchronous execution. For training, we adopt a two-stage ParaManager training pipeline. It improves robustness by incorporating supervised fine-tuning (SFT) trajectories equipped with recovery mechanisms, and further applies reinforcement learning (RL) to achieve an optimal balance among task success, protocol compliance, diversity, and reasoning efficiency. Experiments show that ParaManager achieves strong performance across multiple benchmarks and exhibits robust generalization under unseen model pools.

ai.tldr: 提出Agent-as-Tool统一并行编排范式，将智能体和工具抽象为标准化的可学习动作空间，并训练轻量级编排器ParaManager实现状态感知的并行子任务分解与执行。
ai.motivation: 现有多智能体系统编排方法依赖静态工作流或串行调度，且工具与智能体间接口协议异构，导致系统复杂度高、扩展性差。
ai.method: 通过协议归一化和显式状态反馈构建统一动作空间；训练ParaManager编排器，采用两阶段训练流程：结合恢复机制的监督微调轨迹提升鲁棒性，再应用强化学习优化任务成功率、协议合规性、多样性和推理效率的平衡。
ai.result: ParaManager在多个基准测试中表现优异，并在未见过的模型池中展现出强大的泛化能力。
ai.help_to_user: 为构建可扩展、低复杂度的多智能体工具协作系统提供了统一的并行编排框架和轻量级训练方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将编排决策与子任务求解解耦，通过状态反馈实现并行分解与异步执行，可能提升复杂工作流的整体效率。
idea_spark.risk: 协议归一化可能损失特定工具的高级功能；轻量级编排器的容量可能限制其处理极端复杂任务的能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 两阶段训练（SFT+RL）结合显式恢复机制的设计，可为其他需要鲁棒性和多目标优化的序列决策模型提供参考。

papers.title: On the Unification of Optimal Current Reference Theory for Wound Rotor Synchronous Machines
papers.summary: Controllers for motor drives typically require a current reference which will satisfy the requested torque subject to system constraints. This work generalizes existing current reference theory to the case of the Wound Rotor Synchronous Machine (WRSM). By incorporating the additional rotor-current degree-of-freedom, along with magnetic saturation, cross-coupling, and speed-dependent core losses, the problem of finding an optimal current reference is formulated within affine flux regions as a quadratically constrained quadratic program using a piecewise-affine approximation derived from finite-element data. The solution is characterized according to the active constraint regime, yielding closed-form or low-dimensional polynomial solutions in several cases, and a small semidefinite program in the voltage constrained regime. The proposed framework extends unified optimal current reference theory beyond the permanent-magnet setting to three degree-of-freedom WRSMs while remaining computationally tractable. Results on a physical WRSM prototype illustrate the effectiveness of the approach across the torque-speed operating envelope.

ai.tldr: 本文推广了最优电流参考理论，将其应用于绕线转子同步电机，通过考虑转子电流自由度、磁饱和、交叉耦合和速度相关铁损，在仿射磁通区域内将问题表述为二次约束二次规划，并给出了闭式解或低维多项式解。
ai.motivation: 电机驱动器控制器需要满足转矩要求且受系统约束的电流参考。现有理论主要针对永磁电机，需要扩展到具有额外转子电流自由度的绕线转子同步电机。
ai.method: 结合有限元数据推导的分段仿射近似，将寻找最优电流参考的问题在仿射磁通区域内表述为二次约束二次规划，并根据主动约束机制对解进行表征。
ai.result: 在多种情况下得到闭式解或低维多项式解，在电压约束机制下得到一个小型半定规划。所提框架将统一最优电流参考理论扩展到三自由度绕线转子同步电机，且计算可行。
ai.help_to_user: 为绕线转子同步电机驱动器提供了系统化的最优电流参考计算框架，统一处理了磁饱和、交叉耦合和铁损等非线性因素，提高了控制性能。

idea_spark.idea: 在电机控制中，通过分段仿射近似将非线性磁特性纳入二次约束二次规划，实现复杂约束下的最优电流参考计算。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于有限元数据的准确性，分段仿射近似的精度可能影响最终性能；计算复杂度可能随约束数量增加。
idea_spark.inspiration: 该方法可启发其他多自由度电机系统（如双馈电机）的最优控制问题建模，或将类似框架应用于具有饱和与非线性的其他电磁设备优化。

en.tldr: This work generalizes optimal current reference theory to Wound Rotor Synchronous Machines by incorporating rotor-current degree-of-freedom, magnetic saturation, cross-coupling, and core losses, formulating the problem as a quadratically constrained quadratic program within affine flux regions and providing closed-form or low-dimensional polynomial solutions.

en.method: Using a piecewise-affine approximation derived from finite-element data, the optimal current reference problem is formulated as a quadratically constrained quadratic program within affine flux regions, with solutions characterized by active constraint regimes.

The framework extends unified optimal current reference theory to three-degree-of-freedom WRSMs while remaining computationally tractable.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporating nonlinear magnetic characteristics into quadratically constrained quadratic programming via piecewise-affine approximations for optimal current reference computation under complex constraints in motor control.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on accuracy of finite-element data; precision of piecewise-affine approximation may affect performance; computational complexity may increase with number of constraints.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could inspire optimal control modeling for other multi-degree-of-freedom machine systems (e.g., doubly-fed machines) or application of similar frameworks to optimization of other electromagnetic devices with saturation and nonlinearities.

papers.title: GRAB-ANNS: High-Throughput Indexing and Hybrid Search via GPU-Native Bucketing
papers.summary: Hybrid search, which jointly optimizes vector similarity and structured predicate filtering, has become a fundamental building block for modern AI-driven systems. While recent predicate-aware ANN indices improve filtering efficiency on CPUs, their performance is increasingly constrained by limited memory bandwidth and parallelism. Although GPUs offer massive parallelism and superior memory bandwidth, directly porting CPU-centric hybrid search algorithms to GPUs leads to severe performance degradation due to architectural mismatches, including irregular memory access, branch divergence, and excessive CPU-GPU synchronization. In this paper, we present GRAB-ANNS, a high-throughput, GPU-native graph index for dynamic hybrid search. Our key insight is to rethink hybrid indexing from a hardware-first perspective. We introduce a bucket-based memory layout that transforms range predicates into lightweight bucket selection, enabling coalesced memory accesses and efficient SIMT execution. To preserve global navigability under arbitrary filters, we design a hybrid graph topology that combines dense intra-bucket local edges with sparse inter-bucket remote edges. We further develop an append-only update pipeline that supports efficient batched insertions and parallel graph maintenance on GPUs. Extensive experiments on large-scale datasets show that GRAB-ANNS achieves up to 240.1 times higher query throughput and 12.6 times faster index construction than state-of-the-art CPU-based systems, and up to 10 times higher throughput compared to optimized GPU-native reimplementations, while maintaining high recall.

ai.tldr: GRAB-ANNS是一种GPU原生图索引，通过桶式内存布局和混合图拓扑实现高吞吐量混合搜索，显著超越CPU方案。
ai.motivation: CPU为中心的混合搜索算法在GPU上移植时，因内存访问不规则、分支发散和CPU-GPU同步开销导致性能严重下降。
ai.method: 采用硬件优先视角，设计桶式内存布局将范围谓词转换为轻量桶选择，实现合并内存访问；构建结合桶内稠密边与桶间稀疏边的混合图拓扑；开发仅追加更新流水线支持批量插入。
ai.result: 在大规模数据集上，查询吞吐量最高达CPU方案的240.1倍，索引构建快12.6倍，比GPU原生重实现方案吞吐量高10倍，同时保持高召回率。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要低延迟、高吞吐混合搜索的AI系统提供GPU原生索引方案，显著提升向量相似性与结构化过滤联合查询性能。

idea_spark.idea: 将数据库谓词下推思想与GPU内存层次结构结合，用桶选择替代逐元组过滤
idea_spark.risk: 对高度动态或谓词分布极端倾斜的数据集可能需调整桶策略
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索FPGA或其他加速器上的类似硬件感知索引设计，或应用于时序数据库的混合查询

en.tldr: GRAB-ANNS is a GPU-native graph index enabling high-throughput hybrid search via bucket-based memory layout and hybrid graph topology, significantly outperforming CPU-based solutions.
en.motivation: CPU-centric hybrid search algorithms suffer severe performance degradation when ported to GPUs due to irregular memory access, branch divergence, and excessive CPU-GPU synchronization.
en.method: Adopting a hardware-first perspective: designing a bucket-based memory layout that transforms range predicates into lightweight bucket selection for coalesced memory access; constructing a hybrid graph topology combining dense intra-bucket local edges with sparse inter-bucket remote edges; developing an append-only update pipeline for batched insertions.

en.help_to_user: Provides a GPU-native indexing solution for AI systems requiring low-latency, high-throughput hybrid search, significantly improving joint vector similarity and structured predicate filtering queries.

idea_spark.idea: Combining database predicate pushdown concepts with GPU memory hierarchy, replacing per-tuple filtering with bucket selection
idea_spark.risk: May require bucket strategy adjustments for highly dynamic datasets or extremely skewed predicate distributions
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore similar hardware-aware index designs on FPGAs or other accelerators, or apply to hybrid queries in time-series databases

papers.title: Single-Language Evidence Is Insufficient for Automated Logging: A Multilingual Benchmark and Empirical Study with LLMs
papers.summary: Logging statements are central to debugging, failure diagnosis, and production observability, yet writing them requires developers to decide where to place a logging statement, which API and severity level to use, and what runtime information to expose. Automated logging aims to reduce this burden, but existing evidence remains dominated by Java-centric repository-snapshot dataset. It is therefore unclear whether conclusions about model behavior and model selection generalize across programming-language ecosystems or realistic code evolution. This paper presents MultiLogBench, a multilingual benchmark and empirical study spanning six programming language ecosystems. MultiLogBench contains 63,965 production-code repository-snapshot instances, 744 revision-history cases where developers introduce logging statements during maintenance, and a paired transformed revision-history branch for robustness analysis. Using seven contemporary large language models under a unified protocol, we evaluate logging-site localization, framework-anchor matching, severity prediction, message generation, variable recovery, and cascaded overall quality. Results show clear cross-language variation: framework-anchor matching is the most language-sensitive component, loop and nested-callable sites are the hardest structural contexts, and model rankings are stable only at the top tier. These patterns persist at a coarse level on revision-history data, while transformed inputs do not cause a broad same-direction performance collapse. Overall, MultiLogBench shows that robust claims about automated logging require multilingual evaluation and maintenance-oriented validation.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了MultiLogBench，一个涵盖六种编程语言的多语言日志生成基准，并通过实证研究表明单语言证据不足以支撑自动化日志生成的稳健结论。
ai.motivation: 现有的自动化日志研究证据主要集中于Java语言的仓库快照数据集，不清楚其结论是否能推广到其他编程语言生态系统或真实的代码演化场景。
ai.method: 构建MultiLogBench基准，包含63,965个生产代码仓库快照实例、744个开发者在维护中引入日志的修订历史案例，以及用于鲁棒性分析的配对转换修订历史分支。使用七种当代大语言模型，在统一协议下评估日志位置定位、框架锚点匹配、严重性预测、消息生成、变量恢复和级联整体质量。
ai.result: 结果显示明显的跨语言差异：框架锚点匹配是对语言最敏感的组件，循环和嵌套可调用站点是最难处理的结构上下文，模型排名仅在顶级层次保持稳定。这些模式在修订历史数据上大致保持，而转换输入并未导致广泛的同向性能崩溃。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究者和从业者提供了一个全面的多语言基准，用于评估和比较自动化日志生成模型，强调了跨语言评估和维护导向验证的必要性。

idea_spark.idea: 将MultiLogBench的评估框架扩展到更多编程语言或特定领域语言；研究模型在跨语言日志生成任务中的迁移学习能力；探索自动化日志生成与代码理解、缺陷预测等任务的联合建模。
idea_spark.risk: 基准覆盖的语言和项目类型可能仍有局限；模型评估可能受特定提示工程或超参数设置影响；真实世界日志实践的复杂性可能超出当前任务定义。
idea_spark.inspiration: 揭示了自动化软件工程任务评估中普遍存在的单语言偏差问题，为其他代码生成任务（如注释生成、测试生成）的多语言评估提供了方法论参考。

zh.result: 结果显示明显的跨语言差异：框架锚点匹配是对语言最敏感的组件，循环和嵌套可调用站点是最难处理的结构上下文，模型排名仅在顶级层次保持稳定。这些模式在修订历史数据上大致保持，而转换输入并未导致广泛的同向性能崩溃。

idea_spark.idea: 将MultiLogBench的评估框架扩展到更多编程语言或特定领域语言；研究模型在跨语言日志生成任务中的迁移学习能力；探索自动化日志生成与代码理解、缺陷预测等任务的联合建模。
idea_spark.risk: 基准覆盖的语言和项目类型可能仍有局限；模型评估可能受特定提示工程或超参数设置影响；真实世界日志实践的复杂性可能超出当前任务定义。
idea_spark.inspiration: 揭示了自动化软件工程任务评估中普遍存在的单语言偏差问题，为其他代码生成任务（如注释生成、测试生成）的多语言评估提供了方法论参考。

en.tldr: This paper presents MultiLogBench, a multilingual logging benchmark spanning six programming languages, and empirically demonstrates that single-language evidence is insufficient for robust claims about automated logging.
en.motivation: Existing evidence for automated logging is dominated by Java-centric repository-snapshot datasets, leaving it unclear whether conclusions generalize across programming-language ecosystems or realistic code evolution.
en.method: Constructs MultiLogBench containing 63,965 production-code repository-snapshot instances, 744 revision-history cases where developers introduce logging during maintenance, and a paired transformed revision-history branch for robustness analysis. Evaluates seven contemporary LLMs under a unified protocol for logging-site localization, framework-anchor matching, severity prediction, message generation, variable recovery, and cascaded overall quality.
en.result: Results show clear cross-language variation: framework-anchor matching is the most language-sensitive component, loop and nested-callable sites are the hardest structural contexts, and model rankings are stable only at the top tier. These patterns persist coarsely on revision-history data, while transformed inputs do not cause broad same-direction performance collapse.
en.help_to_user: Provides a comprehensive multilingual benchmark for researchers and practitioners to evaluate and compare automated logging models, emphasizing the necessity of cross-language evaluation and maintenance-oriented validation.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the MultiLogBench evaluation framework to more programming languages or domain-specific languages; investigate transfer learning capabilities of models for cross-language logging generation; explore joint modeling of automated logging with code understanding, defect prediction, or other tasks.
idea_spark.risk: Benchmark coverage of languages and project types may still be limited; model evaluation may be influenced by specific prompt engineering or hyperparameter settings; real-world logging practice complexity may exceed current task definitions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Reveals a prevalent single-language bias in the evaluation of automated software engineering tasks, providing a methodological reference for multilingual evaluation of other code generation tasks such as comment generation or test generation.

papers.title: PDDL-Mind: Large Language Models are Capable on Belief Reasoning with Reliable State Tracking
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) perform substantially below human level on existing theory-of-mind (ToM) benchmarks, even when augmented with chain-of-thought prompting or probabilistic belief updates. We argue that these failures primarily arise from unreliable implicit state tracking rather than limitations in high-level reasoning. We introduce PDDL-Mind, a neuro-symbolic framework that decouples environment state evolution from belief inference. By translating narrative descriptions into explicit states and actions expressed in Planning Domain Definition Language (PDDL), and by verifying action-induced state transitions against a predefined domain, PDDL-Mind provides LLMs with a logically consistent and explicit representation of world states for ToM tasks. Experiments on MMToM-QA, MuMA and FanToM show that PDDL-Mind achieves over 5% absolute accuracy gain over the best existing state-of-the-art method on ToM benchmark questions.

ai.tldr: PDDL-Mind 是一个神经符号框架，通过将叙述转换为 PDDL 中的显式状态和动作，为 LLM 提供逻辑一致的世界状态表示，显著提升了 ToM 任务性能。
ai.motivation: 现有 ToM 基准测试中，LLM 表现远低于人类水平，主要原因是隐式状态跟踪不可靠，而非高层推理能力不足。
ai.method: 引入 PDDL-Mind 框架，将环境状态演化与信念推理解耦。将叙述描述翻译为规划领域定义语言（PDDL）中的显式状态和动作，并依据预定义领域验证动作引发的状态转移。
ai.result: 在 MMToM-QA、MuMA 和 FanToM 基准测试上，PDDL-Mind 相比现有最佳方法实现了超过 5% 的绝对准确率提升。
ai.help_to_user: 该框架提供了一种为 LLM 构建逻辑一致、显式世界状态表示的方法，可用于提升需要复杂状态跟踪的推理任务性能。

idea_spark.idea: 在其他需要精确状态建模的领域（如指令跟随、多轮对话状态管理）中，应用类似的神经符号解耦框架。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于预定义的 PDDL 领域模型，可能限制其对开放域或动态变化环境的泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 通过分离状态跟踪与推理，可以更清晰地诊断和提升 AI 系统在需要心理状态理解的任务中的表现。

en.tldr: PDDL-Mind is a neuro-symbolic framework that translates narratives into explicit states and actions in PDDL, providing LLMs with a logically consistent world state representation, significantly improving performance on ToM tasks.
en.motivation: LLMs perform substantially below human level on existing ToM benchmarks, primarily due to unreliable implicit state tracking rather than limitations in high-level reasoning.
en.method: Introduces the PDDL-Mind framework that decouples environment state evolution from belief inference. It translates narrative descriptions into explicit states and actions expressed in Planning Domain Definition Language (PDDL) and verifies action-induced state transitions against a predefined domain.
en.result: Experiments on MMToM-QA, MuMA, and FanToM show that PDDL-Mind achieves over 5% absolute accuracy gain over the best existing state-of-the-art method.
en.help_to_user: This framework provides a method for constructing logically consistent, explicit world state representations for LLMs, useful for improving performance on reasoning tasks requiring complex state tracking.

idea_spark.idea: Apply a similar neuro-symbolic decoupling framework in other domains requiring precise state modeling, such as instruction following or multi-turn dialogue state management.
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on predefined PDDL domain models may limit its generalization to open-domain or dynamically changing environments.
idea_spark.inspiration: By separating state tracking from reasoning, one can more clearly diagnose and improve AI system performance on tasks requiring mental state understanding.

papers.title: HeLa-Mem: Hebbian Learning and Associative Memory for LLM Agents
papers.summary: Long-term memory is a critical challenge for Large Language Model agents, as fixed context windows cannot preserve coherence across extended interactions. Existing memory systems represent conversation history as unstructured embedding vectors, retrieving information through semantic similarity. This paradigm fails to capture the associative structure of human memory, wherein related experiences progressively strengthen interconnections through repeated co-activation. Inspired by cognitive neuroscience, we identify three mechanisms central to biological memory: association, consolidation, and spreading activation, which remain largely absent in current research.   To bridge this gap, we propose HeLa-Mem, a bio-inspired memory architecture that models memory as a dynamic graph with Hebbian learning dynamics. HeLa-Mem employs a dual-level organization: (1) an episodic memory graph that evolves through co-activation patterns, and (2) a semantic memory store populated via Hebbian Distillation, wherein a Reflective Agent identifies densely connected memory hubs and distills them into structured, reusable semantic knowledge. This dual-path design leverages both semantic similarity and learned associations, mirroring the episodic-semantic distinction in human cognition. Experiments on LoCoMo demonstrate superior performance across four question categories while using significantly fewer context tokens. Code is available on GitHub: https://github.com/ReinerBRO/HeLa-Mem

ai.tldr: HeLa-Mem是一种受生物记忆启发的LLM智能体长期记忆架构，通过赫布学习和动态图建模关联记忆，实现双层级（情景与语义）记忆组织与巩固。
ai.motivation: 现有LLM智能体的记忆系统仅依赖语义相似性检索，无法模拟人类记忆的关联结构、巩固和扩散激活机制，导致长程交互中缺乏连贯性。
ai.method: 提出双层级记忆架构：1）通过共激活模式演化的情景记忆图；2）通过赫布蒸馏提取密集连接记忆枢纽形成的语义记忆库。采用反射智能体识别并蒸馏结构化语义知识。
ai.result: 在LoCoMo基准测试中，在四类问题上表现优异，且上下文令牌使用量显著减少。
ai.help_to_user: 为构建具有生物合理记忆机制的LLM智能体提供可扩展的架构，改善长程交互的连贯性和知识复用效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将记忆巩固过程建模为图上的社区检测与知识蒸馏，实现从情景到语义的渐进式结构化。
idea_spark.risk: 图动态可能引入记忆噪声或过度巩固；蒸馏过程依赖反射智能体的可靠性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 认知神经科学的记忆机制（如整合、扩散激活）可为AI记忆系统提供原则性设计蓝图。

zh.tldr: HeLa-Mem是一种受生物记忆启发的LLM智能体长期记忆架构，通过赫布学习和动态图建模关联记忆，实现双层级（情景与语义）记忆组织与巩固。

zh.help_to_user: 为构建具有生物合理记忆机制的LLM智能体提供可扩展的架构，改善长程交互的连贯性和知识复用效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将记忆巩固过程建模为图上的社区检测与知识蒸馏，实现从情景到语义的渐进式结构化。
idea_spark.risk: 图动态可能引入记忆噪声或过度巩固；蒸馏过程依赖反射智能体的可靠性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 认知神经科学的记忆机制（如整合、扩散激活）可为AI记忆系统提供原则性设计蓝图。

en.tldr: HeLa-Mem is a bio-inspired long-term memory architecture for LLM agents that models associative memory via Hebbian learning and dynamic graphs, implementing dual-level (episodic and semantic) memory organization and consolidation.
en.motivation: Existing memory systems for LLM agents rely solely on semantic similarity retrieval, failing to capture the associative structure, consolidation, and spreading activation mechanisms of human memory, leading to incoherence in extended interactions.
en.method: Proposes a dual-level memory architecture: 1) an episodic memory graph evolving through co-activation patterns, and 2) a semantic memory store populated via Hebbian Distillation, where a Reflective Agent identifies densely connected memory hubs and distills them into structured, reusable semantic knowledge.
en.result: Demonstrates superior performance across four question categories on the LoCoMo benchmark while using significantly fewer context tokens.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable architecture for building LLM agents with biologically plausible memory mechanisms, improving coherence in long-term interactions and knowledge reuse efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Model memory consolidation as community detection and knowledge distillation on graphs, enabling progressive structuring from episodic to semantic memory.
idea_spark.risk: Graph dynamics may introduce memory noise or over-consolidation; the distillation process depends on the reliability of the Reflective Agent.
idea_spark.inspiration: Memory mechanisms from cognitive neuroscience (e.g., consolidation, spreading activation) can provide principled design blueprints for AI memory systems.

papers.title: D-Prism: Differentiable Primitives for Structured Dynamic Modeling
papers.summary: Capturing both geometry and rigid motion for structured dynamic objects, like multi-part assemblies or jointed mechanisms, remains a key challenge. Existing dynamic methods, such as deformable meshes or 3DGS, rely on unstructured representations and fail to jointly model suitable geometry and articulated motion. Primitive-based methods excel at structured static scenes, but their dynamic potential is still unexplored. We propose D-Prism, the first framework to achieve high-fidelity structured dynamic modeling by extending differentiable primitives to the dynamic domain. Specifically, we bind 3DGS to primitive surfaces, leveraging their respective strengths in appearance and geometry. We introduce a deformation network to control primitive motion, ensuring it accurately matches the object's movement. Furthermore, we design a novel adaptive control strategy to dynamically adjust primitive counts, better matching objects' true spatial footprint. Experiments confirm that our method excels at structured dynamic modeling, providing both structured geometry and precise motion tracking.

ai.tldr: D-Prism是首个将可微基元扩展到动态领域的框架，通过将3DGS绑定到基元表面并引入变形网络与自适应控制策略，实现了对结构化动态物体的高保真几何与运动联合建模。
ai.motivation: 现有动态建模方法（如可变形网格或3DGS）依赖非结构化表示，无法同时建模合适的几何与铰接运动；而基于基元的方法虽擅长静态场景，但其动态潜力尚未开发。
ai.method: 将3D高斯溅射（3DGS）绑定到可微基元表面以结合外观与几何优势；引入变形网络控制基元运动；设计自适应控制策略动态调整基元数量以匹配物体真实空间范围。
ai.result: 实验证实该方法在结构化动态建模中表现出色，能同时提供结构化几何与精确运动跟踪。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要建模多部件装配体或关节机构等结构化动态物体的研究人员提供了首个可微基元动态框架，实现了几何与刚性运动的联合捕获。

idea_spark.idea: 用可微基元作为几何骨架，绑定神经渲染表示（3DGS）进行外观建模，通过网络控制基元运动以实现结构化动态建模。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于准确的基元初始化与绑定，对高度非刚性或拓扑变化的物体可能适用性有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将传统几何表示（如基元、体素）与神经场/渲染技术结合，并引入结构化运动先验，以提升动态场景建模的规整性与效率。

zh.tldr: D-Prism是首个将可微基元扩展到动态领域的框架，通过将3DGS绑定到基元表面并引入变形网络与自适应控制策略，实现了对结构化动态物体的高保真几何与运动联合建模。
zh.motivation: 现有动态建模方法（如可变形网格或3DGS）依赖非结构化表示，无法同时建模合适的几何与铰接运动；而基于基元的方法虽擅长静态场景，但其动态潜力尚未开发。

zh.help_to_user: 为需要建模多部件装配体或关节机构等结构化动态物体的研究人员提供了首个可微基元动态框架，实现了几何与刚性运动的联合捕获。

idea_spark.idea: 用可微基元作为几何骨架，绑定神经渲染表示（3DGS）进行外观建模，通过网络控制基元运动以实现结构化动态建模。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于准确的基元初始化与绑定，对高度非刚性或拓扑变化的物体可能适用性有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将传统几何表示（如基元、体素）与神经场/渲染技术结合，并引入结构化运动先验，以提升动态场景建模的规整性与效率。

en.tldr: D-Prism is the first framework to extend differentiable primitives to the dynamic domain, achieving high-fidelity joint modeling of geometry and motion for structured dynamic objects by binding 3DGS to primitive surfaces and introducing a deformation network with adaptive control.
en.motivation: Existing dynamic methods (e.g., deformable meshes or 3DGS) rely on unstructured representations and fail to jointly model suitable geometry and articulated motion, while primitive-based methods excel in static scenes but their dynamic potential remains unexplored.
en.method: Bind 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) to differentiable primitive surfaces to leverage their respective strengths in appearance and geometry; introduce a deformation network to control primitive motion; design an adaptive control strategy to dynamically adjust primitive counts to match objects' true spatial footprint.
en.result: Experiments confirm that the method excels at structured dynamic modeling, providing both structured geometry and precise motion tracking.
en.help_to_user: Provides the first differentiable primitive framework for dynamic modeling of structured dynamic objects like multi-part assemblies or jointed mechanisms, enabling joint capture of geometry and rigid motion.

idea_spark.idea: Use differentiable primitives as a geometric skeleton, bind neural rendering representations (3DGS) for appearance modeling, and control primitive motion via a network to achieve structured dynamic modeling.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on accurate primitive initialization and binding; may have limited applicability to highly non-rigid or topologically changing objects.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining traditional geometric representations (e.g., primitives, voxels) with neural field/rendering techniques and incorporating structured motion priors to improve regularity and efficiency in dynamic scene modeling.

papers.title: Coevolving Representations in Joint Image-Feature Diffusion
papers.summary: Joint image-feature generative modeling has recently emerged as an effective strategy for improving diffusion training by coupling low-level VAE latents with high-level semantic features extracted from pre-trained visual encoders. However, existing approaches rely on a fixed representation space, constructed independently of the generative objective and kept unchanged during training. We argue that the representation space guiding diffusion should itself adapt to the generative task. To this end, we propose Coevolving Representation Diffusion (CoReDi), a framework in which the semantic representation space evolves during training by learning a lightweight linear projection jointly with the diffusion model. While naively optimizing this projection leads to degenerate solutions, we show that stable coevolution can be achieved through a combination of stop-gradient targets, normalization, and targeted regularization that prevents feature collapse. This formulation enables the semantic space to progressively specialize to the needs of image synthesis, improving its complementarity with image latents. We apply CoReDi to both VAE latent diffusion and pixel-space diffusion, demonstrating that adaptive semantic representations improve generative modeling across both settings. Experiments show that CoReDi achieves faster convergence and higher sample quality compared to joint diffusion models operating in fixed representation spaces.

ai.tldr: 提出CoReDi框架，通过联合训练轻量级线性投影使语义表示空间在扩散训练中自适应演化，提升图像生成质量。
ai.motivation: 现有联合图像-特征生成方法依赖固定的表示空间，该空间独立于生成目标构建且在训练中保持不变。我们认为指导扩散的表示空间应适应生成任务。
ai.method: 在扩散模型训练期间，通过结合停止梯度目标、归一化和防止特征崩溃的正则化，联合学习轻量级线性投影，使语义表示空间稳定协同演化。
ai.result: CoReDi在VAE潜在扩散和像素空间扩散中均实现更快收敛和更高样本质量，优于使用固定表示空间的联合扩散模型。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要提升扩散模型生成质量的研究者提供了一种通过表示空间自适应优化来增强语义引导的新框架。

idea_spark.idea: 在训练中动态调整特征提取器的投影，使语义空间专门化以适应生成目标。
idea_spark.risk: 若无适当正则化，优化投影可能导致特征崩溃或表示退化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考其他生成模型中固定预训练特征是否限制了性能，以及如何安全地使其适应任务。

zh.tldr: 提出CoReDi框架，通过联合训练轻量级线性投影使语义表示空间在扩散训练中自适应演化，提升图像生成质量。

zh.result: CoReDi在VAE潜在扩散和像素空间扩散中均实现更快收敛和更高样本质量，优于使用固定表示空间的联合扩散模型。

idea_spark.idea: 在训练中动态调整特征提取器的投影，使语义空间专门化以适应生成目标。
idea_spark.risk: 若无适当正则化，优化投影可能导致特征崩溃或表示退化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发我们思考其他生成模型中固定预训练特征是否限制了性能，以及如何安全地使其适应任务。

en.tldr: Proposes CoReDi framework where semantic representation space evolves during diffusion training via a jointly learned lightweight linear projection, improving image generation quality.
en.motivation: Existing joint image-feature generative methods rely on a fixed representation space constructed independently of the generative objective and kept unchanged during training. We argue the guiding representation space should adapt to the generative task.
en.method: Learns a lightweight linear projection jointly with the diffusion model, enabling stable coevolution of the semantic space through stop-gradient targets, normalization, and regularization preventing feature collapse.
en.result: CoReDi achieves faster convergence and higher sample quality in both VAE latent diffusion and pixel-space diffusion, outperforming joint diffusion models using fixed representation spaces.
en.help_to_user: Provides a novel framework for enhancing semantic guidance in diffusion models through adaptive representation space optimization, useful for researchers seeking to improve generative quality.

idea_spark.idea: Dynamically adjust the projection of feature extractors during training to specialize the semantic space for the generation objective.
idea_spark.risk: Optimizing the projection without proper regularization may lead to feature collapse or representation degeneration.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires consideration of whether fixed pretrained features limit performance in other generative models, and how to safely adapt them to the task.

papers.title: PlankFormer: Robust Plankton Instance Segmentation via MAE-Pretrained Vision Transformers and Pseudo Community Image Generation
papers.summary: Plankton monitoring is essential for assessing aquatic ecosystems but is limited by the labor-intensive nature of manual microscopic analysis. Automating the segmentation of plankton from crowded images is crucial, however, it faces two major challenges: (i) the scarcity of pixel-level annotated datasets and (ii) the difficulty of distinguishing plankton from debris and overlapping individuals using conventional CNN-based methods. To address these issues, we propose PlankFormer, a novel framework for plankton instance segmentation. First, to overcome the data shortage, we introduce a method to generate labeled Pseudo Community Images (PCI) by synthesizing individual plankton images onto diverse backgrounds, including those created by generative models. Second, we propose a segmentation model utilizing a Vision Transformer (ViT) backbone with a Mask2Former decoder. To robustly capture the global structural features of plankton against occlusion and debris, we employ a Masked Autoencoder (MAE) for self-supervised pre-training on unlabeled individual images. Experimental results on real-world datasets demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms conventional methods, such as Mask R-CNN, particularly in challenging environments with high debris density. We demonstrate that our synthetic training strategy and MAE-based architecture enable high-precision segmentation with requiring less manual annotations for individual plankton images.

ai.tldr: PlankFormer：一种基于MAE预训练视觉Transformer和伪群落图像生成的鲁棒性浮游生物实例分割框架，显著减少对人工标注的依赖。
ai.motivation: 浮游生物监测对评估水生态系统至关重要，但传统手动显微分析劳动密集。自动化分割面临两大挑战：像素级标注数据稀缺；传统CNN方法难以区分浮游生物与碎片、重叠个体。
ai.method: 1. 提出伪群落图像生成方法，将单个浮游生物图像合成到多样化背景上以扩充标注数据。2. 构建基于视觉Transformer骨干网络和Mask2Former解码器的分割模型，并采用掩码自编码器在无标注个体图像上进行自监督预训练，以鲁棒捕捉全局结构特征。
ai.result: 在真实数据集上的实验表明，该方法显著优于传统方法（如Mask R-CNN），尤其在碎片密度高的挑战性环境中表现突出，实现了高精度分割且所需人工标注更少。
ai.help_to_user: 为浮游生物监测提供了一种自动化、高精度的实例分割解决方案，有效缓解数据标注瓶颈并提升在复杂场景下的鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 结合生成式模型创建逼真合成场景与自监督学习利用无标注数据，共同应对标注数据稀缺与模型泛化挑战。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据与真实数据的分布差异可能影响模型在极端未知场景下的性能；ViT计算开销需考虑部署可行性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将自监督预训练与特定领域数据增强结合，构建数据高效且鲁棒的视觉识别系统。

en.tldr: PlankFormer: a robust plankton instance segmentation framework via MAE-pretrained Vision Transformers and pseudo community image generation, significantly reducing reliance on manual annotation.
en.motivation: Plankton monitoring is essential for aquatic ecosystem assessment but limited by labor-intensive manual microscopy. Automated segmentation faces two major challenges: scarcity of pixel-level annotated data, and difficulty distinguishing plankton from debris/overlapping individuals with conventional CNNs.
en.method: 1. Propose pseudo community image generation by synthesizing individual plankton images onto diverse backgrounds (including generative model outputs) to expand labeled data. 2. Develop a segmentation model with a Vision Transformer backbone and Mask2Former decoder, employing Masked Autoencoder for self-supervised pre-training on unlabeled individual images to robustly capture global structural features.
en.result: Experiments on real-world datasets show the method significantly outperforms conventional methods like Mask R-CNN, especially in high-debris environments, achieving high-precision segmentation with reduced manual annotation requirements.
en.help_to_user: Provides an automated, high-precision instance segmentation solution for plankton monitoring, effectively alleviating data annotation bottlenecks and improving robustness in complex scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Combine generative models for realistic synthetic scenes with self-supervised learning on unlabeled data to jointly address annotation scarcity and model generalization challenges.
idea_spark.risk: Distribution gap between synthetic and real data may affect performance in extreme unseen scenarios; ViT computational overhead requires deployment consideration.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires integrating self-supervised pre-training with domain-specific data augmentation to build data-efficient and robust visual recognition systems.

papers.title: Multimodal Fusion of Histopathology Images and Electronic Health Records for Early Breast Cancer Diagnosis
papers.summary: Breast cancer is a leading cause of cancer-related mortality worldwide, and timely accurate diagnosis is critical to improving survival outcomes. While convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have demonstrated strong performance on histopathology image classification, and machine learning models on structured electronic health records (EHR) have shown utility for clinical risk stratification, most existing work treats these modalities in isolation. This paper presents a systematic multimodal framework that integrates patch-level histopathology features from the BreCaHAD dataset with structured clinical data from MIMIC-IV. We train and evaluate unimodal image models (a simple CNN baseline and ResNet-18 with transfer learning), unimodal tabular models (XGBoost and a multilayer perceptron), and an intermediate-fusion model that concatenates latent representations from both modalities. ResNet-18 achieves near-perfect accuracy (1.000) and AUC (1.000) on three-class patch-level classification, while XGBoost achieves 98% accuracy on the EHR prediction task. The intermediate fusion model yields a macro-average AUC of 0.997, outperforming all unimodal baselines and delivering the largest improvements on the diagnostically critical but class-imbalanced mitosis category (AUC 0.994). Grad-CAM and SHAP interpretability analyses validate that model decisions align with established pathological and clinical criteria. Our results demonstrate that multimodal integration delivers meaningful improvements in both predictive performance and clinical transparency.

ai.tldr: 本文提出一种融合组织病理学图像与电子健康记录的多模态框架，用于乳腺癌早期诊断，在关键类别上超越单模态基线。
ai.motivation: 乳腺癌诊断中，现有工作通常孤立处理图像与临床数据，缺乏有效整合。
ai.method: 使用BreCaHAD数据集提取图像块特征，结合MIMIC-IV结构化临床数据；训练单模态图像模型（CNN、ResNet-18）与表格模型（XGBoost、MLP），并设计中间融合模型拼接两种模态的潜在表示。
ai.result: ResNet-18在图像分类上达到近乎完美的准确率（1.000）与AUC（1.000）；XGBoost在EHR预测上准确率达98%；中间融合模型的宏观平均AUC为0.997，在关键的有丝分裂类别上提升最显著（AUC 0.994）。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种可复现的多模态融合框架，证明了整合图像与临床数据能提升诊断性能与可解释性。

idea_spark.idea: 在模型潜在表示层进行融合，而非早期（原始数据）或晚期（决策层）融合。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖特定数据集（BreCaHAD、MIMIC-IV），泛化能力需在更多临床环境中验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索更复杂的融合机制（如注意力）或纳入更多模态（如基因组数据）。

en.tldr: This paper presents a multimodal framework fusing histopathology images and electronic health records for early breast cancer diagnosis, outperforming unimodal baselines on critical categories.
en.motivation: Existing breast cancer diagnosis work often treats imaging and clinical data in isolation, lacking effective integration.
en.method: Extract patch-level features from the BreCaHAD dataset and combine with structured clinical data from MIMIC-IV; train unimodal image models (CNN, ResNet-18) and tabular models (XGBoost, MLP), and design an intermediate-fusion model that concatenates latent representations from both modalities.
en.result: ResNet-18 achieves near-perfect accuracy (1.000) and AUC (1.000) on image classification; XGBoost achieves 98% accuracy on EHR prediction; the intermediate fusion model yields a macro-average AUC of 0.997, with the largest improvement on the critical mitosis category (AUC 0.994).
en.help_to_user: Provides a reproducible multimodal fusion framework demonstrating that integrating imaging and clinical data improves diagnostic performance and interpretability.

idea_spark.idea: Fuse at the latent representation level rather than early (raw data) or late (decision) fusion.
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on specific datasets (BreCaHAD, MIMIC-IV); generalization needs validation in more clinical settings.
idea_spark.inspiration: Could explore more complex fusion mechanisms (e.g., attention) or incorporate additional modalities (e.g., genomic data).

papers.title: Unified Ultrasound Intelligence Toward an End-to-End Agentic System
papers.summary: Clinical ultrasound analysis demands models that generalize across heterogeneous organs, views, and devices, while supporting interpretable workflow-level analysis. Existing methods often rely on task-wise adaptation, and joint learning may be unstable due to cross-task interference, making it hard to deliver workflow-level outputs in practice. To address these challenges, we present USTri, a tri-stage ultrasound intelligence pipeline for unified multi-organ, multi-task analysis. Stage I trains a universal generalist USGen on different domains to learn broad, transferable priors that are robust to device and protocol variability. To better handle domain shifts and reach task-aligned performance while preserving ultrasound shared knowledge, Stage II builds USpec by keeping USGen frozen and finetuning dataset-specific heads. Stage III introduces USAgent, which mimics clinician workflows by orchestrating USpec specialists for multi-step inference and deterministic structured reports. On the FMC\_UIA validation set, our model achieves the best overall performance across 4 task types and 27 datasets, outperforming state-of-the-art methods. Moreover, qualitative results show that USAgent produces clinically structured reports with high accuracy and interpretability. Our study suggests a scalable path to ultrasound intelligence that generalizes across heterogeneous ultrasound tasks and supports consistent end-to-end clinical workflows.

ai.tldr: 提出USTri三阶段超声智能管道，通过通用模型、专用微调和智能体编排，实现跨器官、跨任务、跨设备的统一分析，并生成结构化临床报告。
ai.motivation: 临床超声分析需要模型能泛化到不同器官、视图和设备，并支持可解释的工作流级分析。现有方法依赖任务级适配，联合学习不稳定，难以输出工作流级结果。
ai.method: 第一阶段训练通用模型USGen学习跨域可迁移先验；第二阶段冻结USGen，微调数据集专用头得到USpec；第三阶段引入USAgent，编排USpec专家进行多步推理，生成确定性结构化报告。
ai.result: 在FMC_UIA验证集上，模型在4种任务类型和27个数据集上取得最佳整体性能，定性结果显示USAgent能生成高精度、高可解释性的临床结构化报告。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一个可扩展的超声智能路径，能泛化到异构超声任务，并支持一致的端到端临床工作流。

idea_spark.idea: 将通用知识学习、领域适应与临床工作流模拟解耦，通过冻结共享主干保留知识，避免任务间干扰。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖于大量异构数据训练通用模型；智能体编排逻辑需要临床先验知识定义。
idea_spark.inspiration: 模仿临床医生工作流，将多步推理和报告生成自动化，提升诊断流程的连贯性和可解释性。

en.tldr: Proposes USTri, a tri-stage ultrasound intelligence pipeline that achieves unified multi-organ, multi-task, multi-device analysis and generates structured clinical reports via a universal model, specialized fine-tuning, and agentic orchestration.
en.motivation: Clinical ultrasound analysis requires models that generalize across heterogeneous organs, views, and devices while supporting interpretable workflow-level analysis. Existing methods rely on task-wise adaptation, and joint learning is unstable, making workflow-level outputs hard to deliver.
en.method: Stage I trains a universal generalist USGen to learn broad, transferable priors. Stage II builds USpec by keeping USGen frozen and fine-tuning dataset-specific heads. Stage III introduces USAgent, which orchestrates USpec specialists for multi-step inference and deterministic structured reports.
en.result: On the FMC_UIA validation set, the model achieves the best overall performance across 4 task types and 27 datasets. Qualitative results show USAgent produces clinically structured reports with high accuracy and interpretability.
en.help_to_user: Provides a scalable path to ultrasound intelligence that generalizes across heterogeneous ultrasound tasks and supports consistent end-to-end clinical workflows.

idea_spark.idea: Decouple universal knowledge learning, domain adaptation, and clinical workflow simulation by freezing the shared backbone to preserve knowledge and avoid cross-task interference.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on large-scale heterogeneous data for training the universal model; agent orchestration logic requires clinical prior knowledge to define.
idea_spark.inspiration: Mimics clinician workflows to automate multi-step inference and report generation, enhancing the coherence and interpretability of the diagnostic process.

papers.title: Incoherent Deformation, Not Capacity: Diagnosing and Mitigating Overfitting in Dynamic Gaussian Splatting
papers.summary: Dynamic 3D Gaussian Splatting methods achieve strong training-view PSNR on monocular video but generalize poorly: on the D-NeRF benchmark we measure an average train-test PSNR gap of 6.18 dB, rising to 11 dB on individual scenes. We report two findings that together account for most of that gap.   Finding 1 (the role of splitting). A systematic ablation of the Adaptive Density Control pipeline (split, clone, prune, frequency, threshold, schedule) shows that splitting is responsible for over 80% of the gap: disabling split collapses the cloud from 44K to 3K Gaussians and the gap from 6.18 dB to 1.15 dB. Across all threshold-varying ablations, gap is log-linear in count (r = 0.995, bootstrap 95% CI [0.99, 1.00]), which suggests a capacity-based explanation.   Finding 2 (the role of deformation coherence). We show that the capacity explanation is incomplete. A local-smoothness penalty on the per-Gaussian deformation field -- Elastic Energy Regularization (EER) -- reduces the gap by 40.8% while growing the cloud by 85%. Measuring per-Gaussian strain directly on trained checkpoints, EER reduces mean strain by 99.72% (median 99.80%) across all 8 scenes; on 8/8 scenes the median Gaussian under EER is less strained than the 1st-percentile (best-behaved) Gaussian under baseline. Alongside EER, we evaluate two further regularizers: GAD, a loss-rate-aware densification threshold, and PTDrop, a jitter-weighted Gaussian dropout. GAD+EER reduces the gap by 48%; adding PTDrop and a soft growth cap reaches 57%. We confirm that coherence generalizes to (a) a different deformation architecture (Deformable-3DGS, +40.6% gap reduction at re-tuned lambda), and (b) real monocular video (4 HyperNeRF scenes, reducing the mean PSNR gap by 14.9% at the same lambda as D-NeRF, with near-zero quality cost). The overfitting in dynamic 3DGS is driven by incoherent deformation, not parameter count.

ai.tldr: 研究发现动态3D高斯泼溅方法在单目视频训练中泛化差的主要原因是变形场不连贯，而非参数容量。提出弹性能量正则化等方法，将泛化差距降低57%。
ai.motivation: 动态3D高斯泼溅方法在训练视角PSNR表现良好，但在测试时泛化性能显著下降（D-NeRF基准平均差距6.18 dB），需要诊断和缓解过拟合问题。
ai.method: 1) 系统分析自适应密度控制流程，发现分裂操作导致80%以上泛化差距；2) 提出弹性能量正则化（EER）对每个高斯的变形场施加局部平滑惩罚；3) 评估GAD（损失率感知密度阈值）和PTDrop（抖动加权高斯丢弃）等正则化方法。
ai.result: EER将泛化差距降低40.8%，同时高斯云增长85%；GAD+EER降低48%；完整方案降低57%。EER使平均应变降低99.72%，在8个场景中，EER下中位数高斯比基线最佳1%高斯的应变更小。方法在Deformable-3DGS架构和真实单目视频（HyperNeRF场景）上同样有效。
ai.help_to_user: 为动态神经渲染中的过拟合问题提供了新的诊断视角和有效的正则化解决方案，特别关注变形场的物理合理性而非单纯控制参数数量。

idea_spark.idea: 在动态3D重建中，对变形场施加基于物理的弹性能量约束，强制相邻高斯点变形协调一致。
idea_spark.risk: 正则化强度需要仔细调参；弹性模型假设可能不适用于所有类型的非刚性变形。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从连续介质力学角度思考神经渲染中的变形表示，将物理先验引入数据驱动的重建过程。

zh.result: EER将泛化差距降低40.8%，同时高斯云增长85%；GAD+EER降低48%；完整方案降低57%。EER使平均应变降低99.72%，在8个场景中，EER下中位数高斯比基线最佳1%高斯的应变更小。方法在Deformable-3DGS架构和真实单目视频（HyperNeRF场景）上同样有效。
zh.help_to_user: 为动态神经渲染中的过拟合问题提供了新的诊断视角和有效的正则化解决方案，特别关注变形场的物理合理性而非单纯控制参数数量。

idea_spark.idea: 在动态3D重建中，对变形场施加基于物理的弹性能量约束，强制相邻高斯点变形协调一致。
idea_spark.risk: 正则化强度需要仔细调参；弹性模型假设可能不适用于所有类型的非刚性变形。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从连续介质力学角度思考神经渲染中的变形表示，将物理先验引入数据驱动的重建过程。

en.tldr: The study finds that poor generalization in dynamic 3D Gaussian Splatting is primarily caused by incoherent deformation fields rather than parameter capacity. Elastic Energy Regularization and related methods reduce the generalization gap by up to 57%.
en.motivation: Dynamic 3D Gaussian Splatting methods achieve strong training-view PSNR but generalize poorly to novel views (average 6.18 dB gap on D-NeRF benchmark), requiring diagnosis and mitigation of overfitting.
en.method: 1) Systematic ablation of Adaptive Density Control shows splitting accounts for >80% of the gap; 2) Propose Elastic Energy Regularization (EER) imposing local smoothness penalty on per-Gaussian deformation fields; 3) Evaluate additional regularizers: GAD (loss-rate-aware densification threshold) and PTDrop (jitter-weighted Gaussian dropout).
en.result: EER reduces generalization gap by 40.8% while growing Gaussian cloud by 85%; GAD+EER reduces gap by 48%; full combination reaches 57% reduction. EER reduces mean strain by 99.72% across all scenes. The median Gaussian under EER is less strained than the best 1% Gaussian under baseline in all 8 scenes. Methods generalize to different deformation architectures and real monocular video.
en.help_to_user: Provides new diagnostic perspective and effective regularization solutions for overfitting in dynamic neural rendering, focusing on physical plausibility of deformation fields rather than mere parameter count control.

idea_spark.idea: Impose physics-based elastic energy constraints on deformation fields in dynamic 3D reconstruction, enforcing coordinated deformation of neighboring Gaussians.
idea_spark.risk: Regularization strength requires careful tuning; elastic model assumptions may not hold for all non-rigid deformations.
idea_spark.inspiration: Consider deformation representation in neural rendering from continuum mechanics perspective, incorporating physical priors into data-driven reconstruction.

papers.title: Trustworthy Endoscopic Super-Resolution
papers.summary: Super-resolution (SR) models are attracting growing interest for enhancing minimally invasive surgery and diagnostic videos under hardware constraints. However, valid concerns remain regarding the introduction of hallucinated structures and amplified noise, limiting their reliability in safety-critical settings. We propose a direct and practical framework to make SR systems more trustworthy by identifying where reconstructions are likely to fail. Our approach integrates a lightweight error-prediction network that operates on intermediate representations to estimate pixel-wise reconstruction error. The module is computationally efficient and low-latency, making it suitable for real-time deployment. We convert these predictions into operational failure decisions by constructing Conformal Failure Masks (CFM), which localize regions where the SR output should not be trusted. Built on conformal risk control principles, our method provides theoretical guarantees for controlling both the tolerated error limit and the miscoverage in detected failures. We evaluate our approach on image and video SR, demonstrating its effectiveness in detecting unreliable reconstructions in endoscopic and robotic surgery settings. To our knowledge, this is the first study to provide a model-agnostic, theoretically grounded approach to improving the safety of real-time endoscopic image SR.

ai.tldr: 提出一种轻量级误差预测网络与保形失效掩码框架，为内窥镜超分辨率系统提供理论保证的不可信区域定位，提升实时手术视频增强的安全性。
ai.motivation: 超分辨率模型在硬件受限的微创手术视频增强中应用广泛，但存在幻觉结构与噪声放大风险，在安全关键场景中可靠性不足。
ai.method: 集成轻量级误差预测网络，基于中间特征估计像素级重建误差；构建保形失效掩码，将误差预测转换为具有理论保证的失效区域定位。
ai.result: 在图像与视频超分辨率任务中验证了方法有效性，能可靠检测内窥镜与机器人手术场景中的不可信重建区域。
ai.help_to_user: 为内窥镜超分辨率系统提供实时、模型无关的可靠性评估工具，帮助医生识别可能失真的区域，降低临床误判风险。

idea_spark.idea: 在生成式图像增强模型中嵌入实时误差估计与理论保证的失效检测，构建“自知不可信”的AI系统。
idea_spark.risk: 误差预测网络本身可能存在偏差，且保形保证依赖于校准数据分布，分布外场景性能可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将统计学习中的保形预测与计算机视觉任务结合，为安全关键应用提供可量化的可靠性指标。

en.tldr: Proposes a lightweight error-prediction network and conformal failure mask framework to localize untrustworthy regions in endoscopic super-resolution with theoretical guarantees, enhancing safety for real-time surgical video enhancement.
en.motivation: Super-resolution models are increasingly used for minimally invasive surgery video enhancement under hardware constraints, but concerns about hallucinated structures and amplified noise limit reliability in safety-critical settings.
en.method: Integrates a lightweight error-prediction network operating on intermediate features to estimate pixel-wise reconstruction error; constructs Conformal Failure Masks to convert predictions into theoretically-grounded failure localization.
en.result: Demonstrates effectiveness in detecting unreliable reconstructions in endoscopic and robotic surgery settings for both image and video super-resolution tasks.
en.help_to_user: Provides a real-time, model-agnostic reliability assessment tool for endoscopic SR systems, helping clinicians identify potentially distorted regions and reducing clinical misinterpretation risks.

idea_spark.idea: Embed real-time error estimation and theoretically-grounded failure detection into generative image enhancement models, building 'self-aware unreliable' AI systems.
idea_spark.risk: The error-prediction network may itself be biased, and conformal guarantees depend on calibration data distribution, with potential performance drops in out-of-distribution scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining conformal prediction from statistical learning with computer vision tasks to provide quantifiable reliability metrics for safety-critical applications.

papers.title: Beyond Pattern Matching: Seven Cross-Domain Techniques for Prompt Injection Detection
papers.summary: Current open-source prompt-injection detectors converge on two architectural choices: regular-expression pattern matching and fine-tuned transformer classifiers. Both share failure modes that recent work has made concrete. Regular expressions miss paraphrased attacks. Fine-tuned classifiers are vulnerable to adaptive adversaries: a 2025 NAACL Findings study reported that eight published indirect-injection defenses were bypassed with greater than fifty percent attack success rates under adaptive attacks. This work proposes seven detection techniques that each port a specific mechanism from a discipline outside large-language-model security: forensic linguistics, materials-science fatigue analysis, deception technology from network security, local-sequence alignment from bioinformatics, mechanism design from economics, spectral signal analysis from epidemiology, and taint tracking from compiler theory. Three of the seven techniques are implemented in the prompt-shield v0.4.1 release (Apache 2.0) and evaluated in a four-configuration ablation across six datasets including deepset/prompt-injections, NotInject, LLMail-Inject, AgentHarm, and AgentDojo. The local-alignment detector lifts F1 on deepset from 0.033 to 0.378 with zero additional false positives. The stylometric detector adds 11.1 percentage points of F1 on an indirect-injection benchmark. The fatigue tracker is validated via a probing-campaign integration test. All code, data, and reproduction scripts are released under Apache 2.0.

ai.tldr: 本文提出七种跨领域技术用于检测提示注入攻击，其中三种已在prompt-shield v0.4.1中实现，并在多个数据集上验证了有效性。
ai.motivation: 现有开源提示注入检测器主要依赖正则表达式模式匹配和微调Transformer分类器，两者均存在明显缺陷：正则表达式无法识别改写攻击，微调分类器在自适应攻击下易被绕过。
ai.method: 从七个外部领域引入特定机制：法医语言学、材料科学疲劳分析、网络安全欺骗技术、生物信息学局部序列比对、经济学机制设计、流行病学频谱信号分析、编译器理论污点追踪。
ai.result: 局部比对检测器在deepset数据集上将F1从0.033提升至0.378且无额外误报；文体特征检测器在间接注入基准上提升F1 11.1个百分点；疲劳追踪器通过探测活动集成测试验证。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了七种可移植的检测思路，其中三种已开源实现，为构建更鲁棒的提示注入防御系统提供跨学科技术参考。

idea_spark.idea: 利用局部序列比对技术识别恶意提示与正常提示的差异模式。
idea_spark.risk: 跨领域方法可能需要针对LLM上下文进行大量适配，计算开销可能增加。
idea_spark.inspiration: 安全检测问题可借鉴其他学科中已解决的模式识别、异常检测和对抗分析机制。
